{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01GZqGkQtHB58eVVTcpTZqa8","type":"tool_result","content":"0\t//! Role-based and attribute-based access control for Axon (FEAT-012).\n1\t//!\n2\t//! # Layers\n3\t//!\n4\t//! **Layer 1 — RBAC** ([`Role`], [`CallerIdentity`], [`Operation`])\n5\t//!\n6\t//! Four built-in roles (`Admin > Write > Read > None`) control access to\n7\t//! Axon operations.  Roles are derived from the identity provider (Tailscale\n8\t//! ACL tags, OIDC claims, etc.) by the server layer and passed into handlers\n9\t//! as a [`CallerIdentity`].  Handlers call [`CallerIdentity::check`] to\n10\t//! enforce the minimum required role.\n11\t//!\n12\t//! **Layer 2 — Field-level masking** ([`MaskPolicy`])\n13\t//!\n14\t//! A `MaskPolicy` hides individual entity fields from callers whose role\n15\t//! falls below a configured minimum.  Applied by [`CallerIdentity::apply_masks`]\n16\t//! before returning entity data to the caller.  Admins always see all fields.\n17\t//!\n18\t//! **Layer 3 — Collection write control** ([`WritePolicy`])\n19\t//!\n20\t//! A `WritePolicy` sets a per-collection minimum write role and marks fields\n21\t//! as immutable after creation.  Checked by the handler before any mutation.\n22\t//!\n23\t//! **Layer 4 — Database-scoped grants** ([`DatabaseGrant`], [`GrantRegistry`])\n24\t//!\n25\t//! Grants scope a role to a specific database (or `\"*\"` for all databases).\n26\t//! Without a matching grant a caller has `Role::None` on that database.\n27\t//! Admins with a global grant bypass per-database checks.  The grant registry\n28\t//! is stored in `__axon_policies__` and enforced at the routing layer.\n29\t\n30\tuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\n31\t\n32\tuse crate::error::AxonError;\n33\t\n34\t/// Built-in roles for Axon RBAC (FEAT-012).\n35\t///\n36\t/// Roles are ordered by privilege: `Admin > Write > Read > None`.\n37\t/// When a user has multiple roles (e.g., from multiple ACL tags),\n38\t/// the highest-privilege role wins.\n39\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, PartialOrd, Ord, Hash, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n40\tpub enum Role {\n41\t    /// No access. Explicitly denied.\n42\t    None = 0,\n43\t    /// Read entities, query, traverse, browse audit log.\n44\t    Read = 1,\n45\t    /// Create, update, delete entities and links in any collection.\n46\t    Write = 2,\n47\t    /// All operations including drop and schema changes.\n48\t    Admin = 3,\n49\t}\n50\t\n51\timpl Role {\n52\t    /// Parse a role from a Tailscale ACL tag or string name.\n53\t    ///\n54\t    /// Accepted inputs (both full `tag:` prefix and bare name):\n55\t    ///\n56\t    /// | Input | Role |\n57\t    /// |-------|------|\n58\t    /// | `\"tag:axon-admin\"` / `\"tag:admin\"` / `\"admin\"` | `Admin` |\n59\t    /// | `\"tag:axon-write\"` / `\"tag:axon-agent\"` / `\"tag:write\"` / `\"write\"` | `Write` |\n60\t    /// | `\"tag:axon-read\"` / `\"tag:read\"` / `\"read\"` | `Read` |\n61\t    /// | anything else | `None` |\n62\t    ///\n63\t    /// `tag:axon-agent` is an alias for `write` — the conventional tag for\n64\t    /// automated agent workloads that need read/write but not admin access.\n65\t    pub fn from_tag(tag: &str) -> Self {\n66\t        match tag {\n67\t            \"tag:axon-admin\" | \"tag:admin\" | \"admin\" => Role::Admin,\n68\t            \"tag:axon-write\" | \"tag:axon-agent\" | \"tag:write\" | \"write\" => Role::Write,\n69\t            \"tag:axon-read\" | \"tag:read\" | \"read\" => Role::Read,\n70\t            _ => Role::None,\n71\t        }\n72\t    }\n73\t\n74\t    /// Determine the highest-privilege role from a set of tags.\n75\t    pub fn from_tags(tags: &[String]) -> Self {\n76\t        tags.iter()\n77\t            .map(|t| Role::from_tag(t))\n78\t            .max()\n79\t            .unwrap_or(Role::None)\n80\t    }\n81\t}\n82\t\n83\timpl std::fmt::Display for Role {\n84\t    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut std::fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> std::fmt::Result {\n85\t        match self {\n86\t            Role::None => write!(f, \"none\"),\n87\t            Role::Read => write!(f, \"read\"),\n88\t            Role::Write => write!(f, \"write\"),\n89\t            Role::Admin => write!(f, \"admin\"),\n90\t        }\n91\t    }\n92\t}\n93\t\n94\t/// The kind of operation being attempted.\n95\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]\n96\tpub enum Operation {\n97\t    /// Read an entity, query, traverse, audit.\n98\t    Read,\n99\t    /// Create, update, delete entities/links.\n100\t    Write,\n101\t    /// Drop collections, modify schemas, manage namespaces.\n102\t    Admin,\n103\t}\n104\t\n105\timpl Operation {\n106\t    /// The minimum role required for this operation.\n107\t    pub fn required_role(self) -> Role {\n108\t        match self {\n109\t            Operation::Read => Role::Read,\n110\t            Operation::Write => Role::Write,\n111\t            Operation::Admin => Role::Admin,\n112\t        }\n113\t    }\n114\t}\n115\t\n116\t/// A simple field-equality filter used as an agent scope constraint\n117\t/// (FEAT-022 / ADR-016).\n118\t///\n119\t/// When attached to a [`CallerIdentity`], the agent guardrails layer rejects\n120\t/// any mutation whose target entity data does not satisfy\n121\t/// `data[field] == value`. V1 supports only field-equality predicates;\n122\t/// compound and range filters are deferred.\n123\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n124\tpub struct EntityFilter {\n125\t    /// The top-level data field to compare.\n126\t    pub field: String,\n127\t    /// The required value for that field.\n128\t    pub value: serde_json::Value,\n129\t}\n130\t\n131\timpl EntityFilter {\n132\t    /// Convenience constructor.\n133\t    pub fn new(field: impl Into<String>, value: serde_json::Value) -> Self {\n134\t        Self {\n135\t            field: field.into(),\n136\t            value,\n137\t        }\n138\t    }\n139\t\n140\t    /// Returns `true` when the JSON object `data` satisfies this filter.\n141\t    ///\n142\t    /// Returns `false` when `data` is not a JSON object, when the field is\n143\t    /// absent, or when the value does not equal [`Self::value`].\n144\t    pub fn matches(&self, data: &serde_json::Value) -> bool {\n145\t        data.get(&self.field).is_some_and(|v| v == &self.value)\n146\t    }\n147\t}\n148\t\n149\t/// Identity context for a request.\n150\t///\n151\t/// Populated by the auth middleware from the identity provider.\n152\t/// In `--no-auth` mode, all requests get `CallerIdentity::anonymous()`.\n153\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n154\tpub struct CallerIdentity {\n155\t    /// Actor name for audit log entries.\n156\t    pub actor: String,\n157\t    /// The caller's effective role (highest privilege from all their tags).\n158\t    pub role: Role,\n159\t    /// Optional scope constraint enforced by the agent guardrails layer\n160\t    /// (FEAT-022, ADR-016). When `Some`, this caller may only mutate entities\n161\t    /// whose data matches the filter. `None` means no scope restriction.\n162\t    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n163\t    pub entity_filter: Option<EntityFilter>,\n164\t}\n165\t\n166\timpl CallerIdentity {\n167\t    /// Create an anonymous identity with admin privileges (`--no-auth` mode).\n168\t    pub fn anonymous() -> Self {\n169\t        Self {\n170\t            actor: \"anonymous\".into(),\n171\t            role: Role::Admin,\n172\t            entity_filter: None,\n173\t        }\n174\t    }\n175\t\n176\t    /// Create an identity from a name and role.\n177\t    pub fn new(actor: impl Into<String>, role: Role) -> Self {\n178\t        Self {\n179\t            actor: actor.into(),\n180\t            role,\n181\t            entity_filter: None,\n182\t        }\n183\t    }\n184\t\n185\t    /// Attach an entity scope filter (builder style, FEAT-022).\n186\t    pub fn with_entity_filter(mut self, filter: EntityFilter) -> Self {\n187\t        self.entity_filter = Some(filter);\n188\t        self\n189\t    }\n190\t\n191\t    /// Check whether this caller is authorized for the given operation.\n192\t    ///\n193\t    /// Returns `Ok(())` if authorized, or `Err(AxonError::InvalidOperation)`\n194\t    /// with a descriptive message if not.\n195\t    pub fn check(&self, op: Operation) -> Result<(), AxonError> {\n196\t        let required = op.required_role();\n197\t        if self.role >= required {\n198\t            Ok(())\n199\t        } else {\n200\t            Err(AxonError::InvalidOperation(format!(\n201\t                \"permission denied: role '{}' cannot perform {:?} operations (requires '{}')\",\n202\t                self.role, op, required\n203\t            )))\n204\t        }\n205\t    }\n206\t\n207\t    /// Apply field-level masking to entity data based on mask policies.\n208\t    ///\n209\t    /// Admin users see all fields. For other roles, fields listed in a\n210\t    /// mask policy that requires a higher role than the caller's are\n211\t    /// removed from the data.\n212\t    pub fn apply_masks(&self, data: &mut serde_json::Value, policies: &[MaskPolicy]) {\n213\t        if self.role >= Role::Admin {\n214\t            return; // Admin sees all.\n215\t        }\n216\t        if let Some(obj) = data.as_object_mut() {\n217\t            for policy in policies {\n218\t                if self.role < policy.min_role {\n219\t                    obj.remove(&policy.field);\n220\t                }\n221\t            }\n222\t        }\n223\t    }\n224\t}\n225\t\n226\t/// A field-level mask policy (US-046, FEAT-012).\n227\t///\n228\t/// Specifies that a field should be hidden from callers whose role is\n229\t/// below `min_role`. Admin always sees all fields.\n230\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n231\tpub struct MaskPolicy {\n232\t    /// The field name to mask.\n233\t    pub field: String,\n234\t    /// Minimum role required to see this field.\n235\t    pub min_role: Role,\n236\t}\n237\t\n238\t// ── Attribute-based write control (US-047, FEAT-012) ────────────────────────\n239\t\n240\t/// Per-collection write policy for attribute-based access control (ABAC).\n241\t///\n242\t/// Stored in the `__axon_policies__` pseudo-collection. Defines:\n243\t/// - Minimum role required to write to this collection\n244\t/// - Fields that are immutable after creation\n245\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n246\tpub struct WritePolicy {\n247\t    /// Collection this policy applies to.\n248\t    pub collection: String,\n249\t    /// Minimum role required for write operations.\n250\t    pub min_write_role: Role,\n251\t    /// Fields that cannot be modified after the initial create.\n252\t    #[serde(default)]\n253\t    pub immutable_fields: Vec<String>,\n254\t}\n255\t\n256\timpl WritePolicy {\n257\t    /// Check if a caller has sufficient role to write to this collection.\n258\t    pub fn check_write(&self, caller: &CallerIdentity) -> Result<(), AxonError> {\n259\t        if caller.role >= self.min_write_role {\n260\t            Ok(())\n261\t        } else {\n262\t            Err(AxonError::InvalidOperation(format!(\n263\t                \"write policy for '{}': role '{}' insufficient (requires '{}')\",\n264\t                self.collection, caller.role, self.min_write_role\n265\t            )))\n266\t        }\n267\t    }\n268\t\n269\t    /// Check if any immutable fields have been modified.\n270\t    ///\n271\t    /// Returns the list of field names that were illegally modified.\n272\t    pub fn check_immutable_fields(\n273\t        &self,\n274\t        old_data: &serde_json::Value,\n275\t        new_data: &serde_json::Value,\n276\t    ) -> Vec<String> {\n277\t        let mut violations = Vec::new();\n278\t        for field in &self.immutable_fields {\n279\t            let old_val = old_data.get(field);\n280\t            let new_val = new_data.get(field);\n281\t            // If the field existed before and is now different or missing, it's a violation.\n282\t            if old_val.is_some() && old_val != new_val {\n283\t                violations.push(field.clone());\n284\t            }\n285\t        }\n286\t        violations\n287\t    }\n288\t}\n289\t\n290\t// ── Database-scoped access control (US-038, FEAT-014) ────────────────────────\n291\t\n292\t/// A grant that scopes a role to a specific database.\n293\t///\n294\t/// Without a grant, users cannot access a database (unless they have a\n295\t/// wildcard `*` grant or admin role).\n296\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n297\tpub struct DatabaseGrant {\n298\t    /// The actor (user/node) this grant applies to. `\"*\"` means all users.\n299\t    pub actor: String,\n300\t    /// The database name. `\"*\"` means all databases.\n301\t    pub database: String,\n302\t    /// The role granted for this database.\n303\t    pub role: Role,\n304\t}\n305\t\n306\t/// Registry of database-scoped grants.\n307\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n308\tpub struct GrantRegistry {\n309\t    grants: Vec<DatabaseGrant>,\n310\t}\n311\t\n312\timpl GrantRegistry {\n313\t    /// Create a new empty grant registry.\n314\t    pub fn new() -> Self {\n315\t        Self::default()\n316\t    }\n317\t\n318\t    /// Add a grant.\n319\t    pub fn add(&mut self, grant: DatabaseGrant) {\n320\t        self.grants.push(grant);\n321\t    }\n322\t\n323\t    /// Remove all grants for an actor on a database.\n324\t    pub fn revoke(&mut self, actor: &str, database: &str) {\n325\t        self.grants\n326\t            .retain(|g| g.actor != actor || g.database != database);\n327\t    }\n328\t\n329\t    /// Resolve the effective role for an actor on a database.\n330\t    ///\n331\t    /// Checks grants in order: exact match, then wildcard actor, then\n332\t    /// wildcard database. Returns the highest-privilege matching role.\n333\t    pub fn effective_role(&self, actor: &str, database: &str) -> Role {\n334\t        self.grants\n335\t            .iter()\n336\t            .filter(|g| {\n337\t                (g.actor == actor || g.actor == \"*\")\n338\t                    && (g.database == database || g.database == \"*\")\n339\t            })\n340\t            .map(|g| g.role)\n341\t            .max()\n342\t            .unwrap_or(Role::None)\n343\t    }\n344\t\n345\t    /// Check if an actor can perform an operation on a database.\n346\t    pub fn check(\n347\t        &self,\n348\t        actor: &str,\n349\t        database: &str,\n350\t        operation: Operation,\n351\t    ) -> Result<(), AxonError> {\n352\t        let role = self.effective_role(actor, database);\n353\t        if role >= operation.required_role() {\n354\t            Ok(())\n355\t        } else {\n356\t            Err(AxonError::InvalidOperation(format!(\n357\t                \"no grant for actor '{actor}' on database '{database}' with sufficient role (has '{role}', needs '{}')\",\n358\t                operation.required_role()\n359\t            )))\n360\t        }\n361\t    }\n362\t\n363\t    /// List all grants.\n364\t    pub fn list(&self) -> &[DatabaseGrant] {\n365\t        &self.grants\n366\t    }\n367\t}\n368\t\n369\t#[cfg(test)]\n370\tmod tests {\n371\t    use super::*;\n372\t\n373\t    #[test]\n374\t    fn role_ordering() {\n375\t        assert!(Role::Admin > Role::Write);\n376\t        assert!(Role::Write > Role::Read);\n377\t        assert!(Role::Read > Role::None);\n378\t    }\n379\t\n380\t    #[test]\n381\t    fn role_from_tag() {\n382\t        // Primary tags\n383\t        assert_eq!(Role::from_tag(\"tag:axon-admin\"), Role::Admin);\n384\t        assert_eq!(Role::from_tag(\"tag:axon-write\"), Role::Write);\n385\t        assert_eq!(Role::from_tag(\"tag:axon-read\"), Role::Read);\n386\t        // Short-form aliases\n387\t        assert_eq!(Role::from_tag(\"tag:admin\"), Role::Admin);\n388\t        assert_eq!(Role::from_tag(\"tag:write\"), Role::Write);\n389\t        assert_eq!(Role::from_tag(\"tag:read\"), Role::Read);\n390\t        // Bare names (no tag: prefix)\n391\t        assert_eq!(Role::from_tag(\"admin\"), Role::Admin);\n392\t        assert_eq!(Role::from_tag(\"write\"), Role::Write);\n393\t        assert_eq!(Role::from_tag(\"read\"), Role::Read);\n394\t        // Agent alias\n395\t        assert_eq!(Role::from_tag(\"tag:axon-agent\"), Role::Write);\n396\t        // Unknown\n397\t        assert_eq!(Role::from_tag(\"unknown\"), Role::None);\n398\t    }\n399\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01TSMSYvQzdh78YH3Sxyiy8q","session_id":"80f0eb9d-4064-4d06-8560-e247ce1170f2","uuid":"7ede5a95-a576-4691-a591-d02b7bdc8f8e","timestamp":"2026-04-15T01:31:05.922Z"}

This enables use as a\n41\t/// BTreeMap key.\n42\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy)]\n43\tpub struct OrderedFloat(pub u64);\n44\t\n45\timpl OrderedFloat {\n46\t    pub fn new(val: f64) -> Self {\n47\t        Self(val.to_bits())\n48\t    }\n49\t\n50\t    pub fn value(&self) -> f64 {\n51\t        f64::from_bits(self.0)\n52\t    }\n53\t}\n54\t\n55\timpl PartialEq for OrderedFloat {\n56\t    fn eq(&self, other: &Self) -> bool {\n57\t        self.0 == other.0\n58\t    }\n59\t}\n60\timpl Eq for OrderedFloat {}\n61\t\n62\timpl std::hash::Hash for OrderedFloat {\n63\t    fn hash<H: std::hash::Hasher>(&self, state: &mut H) {\n64\t        self.0.hash(state);\n65\t    }\n66\t}\n67\t\n68\timpl PartialOrd for OrderedFloat {\n69\t    fn partial_cmp(&self, other: &Self) -> Option<std::cmp::Ordering> {\n70\t        Some(self.cmp(other))\n71\t    }\n72\t}\n73\t\n74\timpl Ord for OrderedFloat {\n75\t    fn cmp(&self, other: &Self) -> std::cmp::Ordering {\n76\t        let a = f64::from_bits(self.0);\n77\t        let b = f64::from_bits(other.0);\n78\t        a.total_cmp(&b)\n79\t    }\n80\t}\n81\t\n82\t/// Extract an [`IndexValue`] from a JSON value according to the declared index type.\n83\t///\n84\t/// Returns `None` if the value is null, missing, or has a type mismatch\n85\t/// (per FEAT-013: mismatched types are not indexed, not errors).\n86\tpub fn extract_index_value(\n87\t    json_val: &serde_json::Value,\n88\t    index_type: &axon_schema::schema::IndexType,\n89\t) -> Option<IndexValue> {\n90\t    use axon_schema::schema::IndexType;\n91\t    match index_type {\n92\t        IndexType::String => json_val.as_str().map(|s| IndexValue::String(s.to_string())),\n93\t        IndexType::Integer => json_val.as_i64().map(IndexValue::Integer),\n94\t        IndexType::Float => json_val\n95\t            .as_f64()\n96\t            .map(|f| IndexValue::Float(OrderedFloat::new(f))),\n97\t        IndexType::Boolean => json_val.as_bool().map(IndexValue::Boolean),\n98\t        IndexType::Datetime => {\n99\t            // Datetimes are stored as strings for ordering purposes.\n100\t            json_val.as_str().map(|s| IndexValue::String(s.to_string()))\n101\t        }\n102\t    }\n103\t}\n104\t\n105\t/// A compound index key: ordered list of field values for multi-field indexes.\n106\t///\n107\t/// Implements `Ord` lexicographically, enabling prefix matching via\n108\t/// BTreeMap range scans.\n109\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, PartialOrd, Ord, json_val = resolve_field_path(data, &f.field)?;\n123\t        let val = extract_index_value(json_val, &f.index_type)?;\n124\t        values.push(val);\n125\t    }\n126\t    Some(CompoundKey(values))\n127\t}\n128\t\n129\t/// Navigate a dotted field path in a JSON value.\n130\t///\n131\t/// E.g., `\"address.city\"` resolves `{\"address\": {\"city\": \"NY\"}}` to `\"NY\"`.\n132\tpub fn resolve_field_path<'a>(\n133\t    data: &'a serde_json::Value,\n134\t    path: &str,\n135\t) -> Option<&'a serde_json::Value> {\n136\t    let mut current = data;\n137\t    for segment in path.split('.') {\n138\t        current = current.get(segment)?;\n139\t    }\n140\t    Some(current)\n141\t}\n142\t\n143\t/// Abstraction over Axon's backing storage.\n144\t///\n145\t/// Implementations wrap specific databases (SQLite, PostgreSQL, FoundationDB).\n146\t/// All operations are executed within the context of the adapter's internal\n147\t/// transaction ASC\",\n            )\n            .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\n\n        let rows = stmt\n            .query_map([], |row| row.get::<_, String>(0))\n            .map_err(|e| axon_schema::schema::{CollectionSchema, CollectionView};\n\nuse crate::adapter::StorageAdapter;\n\n/// PostgreSQL-backed storage adapter.\n///\n/// Uses the asynchronous `tokio-postgres` crate driven from a dedicated Tokio\n/// runtime. Because `StorageAdapter` is synchronous, each database call\n/// blocks the calling thread via `handle.block_on(...)`.\n///\n/// Transactions are handled via `BEGIN` / `COMMIT` / `ROLLBACK` statements.\n/// The adapter creates the required tables on initialization if they do not\n/// exist.\npub struct PostgresStorageAdapter {\n    client: tokio_postgres::Client,\n    handle: tokio::runtime::Handle,\n    _rt: tokio::runtime::Runtime,\n    in_tx: bool,\n}\n\nimpl PostgresStorageAdapter {\n    /// Connect to a PostgreSQL database using a connection string.\n    ///\n    /// Example: `\"host=localhost user=axon dbname=axon\"`\n    pub fn connect(params: &str) -> Result<Self, AxonError> {\n        let rt = tokio::runtime::Builder::new_multi_thread()\n            .worker_threads(2)\n            .enable_all()\n            .build()\n            .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\n        let handle = rt.handle().clone();\n        let (client, connection) = handle\n            .block_on(tokio_postgres::connect(params, NoTls))\n            .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(format!(\"connection failed: {e}\")))?;\n        handle.spawn(async move {\n            if let Err(e) = connection.await {\n                tracing::error!(\"postgres connection error: {e}\");\n            }\n        });\n        let mut adapter = Self {\n            client,\n            handle,\n            _rt: rt,\n            in_tx: false,\n        };\n        adapter.init_schema()?;\n        Ok(adapter)\n    }\n\n    /// Block the current thread on a `tokio_postgres` future, converting any\n    /// error into `AxonError::Storage`.\n    ///\n    /// When called from within a Tokio async task, uses\n    /// [`tokio::task::block_in_place`] to temporarily un-enter the async\n    /// context before blocking on the adapter's dedicated runtime.  This\n    /// prevents the \"cannot call `block_on` from within an async context\"\n    /// panic that would otherwise occur when gateway route handlers drive\n    /// storage calls.\n    ///\n    /// Requires the multi-thread Tokio scheduler (used by the production\n    /// server).  Tests that use the default `current_thread` scheduler must\n    /// call adapter methods from a `spawn_blocking` closure.\n    fn block<T>(\n        &self,\n        fut: impl Future<Output = Result<T, tokio_postgres::Error>>,\n    ) -> Result<T, AxonError> {\n        match tokio::runtime::Handle::try_current() {\n            Ok(_) => tokio::task::block_in_place(|| self.handle.block_on(fut)),\n            Err(_) => self.handle.block_on(fut),\n        }\n        .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))\n    }\n\n    fn init_schema(&mut self) -> Result<(), AxonError> {\n EXISTS namespaces (\n                    database_name TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES databases(name) ON DELETE CASCADE,\n                    name          TEXT NOT NULL,\n                    PRIMARY KEY serde_json::from_value(view_json)?;\n                Ok(Some(view))\n            }\n            None => Ok(None),\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn delete_collection_view(&mut self, collection: &CollectionId) -> Result<(), AxonError> {\n        let key = self.resolve_catalog_key(collection)?;\n        self.block(self.client.execute(\n            \"DELETE FROM collection_views\n                 WHERE collection = $1 AND database_name = $2 AND schema_name = $3\",\n            &[\n                &key.collection.as_str(),\n                &key.namespace.database,\n                &key.namespace.schema,\n            ],\n        ))?;\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    fn register_collection_in_namespace(\n        &mut self,\n        collection: &CollectionId,\n        namespace: &Namespace,\n    ) -> Result<(), AxonError> {\n        if !self.namespace_exists(namespace)? {\n            return Err(AxonError::NotFound(format!(\"namespace '{namespace}'\")));\n        }\n\n        self.block(self.client.execute(\n            \"INSERT INTO collections (name, database_name, schema_name)\n                 VALUES ($1, $2, $3)\n                 ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING\",\n            &[&collection.as_str(), &namespace.database, &namespace.schema],\n        ))?;\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    fn unregister_collection(&mut self, collection: &CollectionId) -> Result<(), AxonError> {\n        let key = self.resolve_catalog_key(collection)?;\n        let raw_collection = collection.as_str();\n        let default_namespace = Namespace::default_ns();\n        // Upgraded databases may still have a pre-fix collection_views table\n        // without the collection -> collections foreign key, and may still\n        // carry metadata rows keyed by the original qualified identifier in\n        // either the resolved namespace or the old default/default namespace,\n        // so clean them up explicitly instead of relying solely on ON DELETE\n        // CASCADE.\n        self.block(self.client.execute(\n            \"DELETE FROM collection_views\n                 WHERE collection = $1 AND database_name = $2 AND schema_name = $3\",\n            &[\n                &key.collection.as_str(),\n                &key.namespace.database,\n                &key.namespace.schema,\n            ],\n        ))?;\n        if raw_collection != key.collection.as_str() {\n            self.block(self.client.execute(\n                \"DELETE FROM collection_views\n                     WHERE collection = $1\n                       AND ((database_name = $2 AND schema_name = $3)\n                            OR (database_name = $4 AND schema_name = $5))\",\n                &[\n                    &raw_collection,\n                    &key.namespace.database,\n                    &key.namespace.schema,\n                    &default_namespace.database,\n                    &default_namespace.schema,\n                ],\n            ))?;\n        }\n        self.block(self.client.execute(\n            \"DELETE FROM schemas\n                 WHERE collection = $1 AND database_name = $2 AND schema_name = $3\",\n            &[\n                &key.collection.as_str(),\n                &key.namespace.database,\n                &key.namespace.schema,\n            ],\n        ))?;\n        if raw_collection != key.collection.as_str() {\n            self.block(self.client.execute(\n                \"DELETE FROM schemas\n                     WHERE collection = $1\n                       AND ((database_name = $2 AND schema_name = $3)\n                            OR (database_name = $4 AND schema_name = $5))\",\n                &[\n                    &raw_collection,\n                    &key.namespace.database,\n                    &key.namespace.schema,\n                    &default_namespace.database,\n                    &default_namespace.schema,\n                ],\n            ))?;\n        }\n        self.block(self.client.execute(\n            \"DELETE FROM collections\n                 WHERE name = $1 AND database_name = $2 AND schema_name = $3\",\n            &[\n                &key.collection.as_str(),\n                &key.namespace.database,\n                &key.namespace.schema,\n            ],\n        ))?;\n        if raw_collection != key.collection.as_str() {\n            self.block(self.client.execute(\n                \"DELETE FROM collections\n                     WHERE name = $1\n                       AND ((database_name = $2 AND schema_name = $3)\n                            OR (database_name = $4 AND schema_name = $5))\",\n                &[\n                    &raw_collection,\n                    &key.namespace.database,\n                    &key.namespace.schema,\n                    &default_namespace.database,\n                    &default_namespace.schema,\n                ],\n            ))?;\n        }\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    fn list_collections(&self) -> Result<Vec<CollectionId>, AxonError> {\n        let rows = self.block(self.client.query(\n            \"SELECT name FROM collections\n                 ORDER BY database_name ASC, schema_name ASC, name ASC\",\n            &[],\n        ))?;\n\n        Ok(rows\n            .iter()\n            .map(|row| {\n                let name: String = row.get(\"name\");\n                CollectionId::new(name)\n            })\n            .collect())\n    }\n\n    fn collection_registered_in_namespace(\n        &self,\n        collection: &CollectionId,\n        namespace: &Namespace,\n    ) -> Result<bool, AxonError> {\n        self.collection_exists_in_namespace(collection, namespace)\n    }\n}\n\n// ── Per-tenant PostgreSQL provisioning ───────────────────────────────────────\n\n/// Validate a PostgreSQL database name to prevent SQL injection in DDL.\n///\n/// PostgreSQL identifiers may contain letters, digits, `_`, and `$`, and\n/// must start with a letter or `_`. We apply a conservative subset of that\n/// rule here to keep things simple and safe.\nfn validate_pg_db_name(name: &str) -> Result<(), AxonError> {\n    if name.is_empty() {\n        return Err(AxonError::InvalidArgument(\n            \"database name must not be empty\".to_owned(),\n        ));\n    }\n    let first = name.chars().next().expect(\"non-empty checked above\");\n    if !first.is_ascii_alphabetic() && first != '_' {\n        return Err(AxonError::InvalidArgument(format!(\n            \"database name '{name}' must start with a letter or underscore\"\n        )));\n    }\n    if !name.chars().all(|c| c.is_ascii_alphanumeric() || c == '_') {\n        return Err(AxonError::InvalidArgument(format!(\n            \"database name '{name}' contains invalid characters (only ASCII alphanumeric and _ allowed)\"\n        )));\n    }\n    Ok(())\n}\n\n/// Derive a per-tenant DSN from a superadmin DSN by replacing/adding the\n/// `dbname` key.\n///\n/// The superadmin DSN uses the `postgres` maintenance database.  We produce a\n/// new DSN that targets `axon_{name}`.\n///\n/// Both libpq keyword–value format (`host=... dbname=...`) and URL format\n/// (`postgres://...`) are supported.\npub fn tenant_dsn(superadmin_dsn: &str, tenant_db_name: &str) -> String {\n    let target = format!(\"axon_{tenant_db_name}\");\n    if superadmin_dsn.starts_with(\"postgres://\") || superadmin_dsn.starts_with(\"postgresql://\") {\n        // URL format: replace path component.\n        // e.g. postgres://user:pass@host/somedb?sslmode=disable\n        if let Some(pos) = superadmin_dsn.find(\"://\") {\n            let after_scheme = &superadmin_dsn[pos + 3..];\n            // Find the slash that separates authority from path.\n            if let Some(slash_pos) = after_scheme.find('/') {\n                let scheme_authority = &superadmin_dsn[..pos + 3 + slash_pos];\n                let rest = &after_scheme[slash_pos + 1..];\n                // Strip existing dbname (everything before '?').\n                let query = if let Some(q) = rest.find('?') {\n                    &rest[q..]\n                } else {\n                    \"\"\n                };\n                return format!(\"{scheme_authority}/{target}{query}\");\n            }\n            // No path: append the target database.\n            return format!(\"{superadmin_dsn}/{target}\");\n        }\n        format!(\"{superadmin_dsn}/{target}\")\n    } else {\n        // Keyword–value format.  Replace existing dbname= or append it.\n        let mut parts: Vec<String> = superadmin_dsn\n            .split_whitespace()\n            .filter(|part| !part.starts_with(\"dbname=\"))\n            .map(str::to_owned)\n            .collect();\n        parts.push(format!(\"dbname={target}\"));\n        parts.join(\" \")\n    }\n}\n\n/// Create a physical PostgreSQL database named `axon_{name}` using a\n/// superadmin connection.\n///\n/// The `superadmin_dsn` must connect to the `postgres` maintenance database\n/// (or any database the superuser can reach) with sufficient privileges to\n/// execute `CREATE DATABASE`.\n///\n/// The database name is validated to prevent SQL injection.\n///\n/// # Errors\n///\n/// Returns `AxonError::AlreadyExists` if the database already exists.\n/// Returns `AxonError::InvalidArgument` if `name` contains invalid characters.\n/// Returns `AxonError::Storage` for other PostgreSQL errors.\npub fn provision_postgres_database(superadmin_dsn: &str, name: &str) -> Result<(), AxonError> {\n    validate_pg_db_name(name)?;\n    let full_name = format!(\"axon_{name}\");\n    let rt = tokio::runtime::Builder::new_current_thread()\n        .enable_all()\n        .build()\n        .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\n    rt.block_on(async {\n        let (client, connection) = tokio_postgres::connect(superadmin_dsn, NoTls)\n            .await\n            .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\n        tokio::spawn(async move {\n            let _ = connection.await;\n        });\n        let row = client\n            .query_one(\n                \"SELECT EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM pg_database WHERE datname = $1)\",\n                &[&full_name.as_str()],\n            )\n            .await\n            .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\n        let exists: bool = row.get(0);\n        if exists {\n            return Err(AxonError::AlreadyExists(format!(\n                \"PostgreSQL database '{full_name}'\"\n            )));\n        }\n        // CREATE DATABASE cannot run inside a transaction; use simple_query to\n        // avoid the implicit transaction that execute() would open.\n        client\n            .simple_query(&format!(\"CREATE DATABASE \\\"{full_name}\\\"\"))\n            .await\n            .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\n        Ok(())\n    })\n}\n\n/// Drop the physical PostgreSQL database named `axon_{name}`.\n///\n/// The `superadmin_dsn` must have sufficient privileges to execute\n/// `DROP DATABASE`.\n///\n/// # Errors\n///\n/// Returns `AxonError::NotFound` if the database does not exist.\n/// Returns `AxonError::InvalidArgument` if `name` contains invalid characters.\n/// Returns `AxonError::Storage` for other PostgreSQL errors.\npub fn deprovision_postgres_database(superadmin_dsn: &str, name: &str) -> Result<(), AxonError> {\n    validate_pg_db_name(name)?;\n    let full_name = format!(\"axon_{name}\");\n    let rt = tokio::runtime::Builder::new_current_thread()\n        .enable_all()\n        .build()\n        .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\n    rt.block_on(async {\n        let (client, connection) = tokio_postgres::connect(superadmin_dsn, NoTls)\n            .await\n            .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\n        tokio::spawn(async move {\n            let _ = connection.await;\n        });\n        let row = client\n            .query_one(\n                \"SELECT EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM pg_database WHERE datname = $1)\",\n                &[&full_name.as_str()],\n            )\n            .await\n            .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\n        let exists: bool = row.get(0);\n        if !exists {\n            return Err(AxonError::NotFound(format!(\n                \"PostgreSQL database '{full_name}'\"\n            )));\n        }\n        client\n            .simple_query(&format!(\"DROP DATABASE \\\"{full_name}\\\"\"))\n            .await\n            .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\n        Ok(())\n    })\n}\n\n// The conformance test suite requires PostgreSQL.\n// Explicit verification path:\n// AXON_TEST_POSTGRES=\"host=localhost user=axon dbname=axon_test\" cargo test -p axon-storage postgres::tests:: -- --nocapture\n// When `AXON_TEST_POSTGRES` is unset, these tests attempt to provision PostgreSQL\n// via testcontainers and skip cleanly if the container runtime is unavailable.\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use std::{\n        ops::{Deref, DerefMut},\n        sync::{Mutex, MutexGuard, OnceLock},\n    };\n\n    use super::*;\n    use axon_core::types::Link;\n    use testcontainers_modules::postgres::Postgres as PgContainer;\n    use testcontainers_modules::testcontainers::{runners::SyncRunner, Container};\n\n    struct TestDatabase {\n        url: String,\n        _container: Option<Container<PgContainer>>,\n    }\n\n    enum TestSetupError {\n        Skip(String),\n        Fail(AxonError),\n    }\n\n    impl TestDatabase {\n        fn connect() -> Result<Self, TestSetupError> {\n            if let Ok(url) = std::env::var(\"AXON_TEST_POSTGRES\") {\n                return Ok(Self {\n                    url,\n                    _container: None,\n                });\n            }\n\n            let container = PgContainer::default()\n                .with_db_name(\"axon_test\")\n                .with_user(\"postgres\")\n                .with_password(\"postgres\")\n                .start()\n                .map_err(|error| {\n                    TestSetupError::Skip(format!(\n                        \"AXON_TEST_POSTGRES is unset and PostgreSQL test container startup failed: {error}\"\n                    ))\n                })?;\n            let host = container.get_host().map_err(|error| {\n                TestSetupError::Fail(AxonError::Storage(format!(\n                    \"failed to resolve PostgreSQL test container host: {error}\"\n                )))\n            })?;\n            let port = container.get_host_port_ipv4(5432).map_err(|error| {\n                TestSetupError::Fail(AxonError::Storage(format!(\n                    \"failed to resolve PostgreSQL test container port: {error}\"\n                )))\n            })?;\n\n            Ok(Self {\n                url: format!(\n                    \"host={host} port={port} user=postgres password=postgres dbname=axon_test\"\n                ),\n                _container: Some(container),\n            })\n        }\n\n        fn url(&self) -> &str {\n            &self.url\n        }\n    }\n\n    struct TestStore {\n        adapter: PostgresStorageAdapter,\n        _database: TestDatabase,\n    }\n\n    impl Deref for TestStore {\n        type Target = PostgresStorageAdapter;\n\n        fn deref(&self) -> &Self::Target {\n            &self.adapter\n        }\n    }\n\n    impl DerefMut for TestStore {\n        fn deref_mut(&mut self) -> &mut Self::Target {\n            &mut self.adapter\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn postgres_test_guard() -> MutexGuard<'static, ()> {\n        static GUARD: OnceLock<Mutex<()>> = OnceLock::new();\n        GUARD\n            .get_or_init(|| Mutex::new(()))\n            .lock()\n            .expect(\"PostgreSQL test guard lock should not be poisoned\")\n    }\n\n    fn skip_postgres_test(test_name: &str, reason: &str) {\n        tracing::warn!(test = test_name, reason, \"skipping PostgreSQL storage test\");\n    }\n\n    fn database_or_skip(test_name: &str) -> Option<TestDatabase> {\n        match TestDatabase::connect() {\n            Ok(database) => Some(database),\n            Err(TestSetupError::Skip(reason)) => {\n                skip_postgres_test(test_name, &reason);\n                None\n            }\n            Err(TestSetupError::Fail(error)) => {\n                panic!(\"PostgreSQL test setup should succeed: {error}\");\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn store() -> Result<TestStore, TestSetupError> {\n        let database = TestDatabase::connect()?;\n        let adapter =\n            PostgresStorageAdapter::connect(database.url()).map_err(TestSetupError::Fail)?;\n        // Clean tables for a fresh test.\n        adapter\n            .block(adapter.client.batch_execute(\n                \"TRUNCATE entities, schemas, collection_views, collections, namespaces, databases, audit_log RESTART IDENTITY CASCADE\",\n            ))\n            .map_err(TestSetupError::Fail)?;\n        adapter\n            .ensure_default_namespace()\n            .map_err(TestSetupError::Fail)?;\n        Ok(TestStore {\n            adapter,\n            _database: database,\n        })\n    }\n\n    fn store_or_skip(test_name: &str) -> Option<TestStore> {\n        match store() {\n            Ok(store) => Some(store),\n            Err(TestSetupError::Skip(reason)) => {\n                skip_postgres_test(test_name, &reason);\n                None\n            }\n            Err(TestSetupError::Fail(error)) => {\n                panic!(\"PostgreSQL test setup should succeed: {error}\");\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn register_unique_namespaced_collection(\n        store: &mut TestStore,\n        qualified: &CollectionId,\n    ) -> (Namespace, CollectionId) {\n        let (namespace, bare_collection) = Namespace::parse(qualified.as_str());\n        let bare_collection = CollectionId::new(bare_collection);\n\n        store\n            .create_database(namespace.database.as_str())\n            .expect(\"database create should succeed\");\n        store\n            .create_namespace(&namespace)\n            .expect(\"namespace create should succeed\");\n        store\n            .register_collection_in_namespace(&bare_collection, &namespace)\n            .expect(\"collection register should succeed\");\n\n tokio::runtime::Builder::new_current_thread()\n            .enable_all()\n            .build()\n            .expect(\"test runtime\");\n        let (client, connection) = rt\n            .block_on(tokio_postgres::connect(url, NoTls))\n            .expect(\"test connection\");\n        rt.spawn(async move {\n            let _ = connection.await;\n        });\n        (client, rt)\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn postgres_roundtrip_when_available() {\n        let _guard = postgres_test_guard();\n        let Some(mut s) = store_or_skip(\"postgres_roundtrip_when_available\") else {\n            return;\n        };\n\n        let col = CollectionId::new(\"tasks\");\n        let entity = Entity::new(\n            col.clone(),\n            EntityId::new(\"t-001\"),\n            serde_json::json!({\"title\": \"hello\"}),\n        );\n        s.put(entity).expect(\"test operation should succeed\");\n        let got = s\n            .get(&col, &EntityId::new(\"t-001\"))\n            .expect(\"test operation should succeed\")\n            .expect(\"test operation should succeed\");\n        assert_eq!(got.data[\"title\"], \"hello\");\n        assert_eq!(got.version, 1);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn unregister_collection_cleans_up_legacy_collection_views_when_available() {\n        let _guard = postgres_test_guard();\n        let Some(database) = database_or_skip(\n connect_raw_for_test(database.url());\n        legacy_rt\n            .block_on(async {\n                legacy_client\n                    .batch_execute(\n                        \"DROP TABLE IF EXISTS collection_views;\n                         DROP TABLE IF EXISTS collections;\n                         DROP TABLE IF EXISTS namespaces;\n                         DROP TABLE IF EXISTS databases;\n                         DROP TABLE IF EXISTS entities;\n                         DROP TABLE IF EXISTS schemas;\n                         DROP TABLE IF EXISTS audit_log;\",\n                    )\n                    .await?;\n                legacy_client\n                    .batch_execute(\n                        \"CREATE TABLE collections (\n                            name TEXT NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY\n                        );\n                        CREATE TABLE collection_views (\n                            collection    TEXT NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY,\n                            version       INTEGER NOT NULL,\n                            view_json     JSONB NOT NULL,\n                            updated_at_ns BIGINT NOT NULL,\n                            updated_by    TEXT\n                        );\",\n                    )\n                    .await?;\n                Ok::<_, tokio_postgres::Error>(())\n            })\n            .expect(\"legacy schema should be created\");\n\n        let collection = CollectionId::new(\"ephemeral\");\n        let legacy_view = CollectionView {\n            collection: collection.clone(),\n            description: Some(\"legacy view\".into()),\n            markdown_template: \"# {{title}}\".into(),\n            version: 1,\n            updated_at_ns: Some(42),\n            updated_by: Some(\"legacy\".into()),\n        };\n        let legacy_view_json =\n            serde_json::to_value(&legacy_view).expect(\"legacy collection view should serialize\");\n\n        legacy_rt\n            .block_on(async {\n                legacy_client\n                    .execute(\n                        \"INSERT INTO collections (name) VALUES ($1)\",\n                        &[&collection.as_str()],\n                    )\n                    .await?;\n                legacy_client\n                    .execute(\n                        \"INSERT INTO collection_views (collection, version, view_json, updated_at_ns, updated_by)\n                         VALUES ($1, $2, $3, $4, $5)\",\n                        &[\n                            &collection.as_str(),\n                            &1_i32,\n                            &legacy_view_json,\n                            &42_i64,\n                            &Some(\"legacy\"),\n                        ],\n                    )\n                    .await?;\n                Ok::<_, tokio_postgres::Error>(())\n            })\n            .expect(\"legacy data should insert\");\n        drop(legacy_client);\n        drop(legacy_rt);\n\n        let mut adapter = PostgresStorageAdapter::connect(database.url())\n            .expect(\"adapter should connect after upgrade\");\n        assert!(\n            adapter\n assert!(\n            adapter\n                .get_collection_view(&collection)\n                .expect(\"collection view lookup should succeed after unregister\")\n                .is_none(),\n            \"legacy collection view should be removed during unregister\"\n        );\n\n        let remaining_views: i64 = adapter\n            .block(adapter.client.query_one(\n                \"SELECT COUNT(*) FROM collection_views WHERE collection = $1\",\n                &[&collection.as_str()],\n            ))\n            .expect(\"count query should succeed\")\n            .get(0);\n        assert_eq!(\n            remaining_views, 0,\n            \"stale collection view rows must be deleted\"\n        );\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn namespace_catalogs_allow_same_name_without_cross_drop() {\n        let _guard = postgres_test_guard();\n        let Some(mut s) = store_or_skip(\"namespace_catalogs_allow_same_name_without_cross_drop\")\n        else {\n            return;\n        };\n        let invoices = CollectionId::new(\"invoices\");\n        let billing = Namespace::new(\"prod\", \"billing\");\n        let engineering = Namespace::new(\"prod\", \"engineering\");\n\n        s.create_database(\"prod\")\n            .expect(\"database create should succeed\");\n        s.create_namespace(&billing)\n            .expect(\"billing namespace create should succeed\");\n        s.create_namespace(&engineering)\n            .expect(\"engineering namespace create should succeed\");\n\n        s.register_collection_in_namespace(&invoices, &Namespace::default_ns())\n            .expect(\"default collection register should succeed\");\n        s.register_collection_in_namespace(&invoices, &billing)\n            .expect(\"billing collection register should succeed\");\n        s.register_collection_in_namespace(&invoices, &engineering)\n            .expect(\"engineering collection register should succeed\");\n\n        assert_eq!(\n            s.list_namespace_collections(&billing)\n                .expect(\"billing list should s.drop_database(\"prod\").expect(\"prod drop should succeed\");\n        assert_eq!(\n            s.list_namespace_collections(&Namespace::default_ns())\n                .expect(\"default list should survive prod drop\"),\n            vec![invoices]\n        );\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn drop_namespace_purges_entities_for_removed_collections() {\n        let _guard = postgres_test_guard();\n        let Some(mut s) = store_or_skip(\"drop_namespace_purges_entities_for_removed_collections\")\n        else {\n            return;\n        };\n        let billing = Namespace::new(\"prod\", \"billing\");\n        let engineering = Namespace::new(\"prod\", \"engineering\");\n        let invoices = CollectionId::new(\"invoices\");\n        let ledger = CollectionId::new(\"ledger\");\n\n        s.create_database(\"prod\")\n            .expect(\"database create should succeed\");\n        s.create_namespace(&billing)\n            .expect(\"billing namespace create should succeed\");\n .expect(\"billing collection register should succeed\");\n        s.register_collection_in_namespace(&ledger, &engineering)\n            .expect(\"engineering collection register should succeed\");\n        s.put(Entity::new(\n            invoices.clone(),\n            EntityId::new(\"inv-001\"),\n            serde_json::json!({\"title\": \"invoice\"}),\n        ))\n        .expect(\"billing entity JWT-based auth pipeline: sign, verify, and enforce credentials (ADR-018 §4).\n//!\n//! # Verification order\n//!\n//! 1. Extract `Authorization: Bearer <token>` → `Unauthenticated` if missing.\n//! 2. Parse + verify HS256 signature → `CredentialInvalid` / `CredentialMalformed`.\n//! 3. Check `iss` against issuer ID → `CredentialForeignIssuer`.\n//! 4. Check `exp`/`nbf` with ±30 s skew → `CredentialExpired` / `CredentialNotYetValid`.\n//! 5. Check `jti` revocation (cache → storage) → `CredentialRevoked`.\n//! 6. Compare `aud` to URL tenant → `CredentialWrongTenant`.\n//! 7. Look up `sub` in users table, check suspension → `UserSuspended`.\n//! 8. Check tenant membership → `NotATenantMember`.\n//! 9. Walk grants for URL database + HTTP-method-derived op → `DatabaseNotGranted` / `OpNotGranted`.\n//!\n//! On success, installs `ResolvedIdentity` into the request extension.\n//! On failure, returns a JSON error response built from `AuthError::status_code` and\n//! `AuthError::error_code`.\n\nuse std::collections::HashSet;\nuse std::sync::{Arc, Mutex, RwLock};\nuse std::time::{SystemTime, UNIX_EPOCH};\n\nuse axum::extract::State;\nuse axum::middleware::Next;\nuse axum::response::{IntoResponse, Response};\nuse http::{HeaderMap, Method, StatusCode};\nuse jsonwebtoken::{Algorithm, DecodingKey, EncodingKey, Header, Validation, decode, encode};\nuse serde_json::json;\nuse uuid::Uuid;\n\nuse axon_core::auth::{AuthError, JwtClaims, Op, ResolvedIdentity, TenantId, UserId};\nuse axon_storage::adapter::StorageAdapter;\n\n// ── JwtIssuer ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n/// Signs and verifies JWTs using HS256.\npub struct JwtIssuer {\n    secret: Vec<u8>,\n    /// Issuer identifier placed in the `iss` claim and verified on every request.\n    pub issuer_id: String,\n}\n\nimpl JwtIssuer {\n    /// Create a new issuer with the given HMAC secret and issuer ID.\n    pub fn new(secret: Vec<u8>, issuer_id: String) -> Self {\n        Self { secret, issuer_id }\n    }\n\n    /// Sign `claims` into a compact HS256 JWT string.\n    pub fn issue(&self, claims: &JwtClaims) -> Result<String, AuthError> {\n        encode(\n            &Header::new(Algorithm::HS256),\n            claims,\n            &EncodingKey::from_secret(&self.secret),\n        )\n        .map_err(|_| AuthError::CredentialMalformed)\n    }\n\n    /// Verify the signature and deserialise the claims.\n    ///\n    /// Time-based checks (`exp`, `nbf`) are intentionally skipped here; the\n    /// middleware performs them manually with a configurable skew budget.\n    pub fn verify(&self, token: &str) -> Result<JwtClaims, AuthError> {\n        let mut validation = Validation::new(Algorithm::HS256);\n        validation.validate_exp = false;\n        validation.validate_nbf = false;\n        validation.validate_aud = false;\n        validation.required_spec_claims = HashSet::new();\n\n        decode::<JwtClaims>(token, &DecodingKey::from_secret(&self.secret), &validation)\n            .map(|td| td.claims)\n            .map_err(|e| match e.kind() {\n                jsonwebtoken::errors::ErrorKind::InvalidSignature => AuthError::CredentialInvalid,\n                jsonwebtoken::errors::ErrorKind::Json(_) => AuthError::CredentialMalformed,\n                jsonwebtoken::errors::ErrorKind::Base64(_) => AuthError::CredentialMalformed,\n                jsonwebtoken::errors::ErrorKind::Utf8(_) => AuthError::CredentialMalformed,\n                jsonwebtoken::errors::ErrorKind::InvalidToken => AuthError::CredentialMalformed,\n                _ => AuthError::CredentialMalformed,\n            })\n    }\n}\n\n// ── InMemoryRevocationCache ───────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n/// In-process cache of revoked JWT IDs.\n///\n/// The middleware checks this first before falling back to the storage adapter,\n/// avoiding a round-trip to storage on every request for known-revoked tokens.\n#[derive(Default)]\npub struct InMemoryRevocationCache {\n    inner: RwLock<HashSet<Uuid>>,\n}\n\nimpl InMemoryRevocationCache {\n    /// Create an empty cache.\n    pub fn new() -> Self {\n        Self::default()\n    }\n\n    /// Returns `true` if `jti` is in the cache.\n    pub fn contains(&self, jti: &Uuid) -> bool {\n        self.inner\n            .read()\n            .map(|g| g.contains(jti))\n            .unwrap_or(false)\n    }\n\n    /// Add `jti` to the cache.\n    pub fn insert(&self, jti: Uuid) {\n        if let Ok(mut g) = self.inner.write() {\n            g.insert(jti);\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n// ── AuthPipelineState ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n/// Shared state for the JWT verification middleware.\n///\n/// Wrap in `Arc` before passing to `axum::middleware::from_fn_with_state`.\n#[derive(Clone)]\npub struct AuthPipelineState {\n    /// Token issuer / verifier.\n    pub issuer: Arc<JwtIssuer>,\n    /// Fast in-process revocation Axon server — HTTP gateway, gRPC service, MCP stdio, and authentication.\n//!\n//! # Authentication\n//!\n//! Identity is resolved once per request by [`auth::AuthContext`] and\n//! injected as a typed extension.  Three modes are supported:\n//!\n//! | Mode | Flag | Actor | Role |\n//! |------|------|-------|------|\n//! | `NoAuth` | `--no-auth` | `\"anonymous\"` | Admin |\n//! | `Tailscale` | *(default)* | node name | from ACL tags |\n//! | `Guest` | `--guest-role <role>` | `\"guest\"` | configured role |\n//!\n//! In `Tailscale` mode the server contacts the local Tailscale daemon over its\n//! Unix socket (`/run/tailscale/tailscaled.sock` by default) and calls\n//! `/localapi/v0/whois?addr=<peer>` to resolve the connecting node's identity.\n//! Resolved identities are cached by peer IP for the duration of\n//! `--auth-cache-ttl-secs` (default 60 s).\n//!\n//! ACL tag → role mapping:\n//! - `tag:axon-admin` → [`Role::Admin`]\n//! - `tag:axon-write` / `tag:axon-agent` → [`Role::Write`]\n//! - `tag:axon-read` → [`Role::Read`]\n//! - no matching tag → `--tailscale-default-role` (default `read`)\n//!\n//! Connections that are not on the tailnet are rejected with HTTP 401 / gRPC\n//! `UNAUTHENTICATED`.  If the Tailscale daemon is unreachable the server\n//! returns HTTP 503 / gRPC `UNAVAILABLE`.\n//!\n//! # Request flow\n//!\n//! ```text\n//! TCP accept\n//!   └─ authenticate_http_request  (gateway.rs middleware)\n//!        ├─ AuthContext::resolve_peer  →  Identity\n//!        └─ insert Identity into request extensions\n//!             └─ route handler\n//!                  ├─ extract Extension<Identity>\n//!                  ├─ identity.require_read() / require_write() / require_admin()\n//!                  └─ handler logic\n//! ```\n//!\n//! gRPC follows the same pattern via [`service::AxonServiceImpl::authorize`].\npub mod actor_scope;\npub mod auth;\nmod collection_listing;\npub(crate) mod embedded_ui;\npub mod control_plane;\npub mod control_plane_routes;\npub mod cors_config;\npub mod user_roles;\npub mod gateway;\npub mod idempotency;\npub(crate) mod mcp_http;\npub mod mcp_stdio;\npub mod rate_limit;\npub mod schema_registry;\npub mod serve;\npub mod service;\npub mod tenant_router;\n\npub use auth::{AuthContext, AuthMode, Identity, Role};\npub use mcp_stdio::run_mcp_stdio;\npub use service::AxonServiceImpl;\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":43,"oldLines":6,"newStart":43,"newLines":7,"lines":[" //! gRPC follows the same pattern via [`service::AxonServiceImpl::authorize`]."," pub mod actor_scope;"," pub mod auth;","+pub mod auth_pipeline;"," mod collection_listing;"," pub(crate) mod embedded_ui;"," pub mod control_plane;"]},{"oldStart":60,"oldLines":5,"newStart":61,"newLines":6,"lines":[" pub mod tenant_router;"," "," pub use auth::{AuthContext, AuthMode, Identity, Role};","+pub use auth_pipeline::{InMemoryRevocationCache, JwtIssuer, jwt_verify_layer};"," pub use mcp_stdio::run_mcp_stdio;"," pub use service::AxonServiceImpl;"]}],"userModified":false,"replaceAll":false}}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-sonnet-4-6","id":"msg_01CK6wwgEq2n1LP4QVdfHsPL","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01G11hf2mEssXRvin3Et23Gc","name":"Write","input":{"file_path":"/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-axon-dccbdf73-20260415T013041-1340da9f/crates/axon-server/tests/auth_pipeline_test.rs","content":"//! Unit tests for the JWT auth pipeline (ADR-018 §4).\n//!\n//! Covers:\n//! - Every row of the failure-mode table (14 variants)\n//! - JWT round-trip\n//! - ResolvedIdentity installation in request extensions\n//! - Revocation cache invalidation\n//! - Clock-skew boundary at ±30 s\n//! - HTTP-method × grant-ops matrix\n\nuse std::sync::{Arc, Mutex};\nuse std::time::{SystemTime, UNIX_EPOCH};\n\nuse axum::body::Body;\nuse axum::extract::Extension;\nuse axum::http::StatusCode;\nuse axum::response::IntoResponse;\nuse axum::routing::get;\nuse axum::Router;\nuse http::Request;\nuse serde::Serialize;\nuse serde_json::Value;\nuse tower::ServiceExt;\nuse uuid::Uuid;\n\nuse axon_core::auth::{\n    AuthError, GrantedDatabase, Grants, JwtClaims, Op, ResolvedIdentity, TenantId, TenantMember,\n    TenantRole, User, UserId,\n};\nuse axon_server::auth_pipeline::{\n    AuthErrorResponse, AuthPipelineState, InMemoryRevocationCache, JwtIssuer,\n    jwt_verify_layer,\n};\nuse axon_storage::MemoryStorageAdapter;\n\n// ── Test helpers ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\nconst ISSUER_ID: &str = \"test-issuer\";\nconst TENANT: &str = \"acme\";\nconst DATABASE: &str = \"orders\";\nconst USER_ID: &str = \"user-01\";\nconst SECRET: &[u8] = b\"super-secret-key-for-tests\";\nconst TEST_PATH: &str = \"/tenants/acme/databases/orders/ping\";\n\nfn now_secs() -> u64 {\n    SystemTime::now()\n        .duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH)\n        .unwrap_or_default()\n        .as_secs()\n}\n\nfn valid_claims() -> JwtClaims {\n    let now = now_secs();\n    JwtClaims {\n        iss: ISSUER_ID.to_string(),\n        sub: USER_ID.to_string(),\n        aud: TENANT.to_string(),\n        jti: Uuid::now_v7().to_string(),\n        iat: now,\n        nbf: now,\n        exp: now + 3600,\n        grants: Grants {\n            databases: vec![GrantedDatabase {\n                name: DATABASE.to_string(),\n                ops: vec![Op::Read, Op::Write],\n            }],\n        },\n    }\n}\n\nfn make_storage() -> MemoryStorageAdapter {\n    let mut storage = MemoryStorageAdapter::default();\n    storage.insert_user(User {\n        id: UserId::new(USER_ID),\n        display_name: \"Test User\".to_string(),\n        email: None,\n        created_at_ms: 0,\n        suspended_at_ms: None,\n    });\n    storage.insert_tenant_member(TenantMember {\n        tenant_id: TenantId::new(TENANT),\n        user_id: UserId::new(USER_ID),\n        role: TenantRole::Write,\n    });\n    storage\n}\n\nfn make_state() -> Arc<AuthPipelineState> {\n    let issuer = JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string());\n    let storage: Box<dyn axon_storage::StorageAdapter + Send + Sync> =\n        Box::new(make_storage());\n    Arc::new(AuthPipelineState {\n        issuer: Arc::new(issuer),\n        revocation_cache: Arc::new(InMemoryRevocationCache::new()),\n        storage: Arc::new(Mutex::new(storage)),\n    })\n}\n\nfn make_app(state: Arc<AuthPipelineState>) -> Router {\n    Router::new()\n        .route(TEST_PATH, get(|| async { StatusCode::OK }))\n        .layer(axum::middleware::from_fn_with_state(\n            state,\n            jwt_verify_layer,\n        ))\n}\n\nasync fn send(app: Router, req: Request<Body>) -> (StatusCode, Value) {\n    let resp = app.oneshot(req).await.expect(\"service call should succeed\");\n    let status = resp.status();\n    let bytes = axum::body::to_bytes(resp.into_body(), 64 * 1024)\n        .await\n        .expect(\"body read should succeed\");\n    let json: Value = serde_json::from_slice(&bytes).unwrap_or(Value::Null);\n    (status, json)\n}\n\nfn get_req(path: &str) -> Request<Body> {\n    Request::builder()\n        .method(\"GET\")\n        .uri(path)\n        .body(Body::empty())\n        .unwrap()\n}\n\nfn bearer_req(path: &str, token: &str) -> Request<Body> {\n    Request::builder()\n        .method(\"GET\")\n        .uri(path)\n        .header(\"Authorization\", format!(\"Bearer {token}\"))\n        .body(Body::empty())\n        .unwrap()\n}\n\nfn error_code(json: &Value) -> &str {\n    json[\"error\"][\"code\"].as_str().unwrap_or(\"\")\n}\n\n// ── JWT round-trip ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n#[test]\nfn jwt_roundtrip_succeeds() {\n    let issuer = JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string());\n    let claims = valid_claims();\n    let token = issuer.issue(&claims).expect(\"issue should succeed\");\n    let decoded = issuer.verify(&token).expect(\"verify should succeed\");\n    assert_eq!(decoded.iss, claims.iss);\n    assert_eq!(decoded.sub, claims.sub);\n    assert_eq!(decoded.aud, claims.aud);\n    assert_eq!(decoded.jti, claims.jti);\n    assert_eq!(decoded.grants, claims.grants);\n}\n\n// ── ADR-018 §4 failure-mode table ─────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n/// Row 1: No Authorization header → 401 unauthenticated\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn error_no_auth_header() {\n    let state = make_state();\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let req = get_req(TEST_PATH);\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED);\n    assert_eq!(error_code(&json), \"unauthenticated\");\n}\n\n/// Row 2a: Header present but not `Bearer` → 401 credential_malformed\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn error_not_bearer_scheme() {\n    let state = make_state();\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let req = Request::builder()\n        .uri(TEST_PATH)\n        .header(\"Authorization\", \"Basic dXNlcjpwYXNz\")\n        .body(Body::empty())\n        .unwrap();\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED);\n    assert_eq!(error_code(&json), \"credential_malformed\");\n}\n\n/// Row 2b: JWT structurally invalid → 401 credential_malformed\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn error_jwt_malformed() {\n    let state = make_state();\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let req = bearer_req(TEST_PATH, \"not.a.valid.jwt\");\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED);\n    assert_eq!(error_code(&json), \"credential_malformed\");\n}\n\n/// Row 2c: `aud` as JSON array → 401 credential_malformed\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn error_aud_is_array() {\n    #[derive(Serialize)]\n    struct ArrayAudClaims {\n        iss: String,\n        sub: String,\n        aud: Vec<String>,\n        jti: String,\n        iat: u64,\n        nbf: u64,\n        exp: u64,\n        grants: Grants,\n    }\n    let now = now_secs();\n    let bad_claims = ArrayAudClaims {\n        iss: ISSUER_ID.to_string(),\n        sub: USER_ID.to_string(),\n        aud: vec![TENANT.to_string()], // array, not string\n        jti: Uuid::now_v7().to_string(),\n        iat: now,\n        nbf: now,\n        exp: now + 3600,\n        grants: Grants {\n            databases: vec![GrantedDatabase {\n                name: DATABASE.to_string(),\n                ops: vec![Op::Read],\n            }],\n        },\n    };\n    let token = jsonwebtoken::encode(\n        &jsonwebtoken::Header::new(jsonwebtoken::Algorithm::HS256),\n        &bad_claims,\n        &jsonwebtoken::EncodingKey::from_secret(SECRET),\n    )\n    .unwrap();\n    let state = make_state();\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let req = bearer_req(TEST_PATH, &token);\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED);\n    assert_eq!(error_code(&json), \"credential_malformed\");\n}\n\n/// Row 3: Signature invalid → 401 credential_invalid\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn error_signature_invalid() {\n    let wrong_issuer = JwtIssuer::new(b\"wrong-secret\".to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string());\n    let claims = valid_claims();\n    let token = wrong_issuer.issue(&claims).unwrap();\n    let state = make_state();\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let req = bearer_req(TEST_PATH, &token);\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED);\n    assert_eq!(error_code(&json), \"credential_invalid\");\n}\n\n/// Row 4: `exp` in past beyond skew → 401 credential_expired\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn error_exp_expired() {\n    let issuer = JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string());\n    let now = now_secs();\n    let mut claims = valid_claims();\n    claims.exp = now - 31; // 31 seconds in the past, beyond the 30s skew\n    let token = issuer.issue(&claims).unwrap();\n    let state = make_state();\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let req = bearer_req(TEST_PATH, &token);\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED);\n    assert_eq!(error_code(&json), \"credential_expired\");\n}\n\n/// Row 5: `nbf` in future beyond skew → 401 credential_not_yet_valid\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn error_nbf_future() {\n    let issuer = JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string());\n    let now = now_secs();\n    let mut claims = valid_claims();\n    claims.nbf = now + 31; // 31 seconds from now, beyond the 30s skew\n    let token = issuer.issue(&claims).unwrap();\n    let state = make_state();\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let req = bearer_req(TEST_PATH, &token);\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED);\n    assert_eq!(error_code(&json), \"credential_not_yet_valid\");\n}\n\n/// Row 6: `jti` revoked → 401 credential_revoked\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn error_jti_revoked() {\n    let issuer = JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string());\n    let claims = valid_claims();\n    let jti_uuid: Uuid = claims.jti.parse().unwrap();\n    let token = issuer.issue(&claims).unwrap();\n\n    let state = make_state();\n    // Insert the JTI directly into the revocation cache.\n    state.revocation_cache.insert(jti_uuid);\n\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let req = bearer_req(TEST_PATH, &token);\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED);\n    assert_eq!(error_code(&json), \"credential_revoked\");\n}\n\n/// Row 7: `iss` unknown → 401 credential_foreign_issuer\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn error_foreign_issuer() {\n    let issuer = JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string());\n    let mut claims = valid_claims();\n    claims.iss = \"some-other-issuer\".to_string();\n    let token = issuer.issue(&claims).unwrap();\n    let state = make_state();\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let req = bearer_req(TEST_PATH, &token);\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED);\n    assert_eq!(error_code(&json), \"credential_foreign_issuer\");\n}\n\n/// Row 8: `aud` ≠ URL tenant → 403 credential_wrong_tenant\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn error_wrong_tenant() {\n    let issuer = JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string());\n    let mut claims = valid_claims();\n    claims.aud = \"other-tenant\".to_string(); // URL says \"acme\"\n    let token = issuer.issue(&claims).unwrap();\n    let state = make_state();\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let req = bearer_req(TEST_PATH, &token);\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status.as_u16(), 403);\n    assert_eq!(error_code(&json), \"credential_wrong_tenant\");\n}\n\n/// Row 9: `sub` suspended → 401 user_suspended\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn error_user_suspended() {\n    let issuer = JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), Unit tests for the JWT auth pipeline (ADR-018 §4).\n//!\n//! Covers:\n//! - Every row of the failure-mode table (14 variants)\n//! - JWT round-trip\n//! - ResolvedIdentity installation in request extensions\n//! - Revocation cache invalidation\n//! - Clock-skew boundary at ±30 s\n//! - HTTP-method × grant-ops matrix\n\nuse std::sync::{Arc, Mutex};\nuse std::time::{SystemTime, UNIX_EPOCH};\n\nuse axum::body::Body;\nuse axum::extract::Extension;\nuse axum::http::StatusCode;\nuse axum::response::IntoResponse;\nuse axum::routing::get;\nuse axum::Router;\nuse http::Request;\nuse serde::Serialize;\nuse serde_json::Value;\nuse tower::ServiceExt;\nuse uuid::Uuid;\n\nuse axon_core::auth::{\n    AuthError, GrantedDatabase, Grants, JwtClaims, Op, ResolvedIdentity, TenantId, TenantMember,\n    TenantRole, User, UserId,\n};\nuse axon_server::auth_pipeline::{\n    AuthErrorResponse, AuthPipelineState, InMemoryRevocationCache, JwtIssuer,\n    jwt_verify_layer,\n};\nuse axon_storage::MemoryStorageAdapter;\n\n// ── Test helpers ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\nconst ISSUER_ID: &str = \"test-issuer\";\nconst TENANT: &str = \"acme\";\nconst DATABASE: &str = \"orders\";\nconst USER_ID: &str = \"user-01\";\nconst SECRET: &[u8] = b\"super-secret-key-for-tests\";\nconst TEST_PATH: &str = \"/tenants/acme/databases/orders/ping\";\n\nfn now_secs() -> u64 {\n    SystemTime::now()\n        .duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH)\n        .unwrap_or_default()\n        .as_secs()\n}\n\nfn valid_claims() -> JwtClaims {\n    let now = now_secs();\n    JwtClaims {\n        iss: ISSUER_ID.to_string(),\n        sub: USER_ID.to_string(),\n        aud: TENANT.to_string(),\n        jti: Uuid::now_v7().to_string(),\n        iat: now,\n        nbf: now,\n        exp: now + 3600,\n        grants: Grants {\n            databases: vec![GrantedDatabase {\n                name: DATABASE.to_string(),\n                ops: vec![Op::Read, Op::Write],\n            }],\n        },\n    }\n}\n\nfn make_storage() -> MemoryStorageAdapter {\n    let mut storage = MemoryStorageAdapter::default();\n    storage.insert_user(User {\n        id: UserId::new(USER_ID),\n        display_name: \"Test User\".to_string(),\n        email: None,\n        created_at_ms: 0,\n        suspended_at_ms: None,\n    });\n    storage.insert_tenant_member(TenantMember {\n        tenant_id: TenantId::new(TENANT),\n        user_id: UserId::new(USER_ID),\n        role: TenantRole::Write,\n    });\n    storage\n}\n\nfn make_state() -> Arc<AuthPipelineState> {\n    let issuer = JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string());\n    let storage: Box<dyn axon_storage::StorageAdapter + Send + Sync> =\n        Box::new(make_storage());\n    Arc::new(AuthPipelineState {\n        issuer: Arc::new(issuer),\n        revocation_cache: Arc::new(InMemoryRevocationCache::new()),\n        storage: Arc::new(Mutex::new(storage)),\n    })\n}\n\nfn make_app(state: Arc<AuthPipelineState>) -> Router {\n    Router::new()\n        .route(TEST_PATH, get(|| async { StatusCode::OK }))\n        .layer(axum::middleware::from_fn_with_state(\n            state,\n            jwt_verify_layer,\n        ))\n}\n\nasync fn send(app: Router, req: Request<Body>) -> (StatusCode, Value) {\n    let resp = app.oneshot(req).await.expect(\"service call should succeed\");\n    let status = resp.status();\n    let bytes = axum::body::to_bytes(resp.into_body(), 64 * 1024)\n        .await\n        .expect(\"body read should succeed\");\n    let json: Value = serde_json::from_slice(&bytes).unwrap_or(Value::Null);\n    (status, json)\n}\n\nfn get_req(path: &str) -> Request<Body> {\n    Request::builder()\n        .method(\"GET\")\n        .uri(path)\n        .body(Body::empty())\n        .unwrap()\n}\n\nfn bearer_req(path: &str, token: &str) -> Request<Body> {\n    Request::builder()\n        .method(\"GET\")\n        .uri(path)\n        .header(\"Authorization\", format!(\"Bearer {token}\"))\n        .body(Body::empty())\n        .unwrap()\n}\n\nfn error_code(json: &Value) -> &str {\n    json[\"error\"][\"code\"].as_str().unwrap_or(\"\")\n}\n\n// ── JWT round-trip ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n#[test]\nfn jwt_roundtrip_succeeds() {\n    let issuer = JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string());\n    let claims = valid_claims();\n    let token = issuer.issue(&claims).expect(\"issue should succeed\");\n    let decoded = issuer.verify(&token).expect(\"verify should succeed\");\n    assert_eq!(decoded.iss, claims.iss);\n    assert_eq!(decoded.sub, claims.sub);\n    assert_eq!(decoded.aud, claims.aud);\n    assert_eq!(decoded.jti, claims.jti);\n    assert_eq!(decoded.grants, claims.grants);\n}\n\n// ── ADR-018 §4 failure-mode table ─────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n/// Row 1: No Authorization header → 401 unauthenticated\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn error_no_auth_header() {\n    let state = make_state();\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let req = get_req(TEST_PATH);\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED);\n    assert_eq!(error_code(&json), \"unauthenticated\");\n}\n\n/// Row 2a: Header present but not `Bearer` → 401 credential_malformed\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn error_not_bearer_scheme() {\n    let state = make_state();\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let req = Request::builder()\n        .uri(TEST_PATH)\n        .header(\"Authorization\", \"Basic dXNlcjpwYXNz\")\n        .body(Body::empty())\n        .unwrap();\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED);\n    assert_eq!(error_code(&json), \"credential_malformed\");\n}\n\n/// Row 2b: JWT structurally invalid → 401 credential_malformed\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn error_jwt_malformed() {\n    let state = make_state();\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let req = bearer_req(TEST_PATH, \"not.a.valid.jwt\");\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED);\n    assert_eq!(error_code(&json), \"credential_malformed\");\n}\n\n/// Row 2c: `aud` as JSON array → 401 credential_malformed\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn error_aud_is_array() {\n    #[derive(Serialize)]\n    struct ArrayAudClaims {\n        iss: String,\n        sub: String,\n        aud: Vec<String>,\n        jti: String,\n        iat: u64,\n        nbf: u64,\n        exp: u64,\n        grants: Grants,\n    }\n    let now = now_secs();\n    let bad_claims = ArrayAudClaims {\n        iss: ISSUER_ID.to_string(),\n        sub: USER_ID.to_string(),\n        aud: vec![TENANT.to_string()], // array, not string\n        jti: Uuid::now_v7().to_string(),\n        iat: now,\n        nbf: now,\n        exp: now + 3600,\n        grants: Grants {\n            databases: vec![GrantedDatabase {\n                name: DATABASE.to_string(),\n                ops: vec![Op::Read],\n            }],\n        },\n    };\n    let token = jsonwebtoken::encode(\n        &jsonwebtoken::Header::new(jsonwebtoken::Algorithm::HS256),\n        &bad_claims,\n        &jsonwebtoken::EncodingKey::from_secret(SECRET),\n    )\n    .unwrap();\n    let state = make_state();\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let req = bearer_req(TEST_PATH, &token);\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED);\n    assert_eq!(error_code(&json), \"credential_malformed\");\n}\n\n/// Row 3: Signature invalid → 401 credential_invalid\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn error_signature_invalid() {\n    let wrong_issuer = JwtIssuer::new(b\"wrong-secret\".to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string());\n    let claims = valid_claims();\n    let token = wrong_issuer.issue(&claims).unwrap();\n    let state = make_state();\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let req = bearer_req(TEST_PATH, &token);\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED);\n    assert_eq!(error_code(&json), \"credential_invalid\");\n}\n\n/// Row 4: `exp` in past beyond skew → 401 credential_expired\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn error_exp_expired() {\n    let issuer = JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string());\n    let now = now_secs();\n    let mut claims = valid_claims();\n    claims.exp = now - 31; // 31 seconds in the past, beyond the 30s skew\n    let token = assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED);\n    assert_eq!(error_code(&json), \"credential_expired\");\n}\n\n/// Row 5: `nbf` in future beyond skew → 401 credential_not_yet_valid\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn error_nbf_future() {\n    let issuer = JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string());\n    let now = now_secs();\n    let mut claims = URL tenant → 403 credential_wrong_tenant\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn error_wrong_tenant() {\n    let issuer = JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string());\n    let mut claims = \"credential_wrong_tenant\");\n}\n\n/// Row 9: `sub` suspended → 401 user_suspended\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn error_user_suspended() {\n    let issuer = JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), created_at_ms: 0,\n        suspended_at_ms: None,\n    });\n    let state = Arc::new(AuthPipelineState {\n        issuer: Arc::new(JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string())),\n = JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string());\n    let mut claims = valid_claims();\n    // Grant is for \"products\", URL database is \"orders\"\n    claims.grants = Grants {\n state.revocation_cache.insert(jti_uuid);\n\n    // Second request must fail with credential_revoked.\n    let (status, json) = send(app_after, bearer_req(TEST_PATH, &token)).await;\n Case { method: Method::PATCH, ops: vec![Op::Read], expected_status: 403 },\n        Case { method: Method::DELETE, ops: vec![Op::Write], expected_status: 200 },\n        Case { method: Method::DELETE, ops: vec![Op::Read], expected_status: 403 },\n    ];\n\n    // Use a route that accepts all HTTP methods.\n    let handler = || async { StatusCode::OK };\n\n    for case in cases {\n        let issuer_inner = JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string());\n        let mut claims = valid_claims();\n        claims.grants = Grants {\n            databases: vec![GrantedDatabase {\n .method(case.method.clone())\n            .uri(TEST_PATH)\n            .header(\"Authorization\", format!(\"Bearer {token}\"))\n            .body(Body::empty())\n            .unwrap();\n        let // Verify every variant maps to the correct status and code string.\n    let cases: &[(AuthError, u16, &str)] = &[\n        (AuthError::Unauthenticated, 401, \"unauthenticated\"),\n        (AuthError::CredentialMalformed, 401, \"credential_malformed\"),\n        (AuthError::CredentialInvalid, 401, \"credential_invalid\"),\n        (AuthError::CredentialExpired, 401,
