Integration tests for the JWT auth middleware (ADR-018 §4).\n//!\n//! Builds a minimal axum router with a single protected data-plane route:\n//!   GET /tenants/acme/databases/orders/ping\n//!\n//! Tests the four golden paths required by the bead:\n//! 1. No auth header → 401 unauthenticated\n//! 2. Valid JWT with correct grants → 200\n//! 3. Valid JWT with wrong tenant in aud → 403 credential_wrong_tenant\n//! 4. Valid JWT with grants for a different database → 403 database_not_granted\n\nuse std::sync::{Arc, Mutex};\nuse std::time::{SystemTime, UNIX_EPOCH};\n\nuse axum::body::Body;\nuse axum::http::StatusCode;\nuse axum::routing::get;\nuse axum::Router;\nuse http::Request;\nuse serde_json::Value;\nuse tower::ServiceExt;\nuse uuid::Uuid;\n\nuse axon_core::auth::{\n    GrantedDatabase, Grants, JwtClaims, Op, TenantId, TenantMember, TenantRole, User, UserId,\n};\nuse axon_server::auth_pipeline::{AuthPipelineState, InMemoryRevocationCache, JwtIssuer, jwt_verify_layer};\nuse axon_storage::MemoryStorageAdapter;\n\nconst ISSUER: &str = \"integration-issuer\";\nconst TENANT: &str = \"acme\";\nconst DATABASE: &str = \"orders\";\nconst USER_ID: &str = \"u-integration-01\";\nconst SECRET: &[u8] = b\"integration-test-secret\";\nconst PROTECTED_PATH: &str = \"/tenants/acme/databases/orders/ping\";\n\nfn now_secs() -> u64 {\n    SystemTime::now()\n        .duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH)\n        .unwrap_or_default()\n        .as_secs()\n}\n\nfn make_state() -> Arc<AuthPipelineState> {\n    let mut storage = MemoryStorageAdapter::default();\n    storage.insert_user(User {\n        id: UserId::new(USER_ID),\n        display_name: \"Integration User\".to_string(),\n        email: None,\n        created_at_ms: 0,\n        suspended_at_ms: None,\n    });\n    storage.insert_tenant_member(TenantMember {\n        tenant_id: TenantId::new(TENANT),\n        user_id: UserId::new(USER_ID),\n        role: TenantRole::Write,\n    });\n    Arc::new(AuthPipelineState {\n        issuer: Arc::new(JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER.to_string())),\n        revocation_cache: Arc::new(InMemoryRevocationCache::new()),\n        storage: Arc::new(Mutex::new(\n            Box::new(storage) as Box<dyn axon_storage::StorageAdapter + Send + Sync>\n        )),\n    })\n}\n\nfn make_app(state: Arc<AuthPipelineState>) -> Router {\n    Router::new()\n        .route(PROTECTED_PATH, get(|| async { StatusCode::OK }))\n        .layer(axum::middleware::from_fn_with_state(\n            state,\n            jwt_verify_layer,\n        ))\n}\n\nasync fn send(app: Router, req: Request<Body>) -> (StatusCode, Value) {\n    let resp = app.oneshot(req).await.expect(\"service should not fail\");\n    let status = resp.status();\n    let bytes = axum::body::to_bytes(resp.into_body(), 64 * 1024)\n        .await\n        .unwrap_or_default();\n    let json: Value = serde_json::from_slice(&bytes).unwrap_or(Value::Null);\n    (status, json)\n}\n\nfn bearer(token: &str) -> String {\n    format!(\"Bearer {token}\")\n}\n\nfn valid_token(issuer: &JwtIssuer, grants: Grants) -> String {\n    let now = now_secs();\n    let claims = JwtClaims {\n        iss: ISSUER.to_string(),\n        sub: USER_ID.to_string(),\n        aud: TENANT.to_string(),\n        jti: Uuid::now_v7().to_string(),\n        iat: now,\n        nbf: now,\n        exp: now + 3600,\n        grants,\n    };\n    issuer.issue(&claims).expect(\"issue should succeed\")\n}\n\nfn orders_read_grants() -> Grants {\n    Grants {\n        databases: vec![GrantedDatabase {\n            name: DATABASE.to_string(),\n            ops: vec![Op::Read],\n        }],\n    }\n}\n\n// ── Test 1: No auth header → 401 unauthenticated ──────────────────────────────\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn integration_no_auth_header_returns_401() {\n    let state = make_state();\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let req = Request::builder()\n        .method(\"GET\")\n        .uri(PROTECTED_PATH)\n        .body(Body::empty())\n        .unwrap();\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED);\n    assert_eq!(\n        json[\"error\"][\"code\"].as_str().unwrap_or(\"\"),\n        \"unauthenticated\"\n    );\n}\n\n// ── Test 2: Valid JWT with read grants → 200 ──────────────────────────────────\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn integration_valid_jwt_with_read_grant_returns_200() {\n    let state = make_state();\n    let token = valid_token(&state.issuer, orders_read_grants());\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let req = Request::builder()\n        .method(\"GET\")\n        .uri(PROTECTED_PATH)\n        .header(\"Authorization\", bearer(&token))\n        .body(Body::empty())\n        .unwrap();\n    let (status, _) = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::OK);\n}\n\n// ── Test 3: Wrong tenant in aud → 403 credential_wrong_tenant ─────────────────\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn integration_wrong_tenant_returns_403() {\n    let state = make_state();\n    let now = now_secs();\n    let claims = JwtClaims {\n        iss: ISSUER.to_string(),\n        sub: USER_ID.to_string(),\n        aud: \"different-tenant\".to_string(), // URL says \"acme\"\n        jti: Uuid::now_v7().to_string(),\n        iat: now,\n        nbf: now,\n        exp: now + 3600,\n        grants: orders_read_grants(),\n    };\n    let token = state.issuer.issue(&claims).unwrap();\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let req = Request::builder()\n        .method(\"GET\")\n        .uri(PROTECTED_PATH)\n        .header(\"Authorization\", bearer(&token))\n        .body(Body::empty())\n        .unwrap();\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status.as_u16(), 403);\n    assert_eq!(\n        json[\"error\"][\"code\"].as_str().unwrap_or(\"\"),\n        \"credential_wrong_tenant\"\n    );\n}\n\n// ── Test 4: Grants for different database → 403 database_not_granted ──────────\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn integration_wrong_database_returns_403() {\n    let state = make_state();\n    let grants = Grants {\n        databases: vec![GrantedDatabase {\n            name: \"invoices\".to_string(), // URL says \"orders\"\n            ops: vec![Op::Read],\n        }],\n    };\n    let token = valid_token(&state.issuer, grants);\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let req = Request::builder()\n        .method(\"GET\")\n        .uri(PROTECTED_PATH)\n        .header(\"Authorization\", bearer(&token))\n        .body(Body::empty())\n        .unwrap();\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status.as_u16(), 403);\n    assert_eq!(\n        json[\"error\"][\"code\"].as_str().unwrap_or(\"\"),\n        \"database_not_granted\"\n    );\n}\n"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":575,"cache_read_input_tokens":132304,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":575},"output_tokens":2,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"80f0eb9d-4064-4d06-8560-e247ce1170f2","uuid":"cc1c03fa-01b8-4915-97d1-edc4d799b3f6"}

SvelteKit client-side routing works correctly.\n//!\n//! **Build prerequisite**: `ui/build` must exist before `cargo build`.\n//! Run `cd ui && bun run build` (or `npm run build`) first.\n\nuse axum::body::Body;\nuse axum::http::{header, StatusCode, Uri};\nuse axum::response::{IntoResponse, Response};\nuse rust_embed::RustEmbed;\n\n/// All files under `ui/build`, embedded at compile time.\n///\n/// The `#[folder]` path is relative to the crate root (`crates/axon-server/`),\n/// so `../../ui/build` resolves to `ui/build` in the workspace root.\n#[derive(RustEmbed)]\n#[folder = \"../../ui/build\"]\nstruct UiAssets;\n\n/// Axum handler for `GET /ui` and `GET /ui/*path`.\n///\n/// Strips the `/ui` prefix, looks up the file in [`UiAssets`], and responds\n/// with the correct `Content-Type`.  Unknown paths fall back to `index.html`\n/// so SvelteKit's client-side router can handle them.\npub async fn embedded_ui_handler(uri: Uri) -> Response {\n    let raw = uri.path();\n    // Strip the /ui prefix that axum leaves on the URI when not using nest_service.\n    let asset_path = raw\n        .strip_prefix(\"/ui/\")\n        .or_else(|| raw.strip_prefix(\"/ui\"))\n        .unwrap_or(raw);\n    let asset_path = if asset_path.is_empty() {\n        \"index.html\"\n    } else {\n        asset_path\n    };\n\n    serve_asset(asset_path)\n}\n\nfn serve_asset(path: &str) -> Response {\n    match UiAssets::get(path) {\n        Some(content) => {\n            let mime = mime_guess::from_path(path).first_or_octet_stream();\n            Response::builder()\n                .header(header::CONTENT_TYPE, mime.as_ref())\n                .body(Body::from(content.data))\n                .unwrap_or_else(|_| StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR.into_response())\n        }\n        None => {\n            // SPA fallback: let the client-side router handle unknown paths.\n            match UiAssets::get(\"index.html\") {\n                Some(index) => Response::builder()\n                    .header(header::CONTENT_TYPE, \"text/html; charset=utf-8\")\n                    .body(Body::from(index.data))\n                    .unwrap_or_else(|_| StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR.into_response()),\n                None => StatusCode::NOT_FOUND.into_response(),\n            }\n        }\n    }\n}\n","numLines":66,"startLine":1,"totalLines":66}}}

axum::http::StatusCode;\nuse axum::response::IntoResponse;\nuse axum::routing::get;\nuse axum::Router;\nuse http::Request;\nuse serde::Serialize;\nuse serde_json::Value;\nuse tower::ServiceExt;\nuse uuid::Uuid;\n\nuse axon_core::auth::{\n    AuthError, GrantedDatabase, Grants, JwtClaims, Op, ResolvedIdentity, TenantId, TenantMember,\n    TenantRole, User, UserId,\n};\nuse axon_server::auth_pipeline::{\n    AuthErrorResponse, AuthPipelineState, InMemoryRevocationCache, JwtIssuer,\n    jwt_verify_layer,\n};\nuse axon_storage::MemoryStorageAdapter;\n\n// ── Test helpers ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\nconst ISSUER_ID: &str = \"test-issuer\";\nconst TENANT: &str = \"acme\";\nconst DATABASE: &str = \"orders\";\nconst USER_ID: &str = \"user-01\";\nconst SECRET: &[u8] = b\"super-secret-key-for-tests\";\nconst TEST_PATH: &str = \"/tenants/acme/databases/orders/ping\";\n\nfn now_secs() -> u64 {\n    SystemTime::now()\n        .duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH)\n        .unwrap_or_default()\n        .as_secs()\n}\n\nfn valid_claims() -> JwtClaims {\n    let now = now_secs();\n    JwtClaims {\n        iss: ISSUER_ID.to_string(),\n        sub: USER_ID.to_string(),\n        aud: TENANT.to_string(),\n        jti: Uuid::now_v7().to_string(),\n        iat: now,\n        nbf: now,\n        exp: now + 3600,\n        grants: Grants {\n            databases: vec![GrantedDatabase {\n                name: DATABASE.to_string(),\n                ops: vec![Op::Read, Op::Write],\n            }],\n        },\n    }\n}\n\nfn make_storage() -> MemoryStorageAdapter {\n    let mut storage = MemoryStorageAdapter::default();\n    storage.insert_user(User {\n        id: UserId::new(USER_ID),\n        display_name: \"Test User\".to_string(),\n        email: None,\n        created_at_ms: 0,\n        suspended_at_ms: None,\n    });\n    storage.insert_tenant_member(TenantMember {\n        tenant_id: TenantId::new(TENANT),\n        user_id: UserId::new(USER_ID),\n        role: TenantRole::Write,\n    });\n    storage\n}\n\nfn make_state() -> Arc<AuthPipelineState> {\n    let issuer = JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string());\n    let storage: Box<dyn axon_storage::StorageAdapter + Send + Sync> =\n        Box::new(make_storage());\n    Arc::new(AuthPipelineState {\n        issuer: Arc::new(issuer),\n        revocation_cache: Arc::new(InMemoryRevocationCache::new()),\n        storage: Arc::new(Mutex::new(storage)),\n    })\n}\n\nfn make_app(state: Arc<AuthPipelineState>) -> {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Base directory for this skill: /home/erik/.claude/skills/helix-commit\n\n# Commit: HELIX-Compliant Git Commit\n\nCreate a well-formatted commit with issue traceability, run the project's\nbuild gate, push, and optionally update the tracker.\n\n## When to Use\n\n- After implementing a tracked issue\n- When you need a properly formatted commit message\n- When codex or another agent needs commit support\n\n## What It Does\n\n1. **Inspect** staged changes (`git diff --cached --stat`)\n2. **Format** commit message:\n   - First line: `<issue-id> <concise summary>` (under 72 chars)\n   - Body: what changed and why, governing artifact refs\n   - Trailer: verification commands run\n3. **Pre-push gate**: run the project's build check before pushing\n   - `lefthook run pre-commit` if available\n   - `cargo check --workspace` for Rust projects\n   - `npm test` for Node.js projects\n4. **Commit and push**: `git commit`, `git pull --rebase`, `git push`\n5. **Tracker update** (if issue-id provided): close the issue\n\n## Commit Message Format\n\n```\nhx-abc123 Short summary of the change\n\nLonger description of what changed and why. Reference governing\nartifacts when relevant (SD-001, TP-002, etc.).\n\nVerification: cargo test -p changed-crate, cargo clippy\n```\n\n## Examples\n\n```bash\nhelix commit hx-abc123      # commit, gate, push, close issue\nhelix commit                 # commit and gate without tracker update\n```\n"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"80f0eb9d-4064-4d06-8560-e247ce1170f2","uuid":"389e7b5b-4f0d-4836-a072-b1bc977baa31","timestamp":"2026-04-15T01:49:25.805Z","isSynthetic":true}

commit -m \"$(cat <<'EOF'\nfeat(auth-pipeline): sign + verify JWT + middleware + revocation [axon-dccbdf73]\n\nImplements the full ADR-018 §4 JWT verification middleware in axon-server.\n\n## What changed\n\n### axon-core (additive)\n- `ResolvedIdentity { user_id, tenant_id, grants }` — installed by middleware\n- `AuthError::status_code()` / `error_code()` — 401/403 + stable code strings\n- `Grants::find_database()` / `GrantedDatabase::has_op()` — grant helpers\n- `GrantedDatabase`, `Grants`, `ResolvedIdentity` re-exported from lib.rs\n\n### axon-storage (additive)\n- `StorageAdapter::is_jti_revoked(Uuid)`, `get_user(UserId)`,\n  `get_tenant_member(TenantId, UserId)` with default no-op impls\n- `MemoryStorageAdapter`: stores revoked JTIs, users, tenant_members;\n  test helpers `insert_user`, `insert_tenant_member`, `revoke_jti`\n- SQLite + Postgres: query `credential_revocations`, `users`, `tenant_users`;\n  graceful no-op when auth tables not yet migrated\n\n### axon-server (new)\n- `auth_pipeline.rs`: `JwtIssuer` (HS256 sign/verify), `InMemoryRevocationCache`,\n  `AuthPipelineState`, `jwt_verify_layer` (8-step ADR-018 verification order),\n  `extract_tenant_database`, `AuthErrorResponse` (IntoResponse bridge)\n- `jsonwebtoken = \"9\"` added to Cargo.toml\n- `auth_pipeline` module exported from lib.rs\n\n## Tests\n- 24 unit tests in `tests/auth_pipeline_test.rs` — every ADR-018 §4 row,\n  round-trip, extension install, revocation cache, clock-skew boundary, ops matrix\n- 4 integration tests in .ddx/beads.jsonl:1:{"acceptance":"1. A reusable delimiter helper exists for untrusted artifact bodies.
2. Prompt assembly uses the helper at every artifact-body interpolation site in scope.
3. `TestArtifactBodyDelimitedAsUntrusted` proves the delimiter contract and payload preservation.
4. `cd cli && go test ./internal/server/... ./internal/agent/...` passes.
5. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.","created_at":"2026-05-05T11:37:14.708641486Z","description":"PROBLEM
Prompt-injection boundary markers are not yet explicit around artifact bodies that later flow into prompt assembly. The current server-side artifact readers in `cli/internal/server/state_executions.go:205-217` clamp text for transport, but they do not wrap the body in an explicit untrusted-data envelope for downstream prompt renderers. The review prompt path in `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1561-1630` only uses XML tags for the bead envelope; it does not mark artifact content with a dedicated boundary contract.

ROOT CAUSE
There is no shared delimiter helper for untrusted artifact bodies. The current prompt builders rely on ad hoc XML wrappers and transport clamping, which are not the same thing as a prompt-injection boundary that clearly distinguishes trusted instructions from untrusted artifact text.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add a small helper (likely in `cli/internal/evidence` or the prompt-rendering package that owns the assembler) that wraps artifact bodies in a canonical untrusted-data delimiter pair.
- Update the prompt-rendering call sites that inline artifact bodies to use the wrapper before interpolation.
- Add a regression test named `TestArtifactBodyDelimitedAsUntrusted` that asserts the delimiter pair appears around the artifact payload and that the raw body is still preserved inside the wrapper.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not alter the transport-level clamp behavior or its `truncated` metadata.
- Do not change review verdict parsing or the FEAT-022 telemetry schema in this slice.
- Do not rewrite unrelated server text egress paths.","events":[{"actor":"ddx","body":"{"resolved_provider":"codex","resolved_model":"gpt-5.4-mini","fallback_chain":[],"actual_power":8}","created_at":"2026-05-05T14:44:37.174355984Z","kind":"routing","source":"ddx agent execute-bead","summary":"provider=codex model=gpt-5.4-mini"},{"actor":"ddx","body":"{"attempt_id":"20260505T143454-88ed54dc","harness":"codex","model":"gpt-5.4-mini","input_tokens":4861400,"output_tokens":17293,"total_tokens":4878693,"cost_usd":0,"duration_ms":580211,"exit_code":0}","created_at":"2026-05-05T14:44:37.425832645Z","kind":"cost","source":"ddx agent execute-bead","summary":"tokens=4878693 model=gpt-5.4-mini"},{"actor":"ddx","body":"{"escalation_count":0,"fallback_chain":[],"final_tier":"","requested_profile":"","requested_tier":"","resolved_model":"gpt-5.4-mini","resolved_provider":"codex","resolved_tier":""}","created_at":"2026-05-05T14:44:44.068530101Z","kind":"routing","source":"ddx agent work","summary":"provider=codex model=gpt-5.4-mini"},{"actor":"erik","body":"failure_class=review-error: transport

4. TestModeMatrix_OperatorExpectationDocumented asserts the suite's package godoc clearly states the cross-mode equivalence invariant.
5. Every spec listed under SPEC UPDATES has a citation to ddx-06cbaa90 or ddx-937de9fc (verified by `rg "ddx-06cbaa90|ddx-937de9fc" docs/helix/`).
6. CLAUDE.md "Persona System" section updated to document install precedence and references ddx-937de9fc.

9. lefthook run pre-commit passes.","created_at":"2026-05-15T01:18:04.1850481Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ddx-43b6b5fe","depends_on_id":"ddx-06cbaa90","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-15T01:18:10Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-43b6b5fe","depends_on_id":"ddx-937de9fc","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-15T01:18:10Z"}],"description":"PROBLEM
The two principles of epic ddx-602ae31a (ddx home resolution + skills/personas install topology) must behave identically across both modes (in-tree and convention). Today there is no single test suite that drives the same scenarios through both modes; coverage is per-mode-ad-hoc and divergence between modes is invisible until operators hit it in production.

ROOT CAUSE
The two parent beads (ddx-06cbaa90 and ddx-937de9fc) each carry per-feature tests. Neither owns the cross-mode equivalence guarantee.

PROPOSED FIX

1. Add cli/internal/agent/test_mode_matrix_test.go (or equivalent suite under a new package) that parameterizes every behaviour-equivalence scenario across both modes:

  for mode in [Convention, InTree]:
    setup_fixture(mode)
    for scenario in [
      "bead-lifecycle":           create/show/update/close
      "bead-execution":           ddx try a bead, observe attempt evidence and state mutation
      "plugin-skill-resolution":  ddx-side plugin lookup honors precedence; agent-tier symlinks resolve to plugin content
      "persona-binding":          role → persona resolves correctly per binding config
      "worker-drain":             ddx work --watch picks beads and lands them
      "meta-prompt-sync":         CLAUDE.md (or equivalent) gets the right content
      "auto-commit-routing":      bead create/update auto-commit lands in the right git repo (project's vs XDG's)
      "attachments":              per-attempt event files written to the right anchor
      "index-lock-recovery":      gitlock recovery fires correctly in both modes
    ]:
      run scenario in current mode
      assert behaviour identical to the other mode modulo storage path

2. Fixture setup must be deterministic and isolated:
   - Convention mode fixture: tempdir as project root, no .ddx/; XDG_DATA_HOME pointed at a per-test tempdir; git remote set to a deterministic URL.
   - In-tree mode fixture: tempdir as project root WITH .ddx/ pre-created; XDG_DATA_HOME pointed elsewhere (must not be consulted).
   - Two clones of the same project share an XDG dir — explicitly tested as a separate sub-scenario.

3. Document the test-mode invariant in cli/internal/agent/test_mode_matrix_test.go's package doc: "Any behavior that diverges between Convention and InTree modes (other than the on-disk anchor) is a regression."

4. SPEC UPDATES — propagate the resolver/mode model to every doc that references .ddx/ paths or project state. Each spec update added in this bead's commit cites principle-numbered tags so future readers see the link:
   - TD-031 bead state machine — "Storage anchors via ddxroot.Path(); see ddx-06cbaa90."
   - SD-019 multi-project-server-topology — Project identity from `host/owner/repo`; reference ddx-06cbaa90.
   - SD-020 multi-machine-coordinator-topology — same.
   - ADR-021 operator-prompt-beads-web-write-path — reference ddx-06cbaa90.
   - workers-sessions-operator-surface.md — worker status paths through ddxroot.Path().
   - plan-2026-04-29-artifact-and-run-architecture — FEAT-010 paths through resolver.
   - library/skills/ddx/SKILL.md and CLAUDE.md — install precedence per ddx-937de9fc.
   - library/README.md — install topology overview.

NON-SCOPE
- New behaviours (every scenario in the matrix is already covered by ddx-06cbaa90 or ddx-937de9fc per-feature tests; this is the cross-mode equivalence layer).
- Migration tooling for existing in-tree repos (out of scope; this repo stays in-tree).

DEPS
ddx-06cbaa90 (ddx home resolution).

The package-level conformance coverage still needs a single matrix that proves both backends satisfy the same behavioral contract. The current chaos harness already names jsonl and axon in cli/internal/bead/chaos_test.go:23-36, but the rest of the package still relies on ad hoc store construction and has no package-wide backend matrix to prove the new Axon path behaves identically.

ROOT CAUSE
cli/internal/bead/chaos_test.go:23-36 only defines the backend table; there is no end-to-end package test matrix yet that runs the conformance suite through both backends and keeps the existing package tests honest. The rest of the test package still defaults to newTestStore(t) in many places, so the Axon path is not yet a first-class test target.

PROPOSED FIX
- Extend the backend test helpers so the package conformance suite can run under both jsonl and axon.
- Keep TestChaos_ConcurrentAppendSafety, TestChaos_AtomicStatusTransitions, TestChaos_ConcurrentCloseAndAppend, and TestChaos_ConcurrentCloseNotLost as explicit backend matrix coverage.
- Make the package test harness clearly fail if either backend diverges.

NON-SCOPE
- GraphQL client generation.
- Backend selection/config plumbing.
- Subscription integration smoke tests.","events":[{"actor":"ddx","body":"{"resolved_provider":"codex","resolved_model":"gpt-5.4-mini","fallback_chain":[],"actual_power":8}","created_at":"0001-01-01T00:00:00Z","kind":"routing","source":"ddx agent execute-bead","summary":"provider=codex model=gpt-5.4-mini"},{"actor":"ddx","body":"{"attempt_id":"20260506T120844-d2a3f066","harness":"codex","model":"gpt-5.4-mini","input_tokens":1670655,"output_tokens":10807,"total_tokens":1681462,"cost_usd":0,"duration_ms":168678,"exit_code":0}","created_at":"0001-01-01T00:00:00Z","kind":"cost","source":"ddx agent execute-bead","summary":"tokens=1681462 model=gpt-5.4-mini"},{"actor":"ddx","body":"{"escalation_count":0,"fallback_chain":[],"final_tier":"","requested_profile":"","requested_tier":"","resolved_model":"gpt-5.4-mini","resolved_provider":"codex","resolved_tier":""}","created_at":"0001-01-01T00:00:00Z","kind":"routing","source":"ddx agent work","summary":"provider=codex model=gpt-5.4-mini"},{"actor":"erik","body":"failure_class=review-error: transport

.ddx/beads.jsonl:106:{"created_at":"2026-06-27T22:50:16.111482848Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ddx-a0a13392","depends_on_id":"ddx-071e9bd6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-06-27T22:50:47Z"}],"description":"PROBLEM
Stop currently performs ad hoc cleanup inside WorkerManager.Stop, releasing the claimed bead and then killing only the recorded pid when pid > 0. That path does not prove that a managed worker tree with fake claude/codex descendants is fully terminated, and the cleanup logic is not centralized for reuse by other lifecycle paths.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/server/workers.go:1214-1312 contains Stop, including the current pid-gated terminateProcessGroup call.
cli/internal/server/workers.go:1234-1286 computes pid and only escalates when pid > 0, so cleanup is limited to the recorded leader process.
cli/internal/server/workers.go:1298-1312 finalizes the worker after the ad hoc kill path, which makes idempotence depend on repeated copies of the same logic rather than a shared helper.

PROPOSED FIX
- Introduce a shared managed-worker tree cleanup helper that sends SIGTERM to the managed process group, waits WatchdogKillGrace, then SIGKILLs any remaining descendants.
- Make Stop call that helper for server-managed workers before terminalizing the record.
- Preserve the bead.stopped event, claim release, and terminal status writes so repeated Stop calls remain idempotent.
- Add fake claude and codex binaries on PATH that spawn sleeping children so the test can prove the full descendant tree is gone after Stop.
- Add a second Stop call assertion so the test proves no duplicate bead transition and no unrelated process kill.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change watchdog reaping in this bead.
- Do not implement server shutdown cleanup in this bead.
- Do not change external reported workers or interactive sessions.

ACCEPTANCE
1. TestManagedWorkerStopKillsClaudeCodexDescendants uses fake claude and codex binaries on PATH that spawn sleeping children, then proves Stop leaves none of those descendants running.
2. TestManagedWorkerStopIsIdempotent calls Stop twice and proves no duplicate bead transition and no unrelated process kill.
3. cd cli && go test ./internal/server/... -run "TestManagedWorkerStopKillsClaudeCodexDescendants|TestManagedWorkerStopIsIdempotent" passes.
4. make install is run after the code change lands.
5. lefthook run pre-commit passes.","events":[{"actor":"ddx","body":"{"resolved_provider":"codex","resolved_model":"gpt-5.4-mini","route_reason":"explicit_pin_discovery_unavailable","fallback_chain":[]}","created_at":"2026-06-30T07:53:22.95957228Z","kind":"routing","source":"legacy agent execute-bead","summary":"provider=codex model=gpt-5.4-mini reason=explicit_pin_discovery_unavailable"},{"actor":"ddx","body":"{"attempt_id":"20260630T074643-d4f75f6d","harness":"codex","model":"gpt-5.4-mini","route_reason":"explicit_pin_discovery_unavailable","input_tokens":1356533,"output_tokens":18838,"total_tokens":1375371,"cost_usd":0,"duration_ms":388894,"exit_code":0}","created_at":"2026-06-30T07:53:23.074524077Z","kind":"cost","source":"legacy agent execute-bead","summary":"tokens=1375371 model=gpt-5.4-mini"},{"actor":"ddx","body":"{"actual_harness":"codex","actual_model":"gpt-5.4-mini","actual_power":0,"actual_provider":"","attempt_id":"20260630T074643-d4f75f6d","bead_id":"ddx-a0a13392","estimated_difficulty":"","rejected_route_pins":null,"requested_power_class":"standard","requested_profile":"default","routing_intent_degraded":false,"routing_intent_note":"","routing_intent_source":"default"}","created_at":"2026-06-30T07:53:25.710933861Z","kind":"execution-routing-intent","source":"ddx work","summary":"source=default powerClass=standard model=gpt-5.4-mini harness=codex"},{"actor":"erik","body":"{"base_rev":"0bd46c5406d6e395e411dc1b350961b3424b86f0","classification":"recoverable","detail":"mixed_commit_and_no_changes_rationale","rationale":"The attempt produced an implementation revision but DDx marked it failed because the result report was internally inconsistent: it mixed committed changes with a no_changes_rationale that only names a verification command. rationale: verification_command: cd cli && go test ./internal/server/... -run 'TestManagedWorkerStopKillsClaudeCodexDescendants|TestManagedWorkerStopIsIdempotent' && cd .. && make install && lefthook run pre-commit

The bead's managed-worker stop behavior is already present in HEAD:
- `cli/internal/server/workers.go` calls `cleanupManagedWorkerProcessTree` from `Stop()` before terminalizing the worker record.
- `cli/internal/server/workers_reap_unix.go` contains the shared SIGTERM -> grace -> SIGKILL process-tree cleanup helper.
- `cli/internal/server/workers_stop_test.go` already includes `TestManagedWorkerStopKillsClaudeCodexDescendants` and `TestManagedWorkerStopIsIdempotent`, which cover descendant cleanup and idempotence.

No additional code change was required in this execution worktree.

.ddx/beads.jsonl:123:{"acceptance":"1. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... -run 'NiflheimEvidence|LandCoordination|RetryEscalation|LandCoordinationDecisionAudit' -count=1` passes.
2. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... -count=1` passes.
3. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.","closing_commit_sha":"6484faf94fc9839d963baaf6ee7a1d20fb83f321","created_at":"2026-06-08T18:36:52.319292895Z","description":"PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-bced4e39 is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 3 of 3 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
PROBLEM
Niflheim land/ref/index/evidence-staging failures were recorded as overall `execution_failed` even when the candidate had succeeded or landed. The worker retried implementation work instead of reconciling land/evidence state or sending the bead to operator attention, wasting route attempts and confusing system failure with model-quality failure.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_orchestrator.go:305-309` wraps `LandingAdvancer` errors as generic `land advancer` errors.
- `cli/internal/agent/failclass/failclass.go:103-142` contains the normative table that only `OutcomeGenuineFailure` can escalate, but execute-bead/work land outcomes are not consistently reduced to this typed table before retry/escalation decisions.

PROPOSED FIX
Add self-contained Niflheim-style land coordination fixtures or table-driven cases under `cli/internal/agent/...` for `cannot lock ref refs/heads/main` after a candidate or merge is already landed, and for `landing worktree has staged changes after waiting 2s` caused by generated DDx evidence. Classify these as land/ref/index/evidence coordination outcomes, not implementation/model failures. If the candidate commit or merge is already an ancestor of the target, reconcile and close/continue without rerunning implementation. If the landing worktree is dirty with generated evidence or DDx metadata, retry/preserve the land/evidence step or move to operator attention according to existing reliability policy.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change Fizeau route selection or hard-code concrete provider/model names.
- Do not edit Niflheim beads or Niflheim implementation code.
- Do not duplicate the low-level index-contention fix tracked by `ddx-2965925e`; reuse or align with it.
- Do not duplicate the post-commit review/land timeout work tracked by `ddx-b2846610`; this is typed classification and reconciliation behavior, not another timeout implementation.

The fixtures must be self-contained and must not reference Niflheim paths as required inputs.
2. `TestExecuteBeadWorker_NiflheimEvidence_EmptyReviewResultCannotClose` proves a non-skipped success path with `cycle_trace.review_result={}` or an equivalent empty `ReviewResult` cannot close a bead and records `review_error` or operator-attention with a non-empty reason.
3. `TestExecuteBeadWorker_NiflheimEvidence_NilReviewerRequiresExplicitSkip` updates the current nil-reviewer behavior so `Reviewer: nil` only closes when an explicit durable no-review/skip reason is present; otherwise it fails review gating.
4. `TestExecuteBeadLoop_NiflheimEvidence_LandCoordinationFailureDoesNotRerunImplementation` simulates both observed details (`cannot lock ref refs/heads/main` after a candidate/merge is already landed, and `landing worktree has staged changes after waiting 2s`) and asserts the action is reconcile/retry-land/operator-attention, not a fresh implementation run and not model escalation.
5. `TestExecuteBeadLoop_NiflheimEvidence_DecomposedParentIsExecutionIneligible` simulates `mixed_commit_and_no_changes_rationale` with `orchestrator_action: decompose`, child bead IDs, and `outcome_reason=decomposed`; it asserts the parent is marked execution-ineligible/proposed or dependency-waiting according to policy and that no further implementation attempt or min-power escalation is scheduled.
6. `TestWorkRetryEscalation_NiflheimEvidence_OnlySemanticFailuresRaiseMinPower` proves only semantic acceptance/review failures can increase requested min power/profile. Land coordination, repeated pre-claim system warnings, decomposed-parent outcomes, and dirty generated DDx evidence must not raise min power.
7. `TestExecuteBeadResult_NiflheimEvidence_CycleTraceCarriesDecisionAudit` proves result JSON/event bodies include `failure_class`, `retry_action`, `escalation_count`, requested/actual routing facts, review status/verdict or explicit skip reason, and land/reconcile status for the Niflheim scenario families.
8. Existing related behavior from `ddx-2965925e`, `ddx-b2846610`, and `ddx-d67f124e` remains covered; this bead either reuses their helpers or waits for those dependencies, and does not add duplicate low-level implementations.

11. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.","archived_at":"2026-07-03T07:18:01Z","created_at":"2026-06-06T15:55:41.332809555Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ddx-334c0958","depends_on_id":"ddx-2965925e","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-06-06T15:55:53Z"}],"description":"PROBLEM
A live Niflheim worker audit on 2026-06-06 showed that `ddx work` is not yet a stable release-grade autonomous worker. Strong-model escalation exists, but it is not reliably tied to the class of failure. The worker spent repeated attempts on structural or system failures that stronger models cannot fix, while automatic review evidence was empty on attempts that were recorded as successful. This violates the DDx engineering principle "Cheapest Viable, Escalate on Evidence" (docs/dev/engineering-principles.md:39-45): review, runtime, or retry evidence should determine escalation, and only lower-power inability to meet acceptance criteria should push to stronger routes. It also threatens FEAT-010 task-execution reliability because beads can be retried, escalated, or closed without a typed, auditable reason.

This bead is a Niflheim incident/regression closure bead. It is not a duplicate of the narrower queue items listed under INTERSECTIONS; instead it captures the external evidence DDx workers will not otherwise have, and requires a DDx-side regression suite proving those observed scenarios classify correctly before rerun, escalation, operator attention, or close.

BACKGROUND AND OBSERVED NIFLHEIM EVIDENCE
The evidence was gathered from Niflheim worker logs and result bundles, but those files are not load-bearing for this bead. The relevant facts are copied here because an isolated DDx worker cannot access the Niflheim repository or `/tmp` logs.

1. Decomposed parent kept consuming attempts and escalated to a stronger model anyway.
- Niflheim bead: `niflheim-64217cc5`, title "Instrument cold replicator with structured per-operation S3 tracing".
- Observed result bundle: `.ddx/executions/20260606T152006-a4ca7254/result.json` plus worker logs from 2026-06-05/2026-06-06.
- Routing showed escalation exists: repeated routes started at `gpt-5.4-mini` with requested min powers 5, 7, 8, and 9, then reached `gpt-5.5` at requested min power 10.
- Metrics on the bead showed `attempt_count=24`, `total_tokens=19371353`, and average duration about `244112ms`.
- Despite triage recommending `close_decomposed_or_mark_execution_ineligible`, retries continued until the latest event recorded:
  `mixed_commit_and_no_changes_rationale`
  `powerClass=standard`
  `rationale: status: open`
  `orchestrator_action: decompose`
  `reason: parent bead exceeds the Step 0 size threshold and is already decomposed into focused child beads for tracing, counters, tests, and runbook work.`
  `child_beads: niflheim-f03c61a2, niflheim-df407aa8, niflheim-7a421ede, niflheim-0bbed6c9`
  `outcome_reason=decomposed`
- Expected DDx behavior: once the worker has reliable evidence that the bead is a decomposed parent or execution-ineligible parent, no further implementation attempt or model escalation should occur. It should mark execution-ineligible/proposed/operator-attention according to policy and move on.

2. Land/evidence coordination failures were treated like implementation failure and retried expensively.
- Niflheim bead: `niflheim-c75aba30`, title "Materializer per-merge memory footprint OOMs even at concurrent_limit=8".
- Two expensive attempts both used `gpt-5.4-mini`; no useful stronger-model escalation was observed.
- Metrics showed `attempt_count=2`, `total_tokens=65513510`, and average duration about `3306461.5ms`.
- First attempt result: `.ddx/executions/20260606T113550-7b29ee42/result.json`.
  - `status=execution_failed`, `outcome=error`.
  - Implementation result commit was `7aaeb0712...`; a merge commit `2c6f4d6e6` was later observed as an ancestor of HEAD, but the result still recorded a land coordination failure.
  - Error detail:
    `land coordination failed: land advancer: restoring refs/heads/main to ba2d3059f14e834dcb147f2a6db3b294d83b0fac after evidence commit failed: git update-ref refs/heads/main: fatal: update_ref failed for ref 'refs/heads/main': cannot lock ref 'refs/heads/main': is at 5a176b332b182f2a959614f63769d835c47ffd97 but expected 2c6f4d6e6961b8a93742cde66bceec056934655a`
  - `cycle_trace[0].final_decision="success"` and `cycle_trace[0].review_result={}`.
- Second attempt result: `.ddx/executions/20260606T142223-94f8f0f8/result.json`.
  - `status=execution_failed`, `outcome=error`.
  - Error detail:
    `land coordination failed: land advancer: landing worktree has staged changes after waiting 2s: A .ddx/executions/20260606T150832-af40450b/result.json`
  - `cycle_trace[0].final_decision="success"` and `cycle_trace[0].review_result={}`.
- At observation time the worker had claimed it again (`20260606T152024-a21d8c54`) and edited six files in a fresh isolated worktree:
  `crates/niflheim-core/src/config/mod.rs`, `crates/niflheim-core/src/config/tests.rs`, `crates/niflheim-core/src/config/types.rs`, `crates/niflheim-delta-writer/src/materializer/config.rs`, `crates/niflheim-delta-writer/src/materializer/executor.rs`, and `crates/niflheim-delta-writer/src/materializer/tests.rs`.
- Expected DDx behavior: land/ref/index/evidence-staging failures are system or coordination failures, not model-quality failures. The retry policy should reconcile a successfully landed candidate when possible, retry only the land/evidence step when safe, or park/operator-attention the bead when the landing worktree is dirty. It should not blindly rerun implementation at the same or stronger route.

3. Pre-claim intake/system dirt caused repeated warning churn instead of a clear operator lane.
- Niflheim bead: `niflheim-a62d31ce`, title "Publish competitive benchmark artifacts and provenance metadata".
- A live worker repeatedly ran readiness/intake. The log showed readiness timeout after five minutes, then:
  `preserved pre-dispatch implementation changes in /home/erik/Projects/niflheim under refs/ddx/pre-dispatch/20260606T153517-f7a32618; released niflheim-a62d31ce and continuing watch. dirty paths: .ddx/harness-sessions/-2929681.json. recover with git stash apply refs/ddx/pre-dispatch/20260606T153517-f7a32618`
- Bead events included `pre_claim_intake.warn` with timeout/system-unready reason and generic suggested action.
- Expected DDx behavior: repeated same/systemic pre-claim warnings and dirty generated DDx artifacts should become an operator-visible/system-unready state after the configured threshold, not an indefinite poll/release loop.

4. Automatic reviews were not forcing meaningful quality improvement.
- Recent Niflheim "success" attempts had empty review results:
  - `niflheim-9dea95da`, result `.ddx/executions/20260606T043655-845c77ee/result.json`: closed success, `cycle_trace.review_result={}`, `final_decision="success"`, model `gpt-5.4-mini`, result commit `78e51a168...`.
  - `niflheim-da4e1311`, result `.ddx/executions/20260606T085358-d80ada3e/result.json`: closed success, `cycle_trace.review_result={}`, `final_decision="success"`, model `gpt-5.4-mini`, result commit `d43be3a3...`.
  - `niflheim-c75aba30` attempts listed above: `cycle_trace.final_decision="success"` with `review_result={}` despite overall land failures.
- Manual Claude review earlier improved bead wording and completeness, but the automatic worker path did not show enforceable per-AC review evidence.
- Expected DDx behavior: unless review is explicitly skipped with a durable reason (`runtime.NoReview` or a valid `review:skip` + reason policy), an empty review result must be a review error or operator-attention signal. It must not be treated as approval, success, or evidence of quality improvement.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `cli/internal/agent/failclass/failclass.go:103-142` contains the normative routing table: only `OutcomeGenuineFailure` can escalate; dirty worktree, transport, quota, auth, setup, timeout, and merge-conflict do not escalate. The live Niflheim behavior shows execute-bead/work outcomes are not consistently reduced to this typed table before retry/escalation decisions.
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_orchestrator.go:305-309` wraps `LandingAdvancer` errors as generic `land advancer` errors. The caller can record an overall `execution_failed` without distinguishing landed-candidate reconciliation, ref/index contention, staged evidence dirt, or dirty landing worktree from implementation failure.
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1967-2072` emits pre-claim timeout/system-unready outcomes as warn-only by default and continues implementation when strict intake blocking is off. This permits repeated same-fingerprint/system warnings to look like a live worker instead of a system-unready/operator lane.
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:2175-2315` handles `PreClaimIntakeTooLargeDecomposed`, but missing/empty decomposition paths can warn and continue. `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:3826-3831` can recognize execution-ineligible/decomposed state only after the bead has the right metadata. The Niflheim parent evidence shows structural/decomposed-parent outcomes can still reach repeated implementation attempts and power escalation.
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:3042-3138` treats success disposition/status as closeable once candidate-cycle review/landing has allowed success, and the old post-merge review is optional. `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review_test.go:799-823` explicitly proves a worker with `Reviewer: nil` can skip review and close the bead. `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go:168-193` defines `ReviewResult` with all fields optional except the zero-value enum field; no invariant rejects `{}` as a success-path review artifact.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add compact Niflheim-style regression fixtures under `cli/internal/agent/testdata/niflheim_worker_evidence/` or equivalent. The fixtures should encode the four scenario families above: empty review success, land coordination/ref-lock/staged-evidence failure, decomposed-parent no-changes rationale with child beads, and repeated pre-claim system-unready warning. Do not depend on Niflheim checkout paths.
- Introduce or tighten a single execute-bead retry classification boundary that maps attempt result, land result, review result, no-changes rationale, and pre-claim intake outcome into exactly one action: close, retry same tier, escalate min power/profile, reconcile land/evidence, execution-ineligible/proposed, or operator-attention. Wire this boundary into `ddx work`/`ddx try` so `failclass.Classify` is not merely a standalone helper.
- Enforce review evidence on success. For non-skipped success paths, `cycle_trace.review_result` or the equivalent result field must contain a non-empty verdict, per-AC evidence or documented reason, reviewer routing/session metadata when available, and a classification. `{}` is invalid and must prevent close or produce a review-error/operator-attention event. Explicit review skips remain allowed only when they are durable, reasoned, and visible in result JSON.
- Classify land/ref/index/evidence-staging failures separately from implementation/model failures. If the candidate commit or merge is already landed or is an ancestor of the target, reconcile and close/continue without rerunning implementation. If the landing worktree has staged generated evidence or DDx metadata, retry/preserve the land/evidence step or move to operator attention according to the existing local reliability policy; do not escalate the model for this class.
- Classify decomposed-parent/no-changes rationale as execution-ineligible or operator work before another implementation attempt. Stronger models cannot fix parent beads that are already split into executable children.
- Make worker result JSON and event bodies carry enough audit fields to explain the decision: `failure_class`, `retry_action`, `escalation_count`, requested/actual power/profile/model, review status/verdict, and land/reconcile status.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change Fizeau route selection or hard-code concrete provider/model names. DDx should continue to pass power/profile intent and let Fizeau choose concrete routes.
- Do not edit Niflheim beads or Niflheim implementation code.
- Do not duplicate the low-level index-contention fix tracked by `ddx-2965925e`; reuse or align with it.
- Do not duplicate the post-commit review/land timeout work tracked by `ddx-b2846610`; this bead requires typed classification and review evidence, not another timeout implementation.
- Do not duplicate the repeated pre-claim warn operator-attention bead `ddx-d67f124e`; this bead uses the Niflheim observation as cross-scenario regression coverage.
- Do not implement a full operator-lane product workflow beyond the state transitions/events needed to keep the worker from blind retry or false close.

INTERSECTIONS
- Parent: `ddx-3106674f` (multi-worker hardening and local reliability gates). This bead is the external Niflheim regression/incident closure layer under that reliability epic.
- Dependency: `ddx-2965925e` covers tracker commit index contention. This bead must not reimplement that fix; it must prove land/ref/index/evidence failures classify separately from model failure once the low-level path is hardened.
- Dependency: `ddx-b2846610` covers bounded post-commit review/land. This bead must still require typed review/land outcomes after the timeout wedge is fixed.
- Dependency: `ddx-d67f124e` covers same-fingerprint pre-claim warning escalation. This bead adds the Niflheim `niflheim-a62d31ce` family as regression evidence.
- Related but not a dependency: `ddx-e1a576a7` is an execution-ineligible decomposed parent and a local precedent for the `niflheim-64217cc5` class.
- Related quality framework: `ddx-a9d130d0` and `ddx-ac10e7af` own broader bead lifecycle/operator-lane policy; this bead is concrete worker behavior and evidence gating.","events_attachment":"ddx-334c0958/events.jsonl","execution-eligible":false,"id":"ddx-334c0958","issue_type":"bug","labels":["adr:024","area:agent","area:execute-bead","area:review","area:workers","incident:niflheim-2026-06-06","kind:bug","kind:reliability","phase:iterate","prevention","severity:critical","spec:FEAT-010","spec:TP-021"],"lifecycle-migrated-at":"2026-07-02T22:52:48Z","lifecycle-migrated-from-status":"closed","lifecycle-migrated-to-status":"closed","notes":"decomposed into ddx-044cd257, ddx-3a6f1ad6, ddx-d4817c9b, ddx-01aa7b18, ddx-1865eadc","parent":"ddx-3106674f","priority":0,"queue-rank":1850,"schema_version":1,"spec-id":"FEAT-010","status":"closed","title":"execute-bead worker: gate success on review evidence and classify Niflheim retry failures","updated_at":"2026-06-17T20:08:03.826920996Z"}
.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:1083:{"acceptance":"1. `TestDefaultVerificationCommandRunnerTimeoutKillsProcessGroup` proves a timed-out nested shell command (`sh -lc 'sleep ...'` or equivalent fixture) leaves no child/grandchild process alive after the runner returns.
2. `TestDefaultVerificationCommandRunnerAllowsConfiguredLongGate` or equivalent proves the verification timeout is configurable/worker-derived and can run a command longer than 60s without forcing `no_changes_unverified`.
3. `TestExecuteBeadWorkerNoChangesVerifiedLongCommandCloses` proves a `verification_command` that runs longer than the old 60s limit but exits 0 closes the bead as `already_satisfied` and emits `no_changes_verified`.
4. `TestExecuteBeadWorkerNoChangesVerificationTimeoutKeepsOpenAndReaps` proves timeout keeps the bead open with `triage:no-changes-unverified`, records timeout evidence, and leaves no verification process tree behind.
5. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -run 'Test(DefaultVerificationCommandRunner|ExecuteBeadWorkerNoChanges.*Verification)' -count=1` passes.
6. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.","archived_at":"2026-07-03T07:18:01Z","closing_commit_sha":"f609f22ef84a0d7b05ba89dd3e9fd5b92fbfa446","created_at":"2026-05-15T13:09:07.942514913Z","description":"PROBLEM
A `no_changes_rationale.txt` with `verification_command:` can time out, leave its child process tree running, and let `ddx work --watch` move on to another bead while the old verification is still consuming the project. Live reproduction on 2026-05-15 in `/Users/erik/Projects/fizeau`: `fizeau-bc9586d7` returned `verification_command: sh -lc "go test ... && lefthook run pre-commit"`; the watcher logged `• fizeau-bc9586d7 → no_changes` and started `fizeau-7af3f548` at 13:07:21Z, while `ps` still showed orphaned PID 3510889 (`sh -lc go test ...`) and child PID 3512539 (`go test ./... -count=1`) under PPID 1.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
`cli/internal/agent/try/no_changes.go:117-128` hard-codes `DefaultVerificationCommandTimeout = 60 * time.Second` and runs the command with `exec.CommandContext(cctx, "/bin/sh", "-c", command)`. `exec.CommandContext` kills only the direct shell on timeout; because workers commonly emit nested shell commands such as `sh -lc "go test ..."`, the actual test process survives as an orphan. The 60s timeout is also shorter than normal bead acceptance gates like `go test ./...` plus `lefthook run pre-commit`, so legitimate already-satisfied checks are classified as unverified/no-progress instead of closing the bead.

PROPOSED FIX
Run no_changes verification commands with the same process-tree discipline used by agent executors: start the shell in its own process group on Unix, terminate the group on timeout/cancel, and wait/reap before returning. Replace the hard-coded 60s ceiling with a worker/config-derived timeout that is long enough for normal acceptance gates, or make it configurable with a conservative default. Preserve the contract: exit 0 closes as `already_satisfied`; non-zero/timeout keeps the bead open with clear `no_changes_unverified` evidence.

NON-SCOPE
Do not weaken no_changes verification semantics. Do not let verification run asynchronously. Do not close a bead without a passing command. Do not change readiness classification or route selection.","events_attachment":"ddx-8a0aea8e/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-8a0aea8e","issue_type":"bug","labels":["area:agent","area:work","kind:bug","operator-ux","downstream:fizeau","dirty-tree"],"priority":0,"queue-rank":600,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-af6f8fc7","status":"closed","title":"work: reap and correctly budget no_changes verification commands","updated_at":"2026-06-13T13:58:29.795710244Z"}

A Niflheim decomposed parent bead kept consuming attempts and escalated to stronger routes despite evidence that the bead was already split into executable children. Stronger models cannot implement a parent bead whose correct action is decompose/execution-ineligible/operator work.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:2175-2315` handles `PreClaimIntakeTooLargeDecomposed`, but missing or empty decomposition paths can warn and continue.
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:3826-3831` recognizes execution-ineligible/decomposed state only after the bead has the right metadata.
- `cli/internal/agent/failclass/failclass.go:103-142` says only genuine failure should escalate, but decomposed-parent/no-changes outcomes can still reach implementation retry and min-power escalation.

PROPOSED FIX
Add a self-contained Niflheim-style decomposed-parent fixture or table-driven case under `cli/internal/agent/...` with `mixed_commit_and_no_changes_rationale`, `orchestrator_action: decompose`, child bead IDs, and `outcome_reason=decomposed`. Classify the parent as execution-ineligible, proposed, dependency-waiting, or operator-attention according to policy before another implementation attempt is scheduled. Preserve child bead evidence in the event/result audit.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change Fizeau route selection or hard-code concrete provider/model names.
- Do not edit Niflheim beads or Niflheim implementation code.
- Do not duplicate the low-level index-contention fix tracked by `ddx-2965925e`.
- Do not duplicate the post-commit review/land timeout work tracked by `ddx-b2846610`.
- Do not duplicate the repeated pre-claim warn operator-attention bead `ddx-d67f124e`.
- Do not implement a full operator-lane product workflow beyond state transitions/events needed to keep the worker from blind retry or false close.

INTERSECTIONS
Parent: `ddx-3106674f`. Dependencies to preserve: `ddx-2965925e`, `ddx-b2846610`, `ddx-d67f124e`. Related precedent: `ddx-e1a576a7` for execution-ineligible decomposed parent behavior. Related quality framework: `ddx-a9d130d0`, `ddx-ac10e7af`. 5. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.","archived_at":"2026-07-03T07:18:01Z","closing_commit_sha":"a37b69f7e9ab1baf6e14e96006d53d56b8cf9bb5","created_at":"2026-06-08T06:13:49.850658966Z","description":"PROBLEM
A parent bead that has already been decomposed can still re-enter the worker as if it were an executable task. That keeps spending attempts on a structurally ineligible parent instead of moving on to its children.

ROOT CAUSE
`cli/internal/agent/recovery_decompose.go:130-210` already marks decomposed children with `Parent=beadID` and sets the parent execution-eligible flag to false, but the main worker loop still has paths that continue past decomposition-like intake and retry handling. `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:2175-2315` handles some intake decomposition cases, and `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:3818-3840` continues processing other outcome branches without consistently parking a parent whose own `no_changes` rationale says to decompose.

PROPOSED FIX
- When the worker sees `mixed_commit_and_no_changes_rationale` with `orchestrator_action: decompose`, child IDs, and `outcome_reason=decomposed`, mark the parent execution-ineligible or proposed before another implementation attempt.
- Do not schedule another implementation run or min-power escalation for that parent.
- Keep the parent visible as a decomposition boundary, not as a retriable implementation failure.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change child-bead content, land handling, or review gating in this child.
- Do not alter the decomposer output format beyond what is needed to detect the decomposed-parent state.","events_attachment":"ddx-d4817c9b/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-d4817c9b","issue_type":"bug","labels":["phase:iterate","area:agent","area:execute-bead","area:workers","area:review","kind:bug","kind:reliability","severity:critical","spec:FEAT-010","spec:TP-021","adr:024","incident:niflheim-2026-06-06","prevention"],"parent":"ddx-334c0958","priority":0,"queue-rank":2050,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-5e04821f","spec-id":"FEAT-010","status":"closed","title":"agent: park decomposed parents before retrying implementation","updated_at":"2026-06-13T13:58:35.835830846Z"}

rationale: Bead ddx-263ad347 — operatorPromptApprove/Cancel + per-project allowlist + auto-approve flag

All four acceptance criteria are already satisfied at the base revision (bb3b5883). No source change is required.

AC #1 — Approve/cancel mutations exist:
  - cli/internal/server/graphql/schema.graphql declares operatorPromptApprove and operatorPromptCancel.
  - Resolvers: cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_operator_prompt.go
      OperatorPromptApprove (line 399)
      OperatorPromptCancel  (line 461)

AC #2 — Per-project allowlist enforced:
  - Resolver.OperatorPromptAutoApproveAllowlist is the per-project allowlist.
  - canSubmit (line 528) gates ts-net submitters; canAutoApprove (line 548) gates approve.
  - Empty allowlist denies approval and ts-net submission (errSubmitDenied / errAutoApproveDenied).
  - Tests:
      TestOperatorPromptApprove_DeniedEmptyAllowlist
      TestOperatorPromptSubmit_TsnetNotInAllowlistRejected
      TestOperatorPromptSubmit_TsnetOnAllowlistAccepted

AC #3 — Auto-approve restricted to configured-localhost:
  - canAutoApprove returns false for any non-localhost identity (line 549), regardless of allowlist contents.
  - OperatorPromptAllowlistLocalhostSentinel ("localhost") is the opt-in sentinel.
  - Tests:
      TestOperatorPromptSubmit_AutoApproveAllowedForLocalhost  (localhost+sentinel → autoApproved=true, status=open)
      TestOperatorPromptSubmit_AutoApproveDeniedForTsnet       (ts-net never auto-approves even when on allowlist)

AC #4 — Tests cover happy + denied paths:
  - Happy: TestOperatorPromptApprove_Happy, TestOperatorPromptCancel_Happy, TestOperatorPromptSubmit_AutoApproveAllowedForLocalhost, TestOperatorPromptSubmit_TsnetOnAllowlistAccepted
  - Denied: TestOperatorPromptApprove_DeniedEmptyAllowlist, TestOperatorPromptSubmit_AutoApproveDeniedForTsnet, TestOperatorPromptSubmit_TsnetNotInAllowlistRejected, TestOperatorPromptApprove_CSRFRequired, TestOperatorPromptCancel_CSRFRequired, TestOperatorPromptApprove_RejectsNonProposed, TestOperatorPromptCancel_RejectsNonProposed

Manual approve = proposed → ready: OperatorPromptApprove sets b.Status = bead.StatusOpen (the "ready" state for work pickup; the codebase uses StatusOpen as the ready bucket — see execute_bead_loop.go and the wake tests).

Prior REVIEW:BLOCK notes on this bead complained that the diff contained only execution metadata. That observation is correct — but the source change those reviewers were looking for had already landed before bb3b5883. The bead's contract is satisfied by the existing code; another implementation pass would be duplicate or churn.

revision (d1342a86) by commit b7dd9f9e (in main):

  feat(web): shared FilterChip primitive + virtualization on Runs [ddx-1978065c]

Evidence in the worktree:

  1. AC1 — Runs page imports the shared chip primitive:
     `import FilterChip from '$lib/components/FilterChip.svelte'`
     at cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/runs/+page.svelte:10
     The same FilterChip is also used by the Artifacts page
     (cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/artifacts/+page.svelte),
     confirming it was extracted as a shared primitive.

  2. AC2 — Virtualization kicks in past threshold:
     `const VIRTUALIZE_THRESHOLD = 1000` and
     `let virtualized = $derived(edges.length >= VIRTUALIZE_THRESHOLD)` at
     runs/+page.svelte:17 / :155, applied per-row via `style={rowStyle}` at
     runs/+page.svelte:373 (content-visibility: auto + contain-intrinsic-size).
     The table exposes `data-virtualized="true|false"` for tests.

  3. AC3 — Live worker phase updates still work through the virtualized list:
     Virtualization uses CSS content-visibility (browser-native row windowing),
     not row unmount. Expanded rows render <RunRowDetail …/> at line 454; that
     component owns the worker subscription/polling and remains mounted in the
     DOM regardless of virtualization. `content-visibility: auto` only skips
     paint/layout for off-screen rows, so reactive updates to row state and to
     RunRowDetail subscriptions continue to flow.

The previous attempt (ddx-1978065c, run 20260503T064620) was BLOCKED by review
because its merged diff carried only execution manifest/result JSON — but the
actual code from that attempt did land via b7dd9f9e and is present in main.
There is no remaining code change to make for this bead.

Schema drift prevention mechanism:
The schema gate prevents reintroduction of the original wedge (producer emitting bool verdict while consumer parses string) by:

5. Prompt lock: TestBuildPreClaimIntakePrompt_UsesDocumentedReadinessSchema catches prompt drift
6. CI enforcement: make readiness-schema runs before merge

Any change to schema, prompt, or decoder that breaks alignment fails the CI gate.

tool, chaos-test integration, fallback documentation, performance benchmarking). Per the
execute-bead instructions, the correct action when a bead is too big is to decompose, not
attempt the work. A prior whole-epic attempt was BLOCKED in post-merge review (events log)
for producing only execution evidence and not addressing any AC.

The decomposition has already been performed in a prior attempt and the seven children
exist in the tracker with correct parent edges and dependency DAG (verified in this
attempt via `ddx bead show` and `ddx bead show <child>` for each):

each passes post-merge review. Triage / orchestrator should now drain the children rather

rationale: Bead ddx-27936512 is a duplicate of work already merged in commit 0b1b3964
("feat(server): run-detail resolver + tool-call persistence + bundle-file
confinement [ddx-6d132f62]"). All four acceptance criteria are already
satisfied by existing code:

AC1: Run resolver returns full detail (lossless)
  - cli/internal/server/state_runs_detail.go:250 loadRunDetail() populates
    prompt/response/stderr from session bodies and bundleFiles via
    listRunBundleFiles(); invoked from state_runs.go:520 on run(id:) lookup.
  - cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_runs.go wires Run, RunToolCalls,
    RunBundleFile resolvers to the state methods.

AC2: ToolCallEntry persisted at drain time (NOT log-parsing)
  - agent.SessionIndexEntry carries a ToolCalls []ToolCallEntry field;
    SessionIndexEntryFromResult copies result.ToolCalls into the entry,
    so the persistence happens once at drain time rather than on each
    resolver call. GetRunToolCallsGraphQL reads only the persisted slice.

AC3: bundleFile endpoint refuses path traversal
  - resolveRunBundlePath (state_runs_detail.go:111) rejects absolute paths,
    "..", paths whose Clean escapes the root, and symlink targets that
    EvalSymlinks resolves outside bundleRoot. Surfaced via
    GetRunBundleFileGraphQL → Query.runBundleFile.

AC4: Tests cover all three
  - state_runs_detail_test.go covers: dotdot/absolute/under-workdir
    rejection, symlink-escape rejection, size cap (>64KB → truncated=true),
    whitelist enforcement (only allowed extensions inline), missing-path
    404, bundleFiles listing with size+mime, drain-time tool-call
    persistence (TestRunToolCalls_PersistedAtDrain and
    TestSessionIndexEntryFromResult_PersistsToolCalls), and the
    resolveRunBundlePath helper directly. All pass at base-rev b10e224f.

S15-2 acceptance is already satisfied by source already merged into the base
revision 727cf03a. The earlier REVIEW:BLOCK note ("Diff contains only an
execution result.json artifact") referred to a prior attempt; in this base
the actual source landed via commit 81e6d338 ("feat(bead): operator-prompt
issueType, proposed/cancelled status, no-self-mutation guard
[ddx-a8939a2b]") and is present in HEAD's tree.

Evidence per AC:

1. issueType=operator-prompt accepted by validator
   - cli/internal/bead/schema/bead-record.schema.json: issue_type is open
     string (minLength 1); operator-prompt accepted.
   - cli/internal/bead/types.go: const IssueTypeOperatorPrompt =
     "operator-prompt".
   - Test: TestSchemaAcceptsOperatorPromptIssueType.

2. 'proposed' status recognized in state machine with valid transitions
   (proposed→ready/open, proposed→cancelled)
   - schema enum: ["open","in_progress","closed","blocked","proposed",
     "cancelled"].
   - types.go: StatusProposed/StatusCancelled constants; CanonicalStatuses
     includes both; IsValidStatusTransition allows proposed→open and
     proposed→cancelled and rejects every other proposed transition.
   - Test: TestStatusTransitionsForProposed (covers allow/deny matrix
     including proposed self-loop and terminal closed/cancelled).

3. Template applied on create populates labels/tier/auto-AC
   - types.go: NewOperatorPromptBead applies labels
     [kind:operator-prompt, source:web-ui], clamps tier to
     MinPriority..MaxPriority, sets Acceptance =
     OperatorPromptDefaultAcceptance, preserves prompt body verbatim in
     Description and uses first non-empty line as Title (with
     "(empty operator prompt)" fallback).
   - Tests: TestStoreAcceptsOperatorPromptBead, TestNewOperatorPromptBeadDefaults.

4. Structural AC check skipped for issueType=operator-prompt
   - OperatorPromptDefaultAcceptance stub stands as the contract; the
     template populates Acceptance unconditionally so structural checks
     that key on a non-empty acceptance pass without enforcing pre-authored
     AC. Default value asserted in TestStoreAcceptsOperatorPromptBead.

5. Runtime guard prevents operator-prompt beads from mutating
   operator-prompt beads
   - types.go: OperatorPromptMutationGuard implements the 2x2 matrix
     (only operator-prompt actor + operator-prompt target denied).
   - cli/internal/bead/store.go (~line 462): Store.Create calls the guard
     with os.Getenv("DDX_ACTOR_ISSUE_TYPE") and rejects self-mutation.
   - Tests: TestOperatorPromptMutationGuardMatrix (allow/deny matrix),
     TestStoreCreateRejectsOperatorPromptSelfMutation (live store path
     keyed off DDX_ACTOR_ISSUE_TYPE).

All listed tests pass under `go test ./internal/bead/` against this base
(see verification_command above).

what is done:
  Verified the current state of the dependency chain and the codebase
  surface this bead would build against.

what is blocking:
  This bead's <description> declares: "Depends on the axon backend
  implementation child." The parent epic ddx-5d49b14e was decomposed
  on 2026-05-02 23:10 into seven children. The migration tool sits
  downstream of:

    - ddx-6bb51a52  TD: design axon-backend integration         (status: ?)
    - ddx-bbdd7564  Introduce selectable bead Backend interface (status: ?)
    - ddx-95ec5ed5  Implement axon Backend for bead tracker     (status: open)

  ddx-95ec5ed5 is still open. The repo confirms this: there is no
  axon package under cli/internal/bead/ (no `axon/` directory, no
  axon_backend.go), no generated gRPC client (no `cli/internal/bead/
  axon/pb/`), no `axon.proto` checked in, and no `BackendAxon` constant
  in cli/internal/bead/backend.go (the file still lists only
  BackendJSONL, BackendBD, BackendBR and a 4-method Backend interface
  WriteAll/ReadAll/Init/WithLock — i.e. the pre-TD-030 surface).

  Without the axon Backend implementation, there is no target API to
  migrate into. Per TD-030 §"Migration plan" the migration command
  must call `CreateEntity`, `CreateLink`, and `CommitTransaction`
  against the axon backend; none of those entry points exist in the
  Go tree yet. Building the migration tool now would require either:
    (a) inventing the axon backend in this same pass — out of scope,
        and ddx-95ec5ed5 is sized as its own bead, or
    (b) writing the migrator against a stub interface that will be
        churned once ddx-95ec5ed5 lands — wasted code that cannot be
        verified end-to-end, and AC #5 (integration test running
        migration on a fixture repo and asserting equivalence) is
        not satisfiable without a real backend to round-trip against.

  The acceptance criteria explicitly require lossless migration plus
  a verification pass; both are unverifiable until the backend exists.

what a follow-up attempt would need:
  ddx-95ec5ed5 (and its upstreams ddx-bbdd7564 and ddx-6bb51a52) must
  close first. Once the axon Backend is registered in the factory
  (cli/internal/bead/registry.go) and exposes the per-entity surface
  described in TD-030 §"Backend interface (Go)" — Create / Get /
  Update / AppendEvent / AddDep / Close / Export / Import — this bead
  becomes straightforward: extend cli/cmd/bead_migrate.go with a
  `--to=axon` flag, iterate beads.jsonl + beads-archive.jsonl + any
  per-bead .ddx/attachments/<id>/events.jsonl sidecars, drive the
  axon Backend per the TD-030 Migration plan, and add a verification
  pass (count parity + sampled hash comparison). Idempotency falls
  out of OCC presence-checks as TD-030 already specifies.

  The bead's dependency graph should be enforced in the queue: this
  bead should not become eligible until ddx-95ec5ed5 is closed. If
  the dependency was not declared via `ddx bead dep add` on this
  bead's id (only the textual description names it), that link
  should be added so `ddx bead ready` does not surface this work
  prematurely.
rationale: status: blocked_by_dependency

rationale: status: open
reason: AC#5 blocked by pre-existing unrelated test breakage in cli/internal/agent/bead_lifecycle_skill_contract_test.go — the checked-in skill mirrors under .agents/skills/ddx/bead-lifecycle/ and .claude/skills/ddx/bead-lifecycle/ were intentionally removed (commits 42c333d61 / d530f68cd / c536d2ff1, "chore: remove checked-in DDx skill mirrors [ddx-11ef34e1]") but TestBeadLifecycleSkillReadinessDocumentsRewriteContract / TestBeadLifecycleSkillLintDocumentsRationaleShape / TestBeadLifecycleSkillFilesStayInSyncOnContractTerms were not updated and still try to os.Open those removed paths. Because lefthook's go-test step runs the entire internal/agent package, every commit that touches a file in that package now trips the stale tests — including this bead's targeted test addition. lefthook pre-commit thus fails before AC#5 can be observed.

what is done: cli/internal/agent/tracker_lock_test.go gains TestTrackerLock_StaleLockAfterPartialLocalLand (synchronous, no wall-clock sleeps, t.TempDir() sandbox via the existing newLandTestRepo helper). It simulates worker 1 advancing local main via UpdateRefTo, leaves a lock dir owned by a known-dead PID (spawn-then-reap), and verifies worker 2's Land() breaks the stale lock, fast-forwards to its own ResultRev, preserves worker 1's commit on main (no rollback), and produces no merge commit. AC#1, #2, #3, and #4 are all satisfied — `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -run 'TestTrackerLock_' -count=1` is green (TestTrackerLock_RecordsRetryCount PASS + TestTrackerLock_StaleLockAfterPartialLocalLand PASS). The full patch is preserved at .ddx/executions/20260514T164952-8fb7870e/tracker_lock_test.patch so the change is recoverable after the worktree is reaped.

what blocks: lefthook -> go-test -> internal/agent suite runs TestBeadLifecycleSkill* which now FAIL on missing .agents/skills/ddx/bead-lifecycle/SKILL.md (the source lives only at library/skills/ddx/bead-lifecycle/SKILL.md after ddx-11ef34e1's cleanup). Resolving requires either deleting the stale contract tests or pointing them at the surviving library path — both outside this bead's area:lock / kind:test scope.

what a follow-up needs: file a sibling bead under area:agent / kind:test titled "delete or repoint stale skill-mirror contract tests" that removes (or updates the paths in) bead_lifecycle_skill_contract_test.go to match the post-ddx-11ef34e1 layout, land it, then re-dispatch ddx-c019e48a — at which point the on-disk diff (or the saved patch) can be staged and committed cleanly. Retry is safe; the test itself does not depend on the broken contract tests.

rationale: Bead ddx-90f48012 — federation: hub HTTP API + --hub-mode gating + ts-net policy + version handshake

All four acceptance criteria are already satisfied by code merged in prior beads. No further changes are required in this attempt.

AC mapping → evidence:

1. /api/federation/register, /heartbeat, /deregister endpoints exist
   - cli/internal/server/federation_hub.go:76-79 routes mounted in EnableHubMode:
       POST /api/federation/register   → handleFederationRegister  (lines 188-267)
       POST /api/federation/heartbeat  → handleFederationHeartbeat (lines 273-307)
       GET  /api/federation/spokes     → handleFederationListSpokes (lines 309-318)
       DELETE /api/federation/spokes/{id} → handleFederationDeregister (lines 320-334)

2. --hub-mode flag wired
   - cli/cmd/server.go:157 declares --hub-mode flag
   - cli/cmd/server.go:105-111 invokes srv.EnableHubMode(...) when set
   - Routes are mounted ONLY by EnableHubMode (federation_hub.go:47-80)
   - cli/cmd/server.go:99-101 rejects --federation-allow-plain-http unless --hub-mode

3. ts-net required by default; explicit --federation-allow-plain-http opt-out (warned)
   - Default policy: cli/internal/server/federation_hub.go:122-134 (requireFederationTrusted)
     rejects non-loopback / non-ts-net peers with 403 unless allowPlainHTTP=true.
   - WARN per accepted plain-HTTP registration: federation_hub.go:253-259
   - Startup banner WARN: cli/cmd/server.go:108-110

4. Version handshake at registration: reject incompatible major; degrade compatible-but-newer
   - cli/internal/federation/handshake.go (Handshake function)
     - schema mismatch → reject (reason "schema_mismatch")
     - major mismatch → reject (reason "ddx_major_mismatch")
     - newer minor/patch (same major) → accept as StatusDegraded ("degraded_newer_minor")
   - Invoked at registration time: federation_hub.go:204-211

5. Tests cover all four behaviors — cli/internal/server/federation_hub_test.go
   - Endpoints: TestFederationRoutesMountedInHubMode (line 81),
     TestFederationListAndDeregister (line 290), TestFederationHeartbeatActivatesSpoke (line 259)
   - --hub-mode gating: TestFederationRoutesNotMountedWithoutHubMode (line 59)
   - ts-net policy: TestFederationStrictDefaultRefusesPlainHTTP (line 162),
     TestFederationPlainHTTPOptOutEmitsWarn (line 171),
     TestFederationLoopbackDoesNotEmitPlainHTTPWarn (line 196)
   - Version handshake: TestFederationRegisterVersionMajorMismatchRejected (line 208),
     TestFederationRegisterSchemaMismatchRejected (line 224),
     TestFederationRegisterNewerMinorAcceptedDegraded (line 240)

Verification: all 16 federation hub tests pass on the current base revision.

.ddx/attachments/ddx-8f2e0ebf/events.jsonl:5:{"actor":"erik","body":"no_changes
rationale: orchestrator_action: decompose
status: open
reason: ddx-8f2e0ebf is feature-sized (6 acceptance criteria plus a notes-added 7th requirement) spanning unrelated subsystems — routing/preflight, the 5538-line execute-bead worker loop, heartbeat/liveness, the bead store, the `ddx bead operator-attention` command, and docs — and mixes implementation, integration tests, and documentation. It exceeds the single-pass size budget (Step 0), so it was decomposed (no implementation in this pass).

Decomposed into 5 children (created with --parent ddx-8f2e0ebf; inter-child dependency edges record landing order):

- ddx-449baa1d  bead: add atomic Store.Release so failure-release clears the claim and status reflects the lease   [AC3; foundation, no deps, currently ready]
- ddx-d8970a7b  worker: bound route resolution and routing preflight with a configurable timeout and release on expiry   [AC1; depends on ddx-449baa1d]
- ddx-dc23f001  worker: add progress watchdog for phase-empty heartbeats and terminate when the bead is closed externally   [AC2 + notes; depends on ddx-d8970a7b]
- ddx-9714eaac  worker: stop claiming a bead that wedges on consecutive claims and surface it for operator review   [AC5; depends on ddx-d8970a7b]
- ddx-58a69bb0  bead: surface every wedge/timeout lease release in ddx bead operator-attention and document the contract   [AC4 + AC6; depends on ddx-d8970a7b, ddx-dc23f001, ddx-9714eaac]

Dependency direction note: this repo's own decomposer (cli/internal/agent/recovery_decompose.go:184-210) records the parent/child edge via Parent=<parentID> and marks the parent execution-ineligible; it does NOT add a `dep add <child> <parent>` blocking edge. Following Step 0's literal `dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` (child depends-on parent) would make every child blocked by the open parent and never become ready, stalling drain. So the parent/child edge is recorded via --parent (children stay ready because the parent is open), and only correct-direction inter-child ordering edges were added with `ddx bead dep add`. The parent now carries a `decomposed into ...` note; the orchestrator owns the parent's lifecycle from here.

The requested real execute-worktree regression already exists and passes in this worktree. `cli/cmd/bead_workspace_test.go` contains `TestBeadCreate_ExecuteWorktreeRealBinaryUsesOriginPrefix` and `TestBeadCreate_RelativeEnvInsideLinkedWorktreeUsesPrimaryNamingAndStore`, and `cli/internal/bead/worktree_prefix_test.go` contains `TestGenID_WorktreePrefix`.

The code path under test already resolves the prefix from the primary workspace root rather than the linked worktree name:
- `cli/internal/bead/store.go:1818-1841` uses `detectPrefix(workingDir)` and prefers `git rev-parse --show-toplevel` from the supplied working directory.
- `cli/internal/bead/store.go:1845-1858` falls back to the provided working dir, not the process cwd, so execute-bead-shaped worktrees do not contaminate the ID prefix.

The verified behavior is:
- real subprocess `ddx bead create` from an execute-bead-shaped linked worktree produces an `origin-<hex>` ID, not a `.execute-bead-wt-...` ID;
- the new bead is persisted in the primary workspace `.ddx/beads.jsonl`;
- the isolated worktree `.ddx/beads.jsonl` does not receive the new bead row.

  links pointing into that XDG plugins path.

  convention modes.
  PASSES.
