reason: All three ACs are already satisfied by code merged via the prior
sibling bead ddx-23c67ca4 ("S15-5: Execute-loop wake-on-approve +
origin_operator_prompt_id backlinks", closed at commit 39ff5d16). The
current bead ddx-1e680a00 is a redundant restatement of the same S15-5
work under the same parent epic ddx-1d85c927. Evidence:

AC1 — "Execute loop picks up newly-ready operator-prompts":
  - cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_operator_prompt.go invokes
    ExecuteLoopWaker.WakeProject(workingDir) on every successful
    operatorPromptApprove and on operatorPromptSubmit auto-approve.
  - cli/internal/server/workers.go wires WorkerManager as the waker.
  - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go consumes the wake signal via
    SleepOrWake, dropping the idle-poll sleep when an approve fires.
  - Tests: TestOperatorPromptApprove_SignalsWake,
    TestOperatorPromptSubmit_AutoApprove_SignalsWake,
    TestOperatorPromptCancel_DoesNotSignalWake,
    TestOperatorPromptApprove_NilWakerNoCrash
    (cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_operator_prompt_wake_test.go).

AC2 — "origin_operator_prompt_id added to events / artifact frontmatter":
  - cli/internal/agent/operator_prompt_backlinks.go computes the
    base..result diff after a successful operator-prompt execution,
    classifies changed paths into affected beads vs artifacts, and
    appends an `origin_operator_prompt_id` BeadEvent to every affected
    bead plus a summary event on the originator listing every affected
    bead and artifact path.
  - The wiring fires from execute_bead_loop.go ~L794-810 inside the
    ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess branch, gated on
    candidate.IssueType == bead.IssueTypeOperatorPrompt.
  - The events form satisfies the spec wording in
    docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-021 §Bead shape: "every artifact
    mutated by an operator-prompt execution gets an
    origin_operator_prompt_id event so provenance survives later edits".
  - Tests: TestComputeOperatorPromptAffected_DiffsBeadsAndArtifacts,
    TestRecordOperatorPromptBacklinks_AppendsEventsOnAffectedAndOriginator,
    TestRecordOperatorPromptBacklinks_EmptyAffectedNoEvents,
    TestRecordOperatorPromptBacklinks_ConcurrentTargetSameBead.

AC3 — "Backlinks queryable via ddx bead show":
  - `ddx bead show <id> --json` calls Store.LoadEventsInline (cmd/bead.go
    L330) which inlines events for both active and archived beads, so
    `origin_operator_prompt_id` events are visible via the JSON shape:
        ddx bead show <originator-or-affected-id> --json \
          | jq '.events[] | select(.kind == "origin_operator_prompt_id")'
  - Equivalently, `ddx bead evidence list <id>` (cmd/bead.go L654-668)
    enumerates the same events.

against the current tree. Exit 0 ⇒ all three ACs remain satisfied, the
bead can close as already-done.

rationale: All acceptance criteria already satisfied at base-rev e82a9bc6.

This bead (ddx-7e9cc0cc) duplicates ddx-1bbd1096 ("B14.2: cli/internal/federation
package - state types + persistence + schema_version"), which was closed and
landed in commit 0dd3cead "feat(federation): add internal/federation state
types + persistence [ddx-1bbd1096]" — an ancestor of base-rev.

AC1 — internal/federation/ package with state types: SATISFIED.
  cli/internal/federation/state.go defines:
    - FederationRegistry { SchemaVersion, Spokes }
    - SpokeRecord { NodeID, Name, URL, DDxVersion, SchemaVersion, Capabilities,
      RegisteredAt, LastHeartbeat, Status } — keyed by stable NodeID
    - SpokeStatus enum: registered/active/stale/offline/degraded (+ Valid())
    - NewRegistry, FindSpoke, UpsertSpoke (NodeID-keyed insert/replace),
      RemoveSpoke, SetStatus, ReconcileLiveness
  Heartbeat data lives on SpokeRecord.LastHeartbeat (time.Time); registration
  version on SpokeRecord.SchemaVersion / DDxVersion plus the registry-level
  CurrentSchemaVersion constant. The bead's named types (Spoke, Heartbeat,
  RegistrationVersion) map onto these existing fields; no new wrapper types
  are needed and adding them would diverge from the call sites already wired
  in fanout.go, spoke.go, handshake.go.

AC2 — persistence layer with atomic writes: SATISFIED.
  cli/internal/federation/persistence.go:
    - DefaultStatePath honors XDG_DATA_HOME, default ~/.local/share/ddx/
      federation-state.json (the "or similar" path slot in the bead).
    - SaveStateTo: marshal -> tmpfile in target dir -> fsync -> chmod 0600 ->
      atomic rename. writeMu serializes intra-process writers.
    - LoadStateFrom: missing-file -> fresh registry; malformed JSON ->
      quarantine to "<path>.corrupt-<ts>" and return fresh registry;
      pre-versioned files default to CurrentSchemaVersion.

AC3 — tests: SATISFIED.
  cli/internal/federation/state_test.go (313 lines) covers round-trip,
  missing-file, malformed-file recovery, status transitions, NodeID upsert
  semantics. `go test ./internal/federation/` passes (verified at this rev).

No code changes were necessary or appropriate; implementing again would
duplicate or churn working, integrated code that downstream beads
(ddx-ab7f6184, ddx-238880c4, ddx-89bc2db4) already depend on.

rationale: outcome: B — gap already resolved in fizeau v0.12.0 (current go.mod pin)

Investigation confirmed that the routing gap described in the bead (fizeau v0.10.3
skipping the routing engine when Harness is set + Model empty + Profile/MinPower
present) no longer exists. fizeau v0.12.0 — already pinned in cli/go.mod — contains:

1. resolveExecuteRoute handles explicit Harness + empty Model + non-empty Policy/MinPower
   by calling resolveExecuteRouteWithEngine within the harness's eligible models.
   (~/go/pkg/mod/github.com/easel/fizeau@v0.12.0/service_execute.go, function
   resolveExecuteRoute, empty-model routing guard block)

2. TestExecute_ExplicitHarnessEmptyModelWithProfile_RoutesWithinHarness (AC4) and
   TestExecute_ExplicitHarnessEmptyModelNoProfile_FailsClearly (AC5) already exist in
   fizeau v0.12.0's service_execute_harness_pin_test.go:270,331 — both explicitly
   reference ddx-1e516bc9 in their docstrings.

3. Class 2 harness guard: TestExecute_Class2HarnessEmptyModelWithProfile_FailsClearly
   at line 351 covers the gemini/opencode/pi AutoRoutingEligible=false path.

4. cli/internal/agent/service_run.go:247-251 already carries the AC10 comment
   documenting the well-formed request shape.

5. go.mod already at v0.12.0 — no bump needed (AC3).

AC6 (end-to-end verification) remains gated on ddx-cfedee8e / ddx-9228a484 C9
supplying MinPower to Execute. This dependency is explicit in the bead description
and is out of scope for this bead.

Full investigation at .ddx/executions/20260511T025545-a3984070/fizeau-execute-routing-investigation.md

  <bead id="axon-dccbdf73">
    <title>auth-jwt-pipeline: sign + verify with revocation cache + request extension</title>
    <description>
Implement the full JWT verification middleware in axon-server.

## What you're building

An axum middleware layer that runs on every data-plane request. It validates the JWT, installs a (user_id, tenant_id, grants) tuple into the request extension, and returns a typed AuthError on any failure. Downstream handlers read the extension and trust it completely.

## Scope — exactly what to add

### New file: `crates/axon-server/src/auth_pipeline.rs`

A module exposing two things:

1. A `JwtIssuer` helper that signs a `JwtClaims` into a compact JWT string. HS256 is the only algorithm required for this bead. The signing key is a `Vec<u8>` passed in at construction. Provide `issue(&self, claims: &JwtClaims) -> Result<String, AuthError>` and a `verify(&self, token: &str) -> Result<JwtClaims, AuthError>` for round-tripping in tests.

2. An axum middleware `async fn jwt_verify_layer` that runs the 8-step verification order below. On success, installs a `ResolvedIdentity { user_id, tenant_id, grants }` into the request extension. On failure, returns a response built via `AuthError::into_response()` so the mapping to HTTP status + error.code is consistent.

The middleware also owns an `InMemoryRevocationCache` (a small `tokio::sync::RwLock<HashSet<Uuid>>` is fine; nothing fancy) and falls back to SQL (via the storage adapter's new `is_jti_revoked` method) on cache miss.

### Extend: `crates/axon-core/src/auth.rs`

- Add `ResolvedIdentity { pub user_id: UserId, pub tenant_id: TenantId, pub grants: Grants }`.
- Add `impl AuthError` with `pub fn status_code(&self) -> u16` returning 401 or 403 per ADR-018 §4 failure table, and `pub fn error_code(&self) -> &'static str` returning the stable code string. (An axum `IntoResponse` impl can live in axon-server.)
- Add `impl Grants` with `pub fn find_database(&self, name: &str) -> Option<&GrantedDatabase>` and `impl GrantedDatabase` with `pub fn has_op(&self, op: Op) -> bool`.

### Extend: `crates/axon-storage/src/adapter.rs` (trait)

Add one method to the `StorageAdapter` trait:

```rust
async fn is_jti_revoked(&self, jti: Uuid) -> Result<bool, AxonError>;
```

Implement it in `crates/axon-storage/src/memory.rs` (store a `HashSet<Uuid>`; default-empty), `crates/axon-storage/src/sqlite.rs`, and `crates/axon-storage/src/postgres.rs` (`SELECT 1 FROM credential_revocations WHERE jti = $1`). The A1 schema (`auth_schema.rs`) already has the `credential_revocations` table — just query it.

### Wire into: `crates/axon-server/src/lib.rs`

Re-export `auth_pipeline::{jwt_verify_layer, JwtIssuer, InMemoryRevocationCache}`. Don't wire it into the main router yet — that's D-phase work. For this bead, the middleware just needs to exist and be testable in isolation.

## Verification order (normative — ADR-018 §4)

1. Extract `Authorization: Bearer <jwt>` from the request. Missing header → `AuthError::Unauthenticated` (401). The `--no-auth` bypass is NOT part of this bead — that's E2.
2. Parse and verify the JWT signature with HS256 against the issuer's key. Signature invalid → `CredentialInvalid` (401). Malformed structure → `CredentialMalformed` (401). `aud` as a JSON array → `CredentialMalformed` (this falls out of the A2 `parse_claims` wrapper — use it).
3. Check `nbf` and `exp` against `SystemTime::now()` with a 30s skew budget. `exp` more than 30s in the past → `CredentialExpired` (401). `nbf` more than 30s in the future → `CredentialNotYetValid` (401).
4. Check `claims.jti` against the revocation cache, then SQL on miss. Hit → `CredentialRevoked` (401). Populate the cache on SQL hit to speed up subsequent checks.
5. Compare `claims.aud` to the `{tenant}` segment extracted from the URL path. Mismatch → `CredentialWrongTenant` (**403**, not 401 — see the table). The URL extraction path is: look at `request.uri().path()` and parse the leading `/tenants/{tenant}/databases/{database}/...` segments. For this bead, implement a small helper `fn extract_tenant_database(path: &str) -> Option<(&str, &str)>` that returns `None` for non-data-plane paths so they skip the middleware.
6. Look up the `sub` UUID in the `users` table via a new `StorageAdapter::get_user(UserId) -> Result<Option<User>, AxonError>` method. User not found or `user.suspended_at_ms.is_some()` → `UserSuspended` (401).
7. Check tenant membership: a new `StorageAdapter::get_tenant_member(TenantId, UserId) -> Result<Option<TenantMember>, AxonError>`. Missing → `NotATenantMember` (403).
8. Walk `claims.grants.databases[]` looking for an entry matching the URL `{database}`. None → `DatabaseNotGranted` (403). Found → compute the required op from the HTTP method (GET = Read, POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE = Write) and intersect with the grant's ops. Empty intersection → `OpNotGranted` (403). Admin-requiring routes are out of scope for this bead — the middleware only handles Read/Write classification.
9. Build `ResolvedIdentity` and install it via `request.extensions_mut().insert(resolved)`.

## Error mapping — must exactly match ADR-018 §4

The mapping table is copied here verbatim. Do not invent variants; the 14 AuthError variants already exist in axon-core.

| Failure | Status | `error.code` |
|---|---|---|
| No Authorization header | 401 | `unauthenticated` |
| Header not `Bearer <token>` or base64 broken | 401 | `credential_malformed` |
| JWT structurally invalid / missing claims | 401 | `credential_malformed` |
| `aud` as JSON array | 401 | `credential_malformed` |
| Signature invalid | 401 | `credential_invalid` |
| `exp` in past (beyond skew) | 401 | `credential_expired` |
| `nbf` in future (beyond skew) | 401 | `credential_not_yet_valid` |
| `jti` revoked | 401 | `credential_revoked` |
| `iss` unknown | 401 | `credential_foreign_issuer` — for this bead, reject if `claims.iss != issuer.issuer_id` |
| `aud` ≠ URL tenant | **403** | `credential_wrong_tenant` |
| `sub` suspended/deleted | 401 | `user_suspended` |
| `sub` not a tenant member | **403** | `not_a_tenant_member` |
| URL database not in grants | **403** | `database_not_granted` |
| Op not in grant | **403** | `op_not_granted` |

Note the 401 vs 403 split: credential-level problems are 401, scope-level problems are 403.

## Testing — all must pass for this bead to be complete

### Unit tests in `crates/axon-server/tests/auth_pipeline_test.rs`

For every row of the error mapping table, build the minimal defective request and assert the middleware produces exactly that `(status, error_code)` pair. Table-driven with `#[test]` or a single `#[test]` that iterates an array of cases.

Additionally:

- `jwt_roundtrip_succeeds`: issue a valid JWT with `JwtIssuer::issue`, verify it with `JwtIssuer::verify`, assert claims match.
- `valid_jwt_populates_request_extension`: run the full middleware on a valid JWT, assert the response is 200 and the handler sees `ResolvedIdentity` in extensions.
- `revocation_takes_effect_within_one_second`: issue a valid JWT, verify it succeeds, insert the jti into the revocation cache, verify the next request returns 401 `credential_revoked`. Do NOT use sleep — invalidate the cache directly.
- `clock_skew_30s_accepted_31s_rejected`: construct a JWT with `exp` at `now - 29s` (should succeed) and one with `exp` at `now - 31s` (should fail with `credential_expired`). Do the same for `nbf`.
- `ops_matrix`: for each HTTP method in {GET, POST, PUT, PATCH, DELETE} and each op set in the grant, assert the expected 200/403 outcome.

### Integration test in `crates/axon-server/tests/auth_pipeline_integration_test.rs`

Build a minimal axum router with one dummy route `GET /tenants/acme/databases/orders/ping`. Install the middleware. Hit it with:
- No auth header → 401 `unauthenticated`
- Valid JWT with `grants: [{name: "orders", ops: [read]}]` → 200
- Valid JWT with the wrong tenant in aud → 403 `credential_wrong_tenant`
- Valid JWT with grants for a different database → 403 `database_not_granted`

### axon-sim workload `inv_018_grant_enforcement`

**If this is too large for one pass, skip the axon-sim workload and note it in the commit message for a follow-up bead.** The core verification must ship; the simulation workload can follow. Do not block the whole bead on the workload.

## Crate dependencies you will need

Add to `crates/axon-server/Cargo.toml` under `[dependencies]`:
```toml
jsonwebtoken = "9"
```

Everything else (axum, tower, tokio, uuid, serde_json, http) is already in the workspace.

## Context you must know about

### A1 landed: schema
- `crates/axon-storage/src/auth_schema.rs` has the SQL for `users`, `user_identities`, `tenant_users`, `credential_revocations`, etc.
- `user_identities.external_id` (NOT `subject`) — already corrected.
- Migration runs via `apply_auth_migrations_sqlite` / `_postgres`.

### A2 landed: types
- `crates/axon-core/src/auth.rs` has all the types: `TenantId`, `UserId`, `User`, `UserIdentity`, `Tenant`, `TenantMember`, `TenantRole`, `Op`, `GrantedDatabase`, `Grants`, `JwtClaims`, `AuthError` (14 variants).
- `parse_claims(&str) -> Result<JwtClaims, AuthError>` wraps serde_json and maps errors to `CredentialMalformed`. USE IT.
- `AUTH_ERROR_VARIANT_COUNT == 14` is enforced via compile-time exhaustive match.
- Delegation helper on `TenantRole`: `admin.can_delegate(op)` / etc.

### Files you should NOT touch
- Anything outside `crates/axon-server/src/auth_pipeline.rs`, `crates/axon-core/src/auth.rs` (additive), `crates/axon-storage/src/{adapter,memory,sqlite,postgres}.rs` (additive), and the two new test files.
- The existing `crates/axon-server/src/auth.rs` — that's the Tailscale whois path. Leave it alone. Your pipeline is a new module.

## Quality bar

You are the implementation step. A post-merge review with opus will verify the result against the AC. If your commit misses an AC item, the bead reopens and escalates to a higher power class. **Don't commit partial work hoping the review slides.** If you cannot complete the bead in this pass, write a `no_changes_rationale.txt` explaining exactly what's done and what's blocking, and do NOT commit — the bead will be re-queued.

Specifically: if you cannot make `cargo test -p axon-server` and `cargo clippy -p axon-server -- -D warnings` green, do not commit. Fix the issues first.

You are a coding agent implementing a single bead inside an isolated git worktree. Work from the bead description above — it is complete and unambiguous. The AC list is the completion contract.

## How to work

1. **Read first, act second.** Read the governing ADR-018 sections and the existing crates/axon-core/src/auth.rs so you understand the AuthError variants and the parse_claims wrapper. Then read crates/axon-storage/src/auth_schema.rs to confirm the credential_revocations table shape. Then start coding.

2. **Cross-reference your work against the AC as you go.** Every AC checkbox must be provably satisfied by a piece of code you can point to. Before committing, re-read the AC and physically tick each one.

3. **Run the verification commands locally before committing.** cargo test and cargo clippy per the AC. If either fails, fix it. Do not commit red code.

4. **Commit once, when everything works.** Do not commit partial or exploratory changes. The bead is a single atomic unit of work.

5. **If you cannot complete the bead**, write `.ddx/executions/b1-enhanced-manual/no_changes_rationale.txt` with (a) what's done, (b) what's blocking, (c) why a follow-up bead is needed. Do not commit red code to "make progress".

## Constraints

- Work only in the files listed under "Scope" and "Files you should NOT touch" above.
- Do not modify CLAUDE.md or add new documentation.
- Do not run `ddx init`.
- Keep the `.ddx/executions/` directory intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- Commit with `git add <specific files> && git commit -m 'feat(auth-pipeline): ... [axon-dccbdf73]'`. Do not `git add -A` — there may be other WIP in the worktree.

## Done definition

`cargo test -p axon-server -p axon-core -p axon-storage` green + `cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings` clean + every AC item ticked + one commit. That's done.

apply_auth_migrations_sqlite(conn: &rusqlite::Connection) -> Result<(), String> {\n19\t    // Create tenants table.\n20\t    // UNIQUE(name) enforced by the UNIQUE constraint on the name column.\n21\t    conn.execute_batch(\n22\t        \"CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS tenants (\n23\t            id                 TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n24\t            name               TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,\n25\t            display_name       TEXT NOT NULL,\n26\t            created_at_ms      INTEGER NOT NULL,\n27\t            updated_at_ms      INTEGER NOT NULL\n28\t        )\",\n29\t    )\n30\t    .map_err(|e| KEY,\n36\t            display_name       TEXT NOT NULL,\n37\t            email              TEXT,\n38\t            created_at_ms      INTEGER NOT NULL,\n39\t            suspended_at_ms    INTEGER\n40\t        )\",\n41\t    )\n42\t    .map_err(|e| format!(\"users table: {e}\"))?;\n43\t\n44\t    // Create user_identities table.\n45\t    // PRIMARY KEY (provider, external_id) enforces UNIQUE(provider, external_id).\n46\t    conn.execute_batch(\n47\t        \"CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS user_identities (\n48\t            provider       TEXT NOT NULL,\n49\t            external_id    TEXT NOT NULL,\n50\t            user_id        TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id),\n51\t            created_at_ms  INTEGER NOT NULL,\n52\t            PRIMARY KEY (provider, external_id)\n53\t        )\",\n54\t    )\n55\t    .map_err(|e| format!(\"user_identities table: {e}\"))?;\n56\t\n57\t    // Create tenant_users table.\n58\t    // PRIMARY KEY (tenant_id, user_id) is the composite PK required )\n68\t    .map_err(|e| format!(\"tenant_users table: {e}\"))?;\n69\t\n70\t    // Create tenant_databases table.\n71\t    conn.execute_batch(\n72\t        \"CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS tenant_databases {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01DGpwMtL9EAnaRPBK3GqEBs","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t//! Axon server — HTTP gateway, gRPC service, MCP stdio, and authentication.\n2\t//!\n3\t//! # Authentication\n4\t//!\n5\t//! Identity is resolved once per request by [`auth::AuthContext`] and\n6\t//! injected as a typed extension.  Three modes are supported:\n7\t//!\n8\t//! | Mode | Flag | Actor | Role |\n9\t//! |------|------|-------|------|\n10\t//! | `NoAuth` | `--no-auth` | `\"anonymous\"` | Admin |\n11\t//! | `Tailscale` | *(default)* | node name | from ACL tags |\n12\t//! | `Guest` | `--guest-role <role>` | `\"guest\"` | configured role |\n13\t//!\n14\t//! In `Tailscale` mode the server contacts the local Tailscale daemon over its\n15\t//! Unix socket (`/run/tailscale/tailscaled.sock` by default) and calls\n16\t//! `/localapi/v0/whois?addr=<peer>` to resolve the connecting node's identity.\n17\t//! Resolved identities are cached by peer IP for the duration of\n18\t//! `--auth-cache-ttl-secs` (default 60 s).\n19\t//!\n20\t//! ACL tag → role mapping:\n21\t//! - `tag:axon-admin` → [`Role::Admin`]\n22\t//! - `tag:axon-write` / `tag:axon-agent` → [`Role::Write`]\n23\t//! - `tag:axon-read` → [`Role::Read`]\n24\t//! - no matching tag → `--tailscale-default-role` (default `read`)\n25\t//!\n26\t//! Connections that are not on the tailnet are rejected with HTTP 401 / gRPC\n27\t//! `UNAUTHENTICATED`.  If the Tailscale daemon is unreachable the server\n28\t//! returns HTTP 503 / gRPC `UNAVAILABLE`.\n29\t//!\n30\t//! # Request flow\n31\t//!\n32\t//! ```text\n33\t//! TCP accept\n34\t//!   └─ authenticate_http_request  (gateway.rs middleware)\n35\t//!        ├─ AuthContext::resolve_peer  →  Identity\n36\t//!        └─ insert Identity into request extensions\n37\t//!             └─ route handler\n38\t//!                  ├─ extract Extension<Identity>\n39\t//!                  ├─ identity.require_read() / require_write() / require_admin()\n40\t//!                  └─ handler logic\n41\t//! ```\n42\t//!\n43\t//! gRPC follows the same pattern via [`service::AxonServiceImpl::authorize`].\n44\tpub mod actor_scope;\n45\tpub mod auth;\n46\tmod collection_listing;\n47\tpub(crate) mod embedded_ui;\n48\tpub mod control_plane;\n49\tpub mod control_plane_routes;\n50\tpub mod cors_config;\n51\tpub mod user_roles;\n52\tpub mod gateway;\n53\tpub mod idempotency;\n54\tpub(crate) mod mcp_http;\n55\tpub mod mcp_stdio;\n56\tpub mod rate_limit;\n57\tpub mod schema_registry;\n58\tpub mod serve;\n59\tpub mod service;\n60\tpub mod tenant_router;\n61\t\n62\tpub use auth::{AuthContext, AuthMode, Identity, Role};\n63\tpub use mcp_stdio::run_mcp_stdio;\n64\tpub use service::AxonServiceImpl;\n65\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01TSMSYvQzdh78YH3Sxyiy8q","session_id":"80f0eb9d-4064-4d06-8560-e247ce1170f2","uuid":"b2e10ba8-f883-42a2-b408-1004661a2ce3","timestamp":"2026-04-15T01:30:53.127Z"}

digits, `_`, and `$`, and\n1183\t/// must start with a letter or `_`. We apply a conservative subset of that\n1184\t/// rule here to keep things simple and safe.\n1185\tfn validate_pg_db_name(name: &str) -> Result<(), AxonError> {\n1186\t    if name.is_empty() {\n1187\t        return Err(AxonError::InvalidArgument(\n1188\t            \"database name must not be empty\".to_owned(),\n1189\t        ));\n1190\t    }\n1191\t    let first = name.chars().next().expect(\"non-empty checked above\");\n1192\t    if !first.is_ascii_alphabetic() && first != '_' {\n1193\t        return Err(AxonError::InvalidArgument(format!(\n1194\t            \"database name '{name}' must start with a letter or underscore\"\n1195\t        )));\n1196\t    }\n1197\t    if !name.chars().all(|c| c.is_ascii_alphanumeric() || c == '_') {\n1198\t        return Err(AxonError::InvalidArgument(format!(\n1199\t            \"database name '{name}' contains invalid characters (only ASCII alphanumeric and _ allowed)\"\n1200\t        )));\n1201\t    }\n1202\t    Ok(())\n1203\t}\n1204\t\n1205\t/// Derive a per-tenant DSN from a superadmin DSN by replacing/adding the\n1206\t/// `dbname` key.\n1207\t///\n1208\t/// The superadmin DSN uses the `postgres` maintenance database.  We produce a\n1209\t/// new DSN that targets `axon_{name}`.\n1210\t///\n1211\t/// Both libpq keyword–value format (`host=... dbname=...`) and URL format\n1212\t/// (`postgres://...`) are supported.\n1213\tpub fn tenant_dsn(superadmin_dsn: &str, tenant_db_name: &str) -> String {\n1214\t    let target = format!(\"axon_{tenant_db_name}\");\n1215\t    if superadmin_dsn.starts_with(\"postgres://\") || superadmin_dsn.starts_with(\"postgresql://\") {\n1216\t        // URL format: replace path component.\n1217\t        // e.g. postgres://user:pass@host/somedb?sslmode=disable\n1218\t        if let Some(pos) = superadmin_dsn.find(\"://\") {\n1219\t            let after_scheme = &superadmin_dsn[pos + 3..];\n1220\t            // Find the slash that separates authority from path.\n1221\t            if let Some(slash_pos) = after_scheme.find('/') {\n1222\t                let scheme_authority = &superadmin_dsn[..pos + 3 + slash_pos];\n1223\t                let rest = &after_scheme[slash_pos + 1..];\n1224\t                // Strip existing dbname (everything before '?').\n1225\t                let query = if let Some(q) = rest.find('?') {\n1226\t                    &rest[q..]\n1227\t                } else {\n1228\t                    \"\"\n1229\t                };\n1230\t                return format!(\"{scheme_authority}/{target}{query}\");\n1231\t            }\n1232\t            // No path: append the target database.\n1233\t            return format!(\"{superadmin_dsn}/{target}\");\n1234\t        }\n1235\t        format!(\"{superadmin_dsn}/{target}\")\n1236\t    } else {\n1237\t        // Keyword–value format.  Replace existing dbname= or append it.\n1238\t        let mut parts: Vec<String> = superadmin_dsn\n1239\t            .split_whitespace()\n1240\t            .filter(|part| !part.starts_with(\"dbname=\"))\n1241\t            .map(str::to_owned)\n1242\t            .collect();\n1243\t        parts.push(format!(\"dbname={target}\"));\n1244\t        parts.join(\" \")\n1245\t    }\n1246\t}\n1247\t\n1248\t/// Create a physical PostgreSQL database named `axon_{name}` using a\n1249\t/// superadmin connection.\n1250\t///\n1251\t/// The `superadmin_dsn` must connect to the `postgres` maintenance database\n1252\t/// (or any database the superuser can reach) with sufficient privileges to\n1253\t/// execute `CREATE DATABASE`.\n1254\t///\n1255\t/// The database name is validated to prevent SQL injection.\n1256\t///\n1257\t/// # Errors\n1258\t///\n1259\t/// Returns `AxonError::AlreadyExists` if the database already exists.\n1260\t/// Returns `AxonError::InvalidArgument` if `name` contains invalid characters.\n1261\t/// Returns `AxonError::Storage` for other PostgreSQL errors.\n1262\tpub fn provision_postgres_database(superadmin_dsn: &str, name: &str) -> Result<(), AxonError> {\n1263\t    validate_pg_db_name(name)?;\n1264\t    let full_name = format!(\"axon_{name}\");\n1265\t    let rt = tokio::runtime::Builder::new_current_thread()\n1266\t        .enable_all()\n1267\t        .build()\n1268\t        .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\n1269\t    rt.block_on(async {\n1270\t        let (client, connection) = tokio_postgres::connect(superadmin_dsn, NoTls)\n1271\t            .await\n1272\t            .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\n1273\t        tokio::spawn(async move {\n1274\t            let _ = connection.await;\n1275\t        });\n1276\t        let row = client\n1277\t            .query_one(\n1278\t                \"SELECT EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM pg_database WHERE datname = $1)\",\n1279\t                &[&full_name.as_str()],\n1280\t            )\n1281\t            .await\n1282\t            .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\n1283\t        let exists: bool = row.get(0);\n1284\t        if exists {\n1285\t            return Err(AxonError::AlreadyExists(format!(\n1286\t implicit transaction that execute() would open.\n1291\t        client\n1292\t            .simple_query(&format!(\"CREATE DATABASE \\\"{full_name}\\\"\"))\n1293\t            .await\n1294\t Role-based and attribute-based access control for Axon (FEAT-012).\n1\t//!\n2\t//! # Layers\n3\t//!\n4\t//! **Layer 1 — RBAC** ([`Role`], [`CallerIdentity`], [`Operation`])\n5\t//!\n6\t//! Four built-in roles (`Admin > Write > Read > None`) control access to\n7\t//! Axon operations.  Roles are derived from the identity provider (Tailscale\n8\t//! ACL tags, OIDC claims, etc.) by the server layer and passed into handlers\n9\t//! as a [`CallerIdentity`].  Handlers call [`CallerIdentity::check`] to\n10\t//! enforce the minimum required role.\n11\t//!\n12\t//! **Layer 2 — Field-level masking** ([`MaskPolicy`])\n13\t//!\n14\t//! A `MaskPolicy` hides individual entity fields from callers whose role\n15\t//! falls below a configured minimum.  Applied by [`CallerIdentity::apply_masks`]\n16\t//! before returning entity data to the caller.  Admins always see all fields.\n17\t//!\n18\t//! **Layer 3 — Collection write control** ([`WritePolicy`])\n19\t//!\n20\t//! A `WritePolicy` sets a per-collection minimum write role and marks fields\n21\t//! as immutable after creation.  Checked by the handler before any mutation.\n22\t//!\n23\t//! **Layer 4 — Database-scoped grants** ([`DatabaseGrant`], [`GrantRegistry`])\n24\t//!\n25\t//! Grants scope a role to a specific database (or `\"*\"` for all databases).\n26\t//! Without a matching grant a caller has `Role::None` on that database.\n27\t//! Admins with a global grant bypass per-database checks.  The grant registry\n28\t//! is stored in `__axon_policies__` and enforced at the routing layer.\n29\t\n30\tuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\n31\t\n32\tuse crate::error::AxonError;\n33\t\n34\t/// Built-in roles for Axon RBAC (FEAT-012).\n35\t///\n36\t/// Roles are ordered by privilege: `Admin > Write > Read > None`.\n37\t/// When a user has multiple roles (e.g., from multiple ACL tags),\n38\t/// the highest-privilege role wins.\n39\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, PartialOrd, Ord, Hash, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n40\tpub enum Role {\n41\t    /// No access. Explicitly denied.\n42\t    None = 0,\n43\t    /// Read entities, query, traverse, browse audit log.\n44\t    Read = 1,\n45\t    /// Create, update, delete entities and links in any collection.\n46\t    Write = 2,\n47\t    /// All operations including drop and schema changes.\n48\t    Admin = 3,\n49\t}\n50\t\n51\timpl Role {\n52\t    /// Parse a role from a Tailscale ACL tag or string name.\n53\t    ///\n54\t    /// Accepted inputs (both full `tag:` prefix and bare name):\n55\t    ///\n56\t    /// | Input | Role |\n57\t    /// |-------|------|\n58\t    /// | `\"tag:axon-admin\"` / `\"tag:admin\"` / `\"admin\"` | `Admin` |\n59\t    /// | `\"tag:axon-write\"` / `\"tag:axon-agent\"` / `\"tag:write\"` / `\"write\"` | `Write` |\n60\t    /// | `\"tag:axon-read\"` / `\"tag:read\"` / `\"read\"` | `Read` |\n61\t    /// | anything else | `None` |\n62\t    ///\n63\t    /// `tag:axon-agent` is an alias for `write` — the conventional tag for\n64\t    /// automated agent workloads that need read/write but not admin access.\n65\t    pub fn from_tag(tag: &str) -> Self {\n66\t        match tag {\n67\t            \"tag:axon-admin\" | \"tag:admin\" | \"admin\" => Role::Admin,\n68\t            \"tag:axon-write\" | \"tag:axon-agent\" | \"tag:write\" | \"write\" => Role::Write,\n69\t            \"tag:axon-read\" | \"tag:read\" | \"read\" => Role::Read,\n70\t            _ => Role::None,\n71\t        }\n72\t    }\n73\t\n74\t    /// Determine the highest-privilege role from a set of tags.\n75\t    pub fn from_tags(tags: &[String]) -> Self {\n76\t        tags.iter()\n77\t   Self(val.to_bits())\n48\t    }\n49\t\n50\t    pub fn value(&self) -> f64 {\n51\t        f64::from_bits(self.0)\n52\t    }\n53\t}\n54\t\n55\timpl PartialEq for OrderedFloat {\n56\t    fn eq(&self, match index_type {\n92\t        IndexType::String => json_val.as_str().map(|s| IndexValue::String(s.to_string())),\n93\t        IndexType::Integer => json_val.as_i64().map(IndexValue::Integer),\n94\t json_val.as_str().map(|s| IndexValue::String(s.to_string()))\n101\t        }\n102\t    }\n103\t}\n104\t\n105\t/// A compound index key: ordered list of field values for multi-field indexes.\n106\t///\n107\t/// Implements `Ord` lexicographically, enabling prefix matching via\n108\t/// BTreeMap range scans.\n109\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, PartialOrd, Ord, Implementations wrap specific databases (SQLite, PostgreSQL, FoundationDB).\n146\t/// All operations are executed within the context of the adapter's internal\n147\t/// transaction semantics.\n148\tpub trait StorageAdapter: Send + Sync {\n149\t    /// Retrieves an entity by collection and ID.\n150\t    fn get(&self, collection: &CollectionId, id: &EntityId) -> `start`: inclusive lower bound (no lower bound if `None`)\n165\t    /// - `end`: inclusive upper bound (no upper bound if `None`)\n166\t    /// - `limit`: maximum number of results (unlimited if `None`)\n167\t    fn range_scan(\n168\t        &self,\n169\t        collection: &CollectionId,\n170\t        start: Option<&EntityId>,\n171\t        end: Option<&EntityId>,\n172\t        limit: Option<usize>,\n173\t    ) -> Result<Vec<Entity>, AxonError>;\n174\t\n175\t    /// Atomically updates an entity using optimistic concurrency control (OCC).\n176\t    ///\n177\t    /// Writes `entity` only if the current stored version equals `expected_version`.\n178\t    /// On success, the stored version is incremented to `expected_version + 1` and\n179\t    /// the updated entity is returned.\n180\t    ///\n181\t    /// Returns [`AxonError::ConflictingVersion`] if the version does not match or\n182\t    /// the entity does not exist.\n183\t    fn compare_and_swap(\n184\t        &mut self,\n185\t        entity: Entity,\n186\t        expected_version: u64,\n187\t    ) -> Result<Entity, AxonError>;\n188\t\n189\t    /// Atomically inserts `entity` only if no live entity currently exists.\n190\t    ///\n191\t    /// This is used for rollback/recovery flows that recreate a deleted entity\n192\t    /// after the caller has already validated the deleted version via audit\n193\t    /// history. `expected_absent_version` is echoed back in\n194\t    /// [`AxonError::ConflictingVersion`] when another writer recreated the\n195\t    /// entity first.\n196\t    fn create_if_absent(\n197\t        &mut self,\n198\t        entity: Entity,\n199\t        expected_absent_version: u64,\n200\t    ) -> Result<Entity, AxonError>;\n201\t\n202\t    /// Begin a storage-level transaction.\n203\t    ///\n204\t    /// After this call, all writes are buffered or protected until [`commit_tx`]\n205\t    /// or [`abort_tx`] is called. axon_schema::schema::{CollectionSchema, CollectionView};\n\nuse crate::adapter::StorageAdapter;\n\n/// PostgreSQL-backed storage adapter.\n///\n/// Uses the asynchronous `tokio-postgres` crate driven from a dedicated Tokio\n/// runtime. Because `StorageAdapter` is synchronous, each database call\n/// blocks the calling thread via `handle.block_on(...)`.\n///\n/// Transactions are handled via `BEGIN` / `COMMIT` / `ROLLBACK` statements.\n/// The adapter creates the required tables on initialization if they do not\n/// exist.\npub struct PostgresStorageAdapter {\n    client: tokio_postgres::Client,\n    handle: tokio::runtime::Handle,\n    _rt: tokio::runtime::Runtime,\n    in_tx: bool,\n}\n\nimpl PostgresStorageAdapter {\n    /// Connect to a PostgreSQL database using a connection string.\n    ///\n    /// Example: `\"host=localhost user=axon dbname=axon\"`\n    pub fn connect(params: &str) -> Result<Self, AxonError> {\n        let rt = tokio::runtime::Builder::new_multi_thread()\n            .worker_threads(2)\n            .enable_all()\n            .build()\n            .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\n        let handle = rt.handle().clone();\n        let (client, connection) = handle\n            .block_on(tokio_postgres::connect(params, NoTls))\n            .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(format!(\"connection failed: {e}\")))?;\n        handle.spawn(async move {\n            if let Err(e) = connection.await {\n                tracing::error!(\"postgres connection error: {e}\");\n            }\n        });\n        let mut adapter = Self {\n            client,\n            handle,\n            _rt: rt,\n            in_tx: false,\n        };\n        adapter.init_schema()?;\n        Ok(adapter)\n    }\n\n    /// Block the current thread on a `tokio_postgres` future, converting any\n    /// error into `AxonError::Storage`.\n    ///\n    /// When called from within a Tokio async task, uses\n    /// [`tokio::task::block_in_place`] to temporarily un-enter the async\n    /// context before blocking on the adapter's dedicated runtime.  This\n    /// prevents the \"cannot call `block_on` from within an async context\"\n    /// panic that would otherwise occur when gateway route handlers drive\n    /// storage calls.\n    ///\n    /// Requires the multi-thread Tokio scheduler (used by the production\n    /// server).  Tests that use the default `current_thread` scheduler must\n    /// call adapter methods from a `spawn_blocking` closure.\n    fn block<T>(\n        &self,\n        fut: impl Future<Output = Result<T, tokio_postgres::Error>>,\n    ) -> Result<T, AxonError> {\n        match tokio::runtime::Handle::try_current() {\n            Ok(_) => tokio::task::block_in_place(|| self.handle.block_on(fut)),\n            Err(_) => self.handle.block_on(fut),\n        }\n        .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))\n    }\n\n    fn init_schema(&mut self) -> Result<(), AxonError> {\n        self.block(self.client.batch_execute(\n            \"CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS databases (\n                    name TEXT NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY\n                );\n                CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS namespaces (\n                    database_name TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES databases(name) ON DELETE CASCADE,\n                    name          TEXT NOT NULL,\n                    PRIMARY KEY (database_name, name)\n                );\n                CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS entities (\n                    collection    TEXT NOT NULL,\n                    database_name TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'default',\n                    schema_name   TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'default',\n                    id            TEXT NOT NULL,\n                    version       BIGINT NOT NULL,\n                    data          JSONB NOT NULL,\n                    PRIMARY KEY (database_name, schema_name, collection, id)\n                );\n                CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS schemas (\n                    collection    TEXT NOT NULL,\n                    database_name TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'default',\n                    schema_name   TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'default',\n                    version       INTEGER NOT NULL,\n                    schema_json   JSONB NOT NULL,\n                    created_at_ns BIGINT NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,\n                    PRIMARY KEY (database_name, schema_name, collection, version)\n                );\n                CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS collections (\n                    name TEXT NOT NULL,\n                    database_name TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'default',\n                    schema_name TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'default',\n                    PRIMARY KEY (database_name, schema_name, name)\n                );\n                CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS collection_views (\n                    collection    TEXT NOT NULL,\n                    database_name TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'default',\n                    schema_name   TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'default',\n                    version       INTEGER NOT NULL,\n                    view_json     JSONB NOT NULL,\n                    updated_at_ns BIGINT NOT NULL,\n                    updated_by    TEXT,\n                    PRIMARY KEY (database_name, schema_name, collection),\n                    FOREIGN KEY (database_name, schema_name, collection)\n                        REFERENCES collections(database_name, schema_name, name)\n                        ON DELETE CASCADE\n                );\n                CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS audit_log (\n                    id             BIGSERIAL PRIMARY KEY,\n                    timestamp_ns   BIGINT NOT NULL,\n                    collection     TEXT NOT NULL,\n                    entity_id      TEXT NOT NULL,\n                    version        BIGINT NOT NULL,\n                    mutation       TEXT NOT NULL,\n                    actor          TEXT NOT NULL,\n                    transaction_id TEXT,\n                    entry_json     JSONB NOT NULL\n                );\",\n        ))?;\n        self.ensure_namespace_catalog_tables()?;\n        self.ensure_default_namespace()\n    }\n\n    fn collection_exists_in_namespace(\n        &self,\n        collection: &CollectionId,\n        namespace: &Namespace,\n    ) -> Result<bool, AxonError> {\n        let row = self.block(self.client.query_one(\n            \"SELECT EXISTS(\n                    SELECT 1 FROM collections\n                    WHERE name = $1 AND database_name = $2 AND schema_name = $3\n                )\",\n            &[&collection.as_str(), &namespace.database, &namespace.schema],\n        ))?;\n        Ok(row.get(0))\n    }\n\n    fn database_exists(&self, database: &str) -> Result<bool, database_name = $2 AND schema_name = $3\n                 ORDER BY version ASC\",\n            &[\n                &key.collection.as_str(),\n                &key.namespace.database,\n                &key.namespace.schema,\n            ],\n        ))?;\n\n        Ok(rows\n            .into_iter()\n            .map(|row| {\n                (\n                    row.get::<_, i32>(\"version\") as u32,\n                    row.get::<_, i64>(\"created_at_ns\") as u64,\n                )\n            })\n            .collect())\n    }\n\n    fn delete_schema(&mut self, collection: &CollectionId) -> Result<(), AxonError> {\n        let key = self.resolve_catalog_key(collection)?;\n        self.block(self.client.execute(\n            \"DELETE FROM schemas\n                 WHERE collection = $1 AND database_name = $2 AND schema_name = $3\",\n            &[\n                &key.collection.as_str(),\n                &key.namespace.database,\n                &key.namespace.schema,\n            ],\n        ))?;\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    fn put_collection_view(&mut self, view: &CollectionView) -> Result<CollectionView, AxonError> {\n        let key = self.resolve_catalog_key(&view.collection)?;\n        if !self.collection_exists_in_namespace(&key.collection, &key.namespace)? {\n            return Err(AxonError::InvalidArgument(format!(\n                \"collection '{}' is not registered\",\n                view.collection.as_str()\n            )));\n        }\n\n        let current_version = self\n            .block(self.client.query_opt(\n                \"SELECT version FROM collection_views\n                 WHERE collection = $1 AND database_name = $2 AND schema_name = $3\",\n                &[\n                    &key.collection.as_str(),\n                    &key.namespace.database,\n                    &key.namespace.schema,\n                ],\n            ))?\n            .map_or(0, |row| row.get::<_, i32>(\"version\"));\n        let next_version = current_version + 1;\n\n        let updated_at_ns = SystemTime::now()\n            .duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH)\n            .unwrap_or_default()\n            .as_nanos() as i64;\n\n        let mut versioned = view.clone();\n        versioned.collection = key.collection.clone();\n        versioned.version = next_version as u32;\n        versioned.updated_at_ns = Some(updated_at_ns as u64);\n        let view_json = serde_json::to_value(&versioned)?;\n\n        self.block(self.client.execute(\n            \"INSERT INTO collection_views\n                    (collection, database_name, schema_name, version, view_json, updated_at_ns, updated_by)\n                 VALUES ($1, $2, $3, $4, $5, $6, $7)\n                 ON CONFLICT (database_name, schema_name, collection) DO UPDATE SET\n                     version = EXCLUDED.version,\n                     view_json = EXCLUDED.view_json,\n                     updated_at_ns = EXCLUDED.updated_at_ns,\n                     updated_by = EXCLUDED.updated_by\",\n            &[\n                &key.collection.as_str(),\n                &key.namespace.database,\n                &key.namespace.schema,\n                &next_version,\n                &view_json,\n                &updated_at_ns,\n                &versioned.updated_by,\n            ],\n        ))?;\n        Ok(versioned)\n    }\n\n    fn get_collection_view(\n        &self,\n        collection: &CollectionId,\n    ) -> Result<Option<CollectionView>, AxonError> {\n        let key = self.resolve_catalog_key(collection)?;\n        let rows = self.block(self.client.query(\n            \"SELECT view_json FROM collection_views\n                 WHERE collection = $1 AND database_name = $2 AND schema_name = $3\",\n            &[\n                &key.collection.as_str(),\n                &key.namespace.database,\n                &key.namespace.schema,\n            ],\n        ))?;\n\n        match rows.first() {\n            Some(row) => {\n                let view_json: serde_json::Value = row.get(\"view_json\");\n                let view: CollectionView = serde_json::from_value(view_json)?;\n                Ok(Some(view))\n            }\n            None => Ok(None),\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn delete_collection_view(&mut self, collection: &CollectionId) -> Result<(), AxonError> {\n        let key = self.resolve_catalog_key(collection)?;\n        self.block(self.client.execute(\n            \"DELETE FROM collection_views\n                 WHERE collection = $1 AND database_name = $2 AND schema_name = $3\",\n            &[\n                &key.collection.as_str(),\n                &key.namespace.database,\n                &key.namespace.schema,\n            ],\n        ))?;\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    fn register_collection_in_namespace(\n        &mut self,\n        collection: &CollectionId,\n        namespace: &Namespace,\n    ) -> Result<(), AxonError> {\n        if !self.namespace_exists(namespace)? {\n            return Err(AxonError::NotFound(format!(\"namespace '{namespace}'\")));\n        }\n\n        self.block(self.client.execute(\n            \"INSERT INTO collections (name, database_name, schema_name)\n                 VALUES ($1, $2, $3)\n                 ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING\",\n            &[&collection.as_str(), &namespace.database, &namespace.schema],\n        ))?;\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    fn unregister_collection(&mut self, collection: &CollectionId) -> Result<(), AxonError> {\n        let key = self.resolve_catalog_key(collection)?;\n        let raw_collection = collection.as_str();\n        let default_namespace = Namespace::default_ns();\n        // Upgraded databases may still have a pre-fix collection_views table\n        // without the collection -> collections foreign key, and may still\n        // carry metadata rows keyed by the original qualified identifier in\n        // either the resolved namespace or the old default/default namespace,\n        // so clean them up explicitly instead of relying solely on ON DELETE\n        // CASCADE.\n        self.block(self.client.execute(\n            \"DELETE FROM collection_views\n                 WHERE collection = $1 AND database_name = $2 AND schema_name = $3\",\n            &[\n                &key.collection.as_str(),\n                &key.namespace.database,\n                &key.namespace.schema,\n            ],\n        ))?;\n        if raw_collection != key.collection.as_str() {\n            self.block(self.client.execute(\n                \"DELETE FROM collection_views\n                     WHERE collection = $1\n                       AND ((database_name = $2 AND schema_name = $3)\n                            OR (database_name = $4 AND schema_name = $5))\",\n                &[\n                    &raw_collection,\n                    &key.namespace.database,\n                    &key.namespace.schema,\n                    &default_namespace.database,\n                    &default_namespace.schema,\n                ],\n            ))?;\n        }\n        self.block(self.client.execute(\n            \"DELETE FROM schemas\n                 WHERE collection = $1 AND database_name = $2 AND schema_name = $3\",\n            &[\n                &key.collection.as_str(),\n                &key.namespace.database,\n                &key.namespace.schema,\n            ],\n        ))?;\n        if raw_collection != key.collection.as_str() {\n            self.block(self.client.execute(\n                \"DELETE FROM schemas\n                     WHERE collection = $1\n                       AND ((database_name = $2 AND schema_name = $3)\n                            OR (database_name = $4 AND schema_name = $5))\",\n                &[\n                    &raw_collection,\n                    &key.namespace.database,\n                    &key.namespace.schema,\n                    &default_namespace.database,\n                    &default_namespace.schema,\n                ],\n            ))?;\n        }\n        self.block(self.client.execute(\n            \"DELETE FROM collections\n                 WHERE name = $1 AND database_name = $2 AND schema_name = $3\",\n            &[\n                &key.collection.as_str(),\n                &key.namespace.database,\n                &key.namespace.schema,\n            ],\n        ))?;\n        if raw_collection != key.collection.as_str() {\n            self.block(self.client.execute(\n                \"DELETE FROM collections\n                     WHERE name = $1\n                       AND ((database_name = $2 AND schema_name = $3)\n                            OR (database_name = $4 AND schema_name = $5))\",\n                &[\n                    &raw_collection,\n                    &key.namespace.database,\n                    &key.namespace.schema,\n                    &default_namespace.database,\n                    &default_namespace.schema,\n                ],\n            ))?;\n        }\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    fn list_collections(&self) -> Result<Vec<CollectionId>, AxonError> {\n        let rows = self.block(self.client.query(\n            \"SELECT name FROM collections\n                 ORDER BY database_name ASC, schema_name ASC, name ASC\",\n            &[],\n        ))?;\n\n        Ok(rows\n            .iter()\n            .map(|row| {\n                let name: String = row.get(\"name\");\n                CollectionId::new(name)\n            })\n            .collect())\n    }\n\n    fn collection_registered_in_namespace(\n        &self,\n        collection: &CollectionId,\n        namespace: &Namespace,\n    ) -> Result<bool, AxonError> {\n        self.collection_exists_in_namespace(collection, namespace)\n    }\n}\n\n// ── Per-tenant PostgreSQL provisioning ───────────────────────────────────────\n\n/// Validate a PostgreSQL database name to prevent SQL injection in DDL.\n///\n/// PostgreSQL identifiers may contain letters, digits, `_`, and `$`, and\n/// must start with a letter or `_`. We apply a conservative subset of that\n/// rule here to keep things simple and safe.\nfn validate_pg_db_name(name: &str) -> Result<(), AxonError> {\n    if name.is_empty() {\n        return Err(AxonError::InvalidArgument(\n            \"database name must not be empty\".to_owned(),\n        ));\n    }\n    let first = name.chars().next().expect(\"non-empty checked above\");\n    if !first.is_ascii_alphabetic() && first != '_' {\n        return Err(AxonError::InvalidArgument(format!(\n            \"database name '{name}' must start with a letter or underscore\"\n        )));\n    }\n    if !name.chars().all(|c| c.is_ascii_alphanumeric() || c == '_') {\n        return Err(AxonError::InvalidArgument(format!(\n            \"database name '{name}' contains invalid characters (only ASCII alphanumeric and _ allowed)\"\n        )));\n    }\n    Ok(())\n}\n\n/// Derive a per-tenant DSN from a superadmin DSN by replacing/adding the\n/// `dbname` key.\n///\n/// The superadmin DSN uses the `postgres` maintenance database.  We produce a\n/// new DSN that targets `axon_{name}`.\n///\n/// Both libpq keyword–value format (`host=... dbname=...`) and URL format\n/// (`postgres://...`) are supported.\npub fn tenant_dsn(superadmin_dsn: &str, tenant_db_name: &str) -> String {\n    let target = format!(\"axon_{tenant_db_name}\");\n    if superadmin_dsn.starts_with(\"postgres://\") || superadmin_dsn.starts_with(\"postgresql://\") {\n        // URL format: replace path component.\n        // e.g. postgres://user:pass@host/somedb?sslmode=disable\n        if let Some(pos) = superadmin_dsn.find(\"://\") {\n            let after_scheme = &superadmin_dsn[pos + 3..];\n            // Find the slash that separates authority from path.\n            if let Some(slash_pos) = after_scheme.find('/') {\n                let scheme_authority = &superadmin_dsn[..pos + 3 + slash_pos];\n                let rest = &after_scheme[slash_pos + 1..];\n                // Strip existing dbname (everything before '?').\n                let query = if let Some(q) = rest.find('?') {\n                    &rest[q..]\n                } else {\n                    \"\"\n                };\n                return format!(\"{scheme_authority}/{target}{query}\");\n            }\n            // No path: append the target database.\n            return format!(\"{superadmin_dsn}/{target}\");\n        }\n        format!(\"{superadmin_dsn}/{target}\")\n    } else {\n        // Keyword–value format.  Replace existing dbname= or append it.\n        let mut parts: Vec<String> = superadmin_dsn\n            .split_whitespace()\n            .filter(|part| !part.starts_with(\"dbname=\"))\n            .map(str::to_owned)\n            .collect();\n        parts.push(format!(\"dbname={target}\"));\n        parts.join(\" \")\n    }\n}\n\n/// Create a physical PostgreSQL database named `axon_{name}` using a\n/// superadmin connection.\n///\n/// The `superadmin_dsn` must connect to the `postgres` maintenance database\n/// (or any database the superuser can reach) with sufficient privileges to\n/// execute `CREATE DATABASE`.\n///\n/// The database name is validated to prevent SQL injection.\n///\n/// # Errors\n///\n/// Returns `AxonError::AlreadyExists` if the database already exists.\n/// Returns `AxonError::InvalidArgument` if `name` contains invalid characters.\n/// Returns `AxonError::Storage` for other PostgreSQL errors.\npub fn provision_postgres_database(superadmin_dsn: &str, name: &str) -> Result<(), AxonError> {\n    validate_pg_db_name(name)?;\n    let full_name = format!(\"axon_{name}\");\n    let rt = tokio::runtime::Builder::new_current_thread()\n        .enable_all()\n        .build()\n        .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\n    rt.block_on(async {\n        let (client, connection) = tokio_postgres::connect(superadmin_dsn, NoTls)\n            .await\n            .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\n        tokio::spawn(async move {\n            let _ = connection.await;\n        });\n        let row = client\n            .query_one(\n                \"SELECT EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM pg_database WHERE datname = $1)\",\n                &[&full_name.as_str()],\n            )\n            .await\n            .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\n        let exists: bool = row.get(0);\n        if exists {\n            return Err(AxonError::AlreadyExists(format!(\n                \"PostgreSQL database '{full_name}'\"\n            )));\n        }\n        // CREATE DATABASE cannot run inside a transaction; use simple_query to\n        // avoid the implicit transaction that execute() would open.\n        client\n            .simple_query(&format!(\"CREATE DATABASE \\\"{full_name}\\\"\"))\n            .await\n            .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\n        Ok(())\n    })\n}\n\n/// Drop the physical PostgreSQL database named `axon_{name}`.\n///\n/// The `superadmin_dsn` must have sufficient privileges to execute\n/// `DROP DATABASE`.\n///\n/// # Errors\n///\n/// Returns `AxonError::NotFound` if the database does not exist.\n/// Returns `AxonError::InvalidArgument` if `name` contains invalid characters.\n/// Returns `AxonError::Storage` for other PostgreSQL errors.\npub fn deprovision_postgres_database(superadmin_dsn: &str, name: &str) -> Result<(), AxonError> {\n    validate_pg_db_name(name)?;\n    let full_name = format!(\"axon_{name}\");\n    let rt = tokio::runtime::Builder::new_current_thread()\n        .enable_all()\n        .build()\n        .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\n    rt.block_on(async {\n        let (client, connection) = tokio_postgres::connect(superadmin_dsn, NoTls)\n            .await\n            .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\n        tokio::spawn(async move {\n            let _ = connection.await;\n        });\n        let row = client\n            .query_one(\n                \"SELECT EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM pg_database WHERE datname = $1)\",\n                &[&full_name.as_str()],\n            )\n            .await\n            .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\n        let exists: bool = row.get(0);\n        if !exists {\n            return Err(AxonError::NotFound(format!(\n                \"PostgreSQL database '{full_name}'\"\n            )));\n        }\n        client\n            .simple_query(&format!(\"DROP DATABASE \\\"{full_name}\\\"\"))\n            .await\n            .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\n        Ok(())\n    })\n}\n\n// The conformance test suite requires PostgreSQL.\n// Explicit verification path:\n// AXON_TEST_POSTGRES=\"host=localhost user=axon dbname=axon_test\" cargo test -p axon-storage postgres::tests:: -- --nocapture\n// When `AXON_TEST_POSTGRES` is unset, these tests attempt to provision PostgreSQL\n// via testcontainers and skip cleanly if the container runtime is unavailable.\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use std::{\n        ops::{Deref, DerefMut},\n        sync::{Mutex, MutexGuard, OnceLock},\n    };\n\n    use super::*;\n    use axon_core::types::Link;\n    use testcontainers_modules::postgres::Postgres as PgContainer;\n    use testcontainers_modules::testcontainers::{runners::SyncRunner, Container};\n\n    struct TestDatabase {\n        url: String,\n        _container: Option<Container<PgContainer>>,\n    }\n\n    enum TestSetupError {\n        Skip(String),\n        Fail(AxonError),\n    }\n\n    impl TestDatabase {\n        fn connect() -> Result<Self, TestSetupError> {\n            if let Ok(url) = std::env::var(\"AXON_TEST_POSTGRES\") {\n                return Ok(Self {\n                    url,\n                    _container: None,\n                });\n            }\n\n            let container = PgContainer::default()\n                .with_db_name(\"axon_test\")\n                .with_user(\"postgres\")\n                .with_password(\"postgres\")\n                .start()\n                .map_err(|error| {\n                    TestSetupError::Skip(format!(\n                        \"AXON_TEST_POSTGRES is unset and PostgreSQL test container startup failed: {error}\"\n                    ))\n                })?;\n            let host = container.get_host().map_err(|error| {\n                TestSetupError::Fail(AxonError::Storage(format!(\n                    \"failed to resolve PostgreSQL test container host: {error}\"\n                )))\n            })?;\n            let port = container.get_host_port_ipv4(5432).map_err(|error| {\n                TestSetupError::Fail(AxonError::Storage(format!(\n                    \"failed to resolve PostgreSQL test container port: {error}\"\n                )))\n            })?;\n\n            Ok(Self {\n                url: format!(\n                    \"host={host} port={port} user=postgres password=postgres dbname=axon_test\"\n                ),\n                _container: Some(container),\n            })\n        }\n\n        fn url(&self) -> &str {\n            &self.url\n        }\n    }\n\n    struct TestStore {\n        adapter: PostgresStorageAdapter,\n        _database: TestDatabase,\n    }\n\n    impl Deref for TestStore {\n        type Target = PostgresStorageAdapter;\n\n        fn deref(&self) -> &Self::Target {\n            &self.adapter\n        }\n    }\n\n    impl DerefMut for TestStore {\n        fn deref_mut(&mut self) -> &mut Self::Target {\n            &mut self.adapter\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn postgres_test_guard() -> MutexGuard<'static, ()> {\n        static GUARD: OnceLock<Mutex<()>> = OnceLock::new();\n        GUARD\n            .get_or_init(|| Mutex::new(()))\n            .lock()\n            .expect(\"PostgreSQL test guard lock should not be poisoned\")\n    }\n\n    fn skip_postgres_test(test_name: &str, reason: &str) {\n        tracing::warn!(test = test_name, reason, \"skipping PostgreSQL storage test\");\n    }\n\n    fn database_or_skip(test_name: &str) -> Option<TestDatabase> {\n        match TestDatabase::connect() {\n            Ok(database) => Some(database),\n            Err(TestSetupError::Skip(reason)) => {\n                skip_postgres_test(test_name, &reason);\n                None\n            }\n            Err(TestSetupError::Fail(error)) => {\n                panic!(\"PostgreSQL test setup should succeed: {error}\");\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn store() -> Result<TestStore, TestSetupError> {\n        let database = TestDatabase::connect()?;\n        let adapter =\n            PostgresStorageAdapter::connect(database.url()).map_err(TestSetupError::Fail)?;\n        // Clean tables for a fresh test.\n        adapter\n            .block(adapter.client.batch_execute(\n                \"TRUNCATE entities, schemas, collection_views, collections, namespaces, databases, audit_log RESTART IDENTITY CASCADE\",\n            ))\n            .map_err(TestSetupError::Fail)?;\n        adapter\n            .ensure_default_namespace()\n            .map_err(TestSetupError::Fail)?;\n        Ok(TestStore {\n            adapter,\n            _database: database,\n        })\n    }\n\n    fn store_or_skip(test_name: &str) -> Option<TestStore> {\n        match store() {\n            Ok(store) => Some(store),\n            Err(TestSetupError::Skip(reason)) => {\n                skip_postgres_test(test_name, &reason);\n                None\n            }\n            Err(TestSetupError::Fail(error)) => {\n                panic!(\"PostgreSQL test setup should succeed: {error}\");\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn register_unique_namespaced_collection(\n        store: &mut TestStore,\n        qualified: &CollectionId,\n    ) -> (Namespace, CollectionId) {\n        let (namespace, bare_collection) = Namespace::parse(qualified.as_str());\n        let bare_collection = CollectionId::new(bare_collection);\n\n        store\n            .create_database(namespace.database.as_str())\n            .expect(\"database create should succeed\");\n        store\n            .create_namespace(&namespace)\n            .expect(\"namespace create should succeed\");\n        store\n            .register_collection_in_namespace(&bare_collection, &namespace)\n            .expect(\"collection register should succeed\");\n\n tokio::runtime::Builder::new_current_thread()\n            .enable_all()\n            .build()\n            .expect(\"test runtime\");\n        let (client, connection) = rt\n            .block_on(tokio_postgres::connect(url, NoTls))\n            .expect(\"test connection\");\n        rt.spawn(async move {\n            let _ = connection.await;\n        });\n        (client, rt)\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn postgres_roundtrip_when_available() {\n        let _guard = postgres_test_guard();\n        let Some(mut s) = store_or_skip(\"postgres_roundtrip_when_available\") else {\n            return;\n        };\n\n        let col = CollectionId::new(\"tasks\");\n        let entity = Entity::new(\n            col.clone(),\n            EntityId::new(\"t-001\"),\n            serde_json::json!({\"title\": \"hello\"}),\n        );\n        s.put(entity).expect(\"test operation should succeed\");\n        let got = s\n            .get(&col, &EntityId::new(\"t-001\"))\n            .expect(\"test operation should succeed\")\n            .expect(\"test operation should succeed\");\n        assert_eq!(got.data[\"title\"], \"hello\");\n        assert_eq!(got.version, 1);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn unregister_collection_cleans_up_legacy_collection_views_when_available() {\n        let _guard = postgres_test_guard();\n        let Some(database) = database_or_skip(\n            \"unregister_collection_cleans_up_legacy_collection_views_when_available\",\n        ) else {\n            return;\n        };\n\n        let (legacy_client, legacy_rt) = connect_raw_for_test(database.url());\n        legacy_rt\n            .block_on(async {\n                legacy_client\n                    .batch_execute(\n                        \"DROP TABLE IF EXISTS collection_views;\n                         DROP TABLE IF EXISTS collections;\n                         DROP TABLE IF EXISTS namespaces;\n                         DROP TABLE IF EXISTS databases;\n                         DROP TABLE IF EXISTS entities;\n                         DROP TABLE IF EXISTS schemas;\n                         DROP TABLE IF EXISTS audit_log;\",\n                    )\n                    .await?;\n                legacy_client\n                    .batch_execute(\n                        \"CREATE TABLE collections (\n                            name TEXT NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY\n                        );\n                        CREATE TABLE collection_views (\n                            collection    TEXT NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY,\n                            version       INTEGER NOT NULL,\n                            view_json     JSONB NOT NULL,\n                            updated_at_ns BIGINT NOT NULL,\n                            updated_by    TEXT\n                        );\",\n                    )\n                    .await?;\n                Ok::<_, tokio_postgres::Error>(())\n            })\n            .expect(\"legacy schema should be created\");\n\n        let collection = CollectionId::new(\"ephemeral\");\n        let legacy_view = CollectionView {\n            collection: collection.clone(),\n            description: Some(\"legacy view\".into()),\n            markdown_template: \"# {{title}}\".into(),\n            version: 1,\n            updated_at_ns: Some(42),\n            updated_by: Some(\"legacy\".into()),\n        };\n        let legacy_view_json =\n            serde_json::to_value(&legacy_view).expect(\"legacy collection view should serialize\");\n\n        legacy_rt\n            .block_on(async {\n                legacy_client\n                    .execute(\n                        \"INSERT INTO collections (name) VALUES ($1)\",\n                        &[&collection.as_str()],\n                    )\n                    .await?;\n                legacy_client\n                    .execute(\n                        \"INSERT INTO collection_views (collection, version, view_json, updated_at_ns, updated_by)\n                         VALUES ($1, $2, $3, $4, $5)\",\n                        &[\n                            &collection.as_str(),\n                            &1_i32,\n                            &legacy_view_json,\n                            &42_i64,\n                            &Some(\"legacy\"),\n                        ],\n                    )\n                    .await?;\n                Ok::<_, tokio_postgres::Error>(())\n            })\n            .expect(\"legacy data should insert\");\n        drop(legacy_client);\n        drop(legacy_rt);\n\n        let mut adapter = PostgresStorageAdapter::connect(database.url())\n            .expect(\"adapter should connect after upgrade\");\n        assert!(\n            adapter\n                .get_collection_view(&collection)\n                .expect(\"legacy view should be readable after upgrade\")\n                .is_some(),\n            \"upgraded adapter should observe the pre-fix collection view\"\n        );\n\n        adapter\n            .unregister_collection(&collection)\n            .expect(\"unregister_collection should succeed on upgraded database\");\n\n        assert!(\n            adapter\n                .get_collection_view(&collection)\n                .expect(\"collection view lookup should succeed after unregister\")\n                .is_none(),\n            \"legacy collection view should be removed during unregister\"\n        );\n\n        let remaining_views: i64 = adapter\n            .block(adapter.client.query_one(\n                \"SELECT COUNT(*) FROM collection_views WHERE collection = $1\",\n                &[&collection.as_str()],\n            ))\n            .expect(\"count query should succeed\")\n            .get(0);\n        assert_eq!(\n            remaining_views, 0,\n            \"stale collection view rows must be deleted\"\n        );\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn namespace_catalogs_allow_same_name_without_cross_drop() {\n        let _guard = postgres_test_guard();\n        let Some(mut s) = JWT-based auth pipeline: sign, verify, and enforce credentials (ADR-018 §4).\n//!\n//! # Verification order\n//!\n//! 1. Extract `Authorization: Bearer <token>` → `Unauthenticated` if missing.\n//! 2. Parse + verify HS256 signature → `CredentialInvalid` / `CredentialMalformed`.\n//! 3. Check `iss` against issuer ID → `CredentialForeignIssuer`.\n//! 4. Check `exp`/`nbf` with ±30 s skew → `CredentialExpired` / `CredentialNotYetValid`.\n//! 5. Check `jti` revocation (cache → storage) → `CredentialRevoked`.\n//! 6. Compare `aud` to URL tenant → `CredentialWrongTenant`.\n//! 7. Look up `sub` in users table, check suspension → `UserSuspended`.\n//! 8. Check tenant membership → `NotATenantMember`.\n//! 9. Walk grants for URL database + HTTP-method-derived op → `DatabaseNotGranted` / `OpNotGranted`.\n//!\n//! On success, installs `ResolvedIdentity` into the request extension.\n//! On failure, returns a JSON error response built from `AuthError::status_code` and\n//! `AuthError::error_code`.\n\nuse std::collections::HashSet;\nuse std::sync::{Arc, Mutex, RwLock};\nuse Axon server — HTTP gateway, gRPC service, MCP stdio, and authentication.\n//!\n//! # Authentication\n//!\n//! Identity is resolved once per request by [`auth::AuthContext`] and\n//! injected as a typed extension.  Three modes are supported:\n//!\n//! | Mode | Flag | Actor | Role |\n//! |------|------|-------|------|\n//! | `NoAuth` | `--no-auth` | `\"anonymous\"` | Admin |\n//! | `Tailscale` | *(default)* | node name | from ACL tags |\n//! | `Guest` | `--guest-role <role>` | `\"guest\"` | configured role |\n//!\n//! In `Tailscale` mode the server contacts the local Tailscale daemon over its\n//! Unix socket (`/run/tailscale/tailscaled.sock` by default) and calls\n//! `/localapi/v0/whois?addr=<peer>` to resolve the connecting node's identity.\n//! Resolved identities are cached by peer IP for the duration of\n//! `--auth-cache-ttl-secs` (default 60 s).\n//!\n//! ACL tag → role mapping:\n//! - `tag:axon-admin` → [`Role::Admin`]\n//! - `tag:axon-write` / `tag:axon-agent` → [`Role::Write`]\n//! - `tag:axon-read` → [`Role::Read`]\n//! - no matching tag → `--tailscale-default-role` (default `read`)\n//!\n//! Connections that are not on the tailnet are rejected with HTTP 401 / gRPC\n//! `UNAUTHENTICATED`.  If the Tailscale daemon is unreachable the server\n//! returns HTTP 503 / gRPC `UNAVAILABLE`.\n//!\n//! # Request flow\n//!\n//! ```text\n//! TCP accept\n//!   └─ authenticate_http_request  (gateway.rs middleware)\n//!        ├─ AuthContext::resolve_peer  →  Identity\n//!        └─ insert Identity into request extensions\n//!             └─ route handler\n//!                  ├─ extract Extension<Identity>\n//!                  ├─ identity.require_read() / require_write() / require_admin()\n//!                  └─ handler logic\n//! ```\n//!\n//! gRPC follows the same pattern via [`service::AxonServiceImpl::authorize`].\npub mod actor_scope;\npub mod auth;\nmod collection_listing;\npub(crate) mod embedded_ui;\npub mod control_plane;\npub mod control_plane_routes;\npub mod cors_config;\npub mod user_roles;\npub mod gateway;\npub mod idempotency;\npub(crate) mod mcp_http;\npub mod mcp_stdio;\npub mod rate_limit;\npub mod schema_registry;\npub mod serve;\npub mod service;\npub mod tenant_router;\n\npub use auth::{AuthContext, AuthMode, Identity, Role};\npub use mcp_stdio::run_mcp_stdio;\npub use service::AxonServiceImpl;\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":43,"oldLines":6,"newStart":43,"newLines":7,"lines":[" //! gRPC follows the same pattern via [`service::AxonServiceImpl::authorize`]."," pub mod actor_scope;"," pub mod auth;","+pub mod auth_pipeline;"," mod collection_listing;"," pub(crate) mod embedded_ui;"," pub mod control_plane;"]},{"oldStart":60,"oldLines":5,"newStart":61,"newLines":6,"lines":[" pub mod tenant_router;"," "," pub use auth::{AuthContext, AuthMode, Identity, Role};","+pub use auth_pipeline::{InMemoryRevocationCache, JwtIssuer, jwt_verify_layer};"," pub use mcp_stdio::run_mcp_stdio;"," pub use service::AxonServiceImpl;"]}],"userModified":false,"replaceAll":false}}
